Title: Dutchman's Pipe
Description: An ultra-private 220-acre club in the heart of West Palm Beach built around a 7,287-yard Jack Nicklaus Signature Course with no tee times, a tour-level practice range, a racquet club, a spa, multiple dining venues, and 22 private villas.
DescriptionLong: An ultra-private members club set on 220 acres east of I-95 in the heart of West Palm Beach, built around a 7,287-yard Jack Nicklaus Signature Course played with no tee times — long, generously spaced fairways framed by native landscaping, including the flowering Dutchman's Pipe vine that gives the club its name. The practice experience runs to tour-stop standards: a driving range exceeding 300 yards stocked with every ball on the market, two acres devoted to the short game, Top 100 instruction, and an on-site custom club-building operation that can fit and assemble a club to a player's specs within minutes. Beyond golf, a cutting-edge racquet club brings together tennis, padel, and pickleball, alongside a fitness center, a day spa, personal training, and event spaces. Three dining venues — including a members-only grill, poolside al fresco dining, and a dining room with a balcony overlooking the range and the first hole — round out the experience, and a private collection of 22 villas sits steps from the club, connected to an adjacent Marriott Autograph Collection resort and spa on the same property.
Developer: Witkoff Group, Access Industries, PPG Development

Title: Eden Residences
Description: A boutique 10-story condominium in Ojus with just 32 residences, a rooftop sky deck with a resort pool and spa, and wellness and fitness amenities scaled to a small resident community — minutes from Aventura Mall, Brightline, and the coast.
DescriptionLong: Eden Residences is a boutique 10-story luxury condominium in Miami's Ojus neighborhood, moments from Aventura Mall, the Brightline station, and the Atlantic coast. Developed by Growin Group with Property Pro Partners and designed by Gaviria Architects, the intimate building holds just 32 turnkey residences — one- to three-bedroom homes plus two duplex penthouses — many configured for flexible monthly rentals. A rooftop sky deck crowns the tower with a resort-style pool, spa, and sweeping South Florida views, complemented by a wellness and fitness program scaled to a small resident community. Thoughtful touches including a Shabbat elevator and curated designer finishes round out one of Ojus's most design-forward new condominium addresses.

Title: Siari, a Ritz-Carlton Reserve
Description: A 91-key Ritz-Carlton Reserve anchoring the private Nauka community on Mexico's Riviera Nayarit — the brand's ninth Reserve worldwide and second in Mexico, set between Pacific coastline and old-growth jungle.
DescriptionLong: Siari, a Ritz-Carlton Reserve, is the resort heart of the private 920-acre Nauka community on the Riviera Nayarit, roughly 45 minutes north of Puerto Vallarta. As the ninth Ritz-Carlton Reserve in the world and the second in Mexico, the 91-key retreat sits among the same dramatic landscapes that define Nauka — Pacific coastline, volcanic cliffs, mangrove estuary, and old-growth jungle — with architecture and interiors by Bernardi + Peschard. Guests draw on the wider community's experiences, from the Tom Fazio championship golf course and beach club to the marina, wellness sanctuary, and adventure program, and beginning in 2026 a limited number of guests receive curated access to tee times at Nauka Golf Club — for now, one of the only doors into the community.

Title: Four Seasons Private Residences Coconut Grove
Description: Florida's first standalone Four Seasons–branded residence — a 20-story waterfront tower on Biscayne Bay with just 70 homes, curved walls of glass, a Roman-inspired spa, and full Four Seasons service, with no hotel attached.
DescriptionLong: Standing in the front row on Biscayne Bay in Coconut Grove, Four Seasons Private Residences Coconut Grove is the first standalone Four Seasons–branded residence in Florida — a purely residential tower with no hotel component, a rarity in the global Four Seasons portfolio. The 20-story building holds only 70 waterfront homes of generous proportions, with two- to four-bedroom layouts reaching nearly 4,000 square feet, 11-foot ceilings, expansive curved balconies, private secured elevators and foyers, and walls of floor-to-ceiling glass framing the bay. Interiors by Florentine designer Michele Bönan pair tropical nonchalance with European craftsmanship — bespoke Molteni&C kitchens, Calacatta and Carrara marble baths over Italian travertine — while the architecture's soft curves follow the coastal silhouette. Residents draw on full Four Seasons service and a deep amenity package: a serviced pool deck and bar lounge, an intimate library, a spa with a Roman-thermae-inspired Caesar Experience moving through five contrast-therapy rooms, an indoor pool, Finnish saunas, and a fitness center and yoga studio, with a signature restaurant offering in-residence dining and a private landscaped setting by Raymond Jungles among the neighborhood's banyan canopy.
Architect: Revuelta Architecture International, Michele Bönan, Raymond Jungles

Title: The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Palm Beach Gardens
Description: A 106-residence Ritz-Carlton–branded waterfront community on the last 14 contiguous acres of Intracoastal frontage in the Palm Beach Gardens Marina District, with a 95-foot infinity pool, a 29-slip private marina, and 20,000+ square feet of resort amenities.
DescriptionLong: Set on the last 14 contiguous acres of Intracoastal waterfront in the Palm Beach Gardens Marina District, The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Palm Beach Gardens brings branded, service-driven living to one of South Florida's fastest-rising waterfront markets. Three buildings hold 106 residences wrapped by more than 20,000 square feet of resort amenities — a 95-foot infinity pool over the Intracoastal with twelve private cabanas and food, beverage, and towel service, a waterfront pavilion with outdoor grilling, pickleball courts, a dog park, and waterfront walking paths. Above the marina, an upper amenity terrace adds green space nearly the size of two football fields, a putting green, and sweeping water views, with select residences offering direct access and private terraces topping 1,800 square feet. Indoors, residents draw on the Lighthouse Lounge, a grand lobby with 24-hour front desk, a waterfront fitness center, and a private clubhouse with bar and dining, alongside 24/7 concierge and Ritz-Carlton anticipatory service and boating from a private 29-slip marina. The large-format Estate Collection residences span more than 3,800 square feet in three- to five-bedroom flow-through layouts with dens, oversized chef's kitchens with Wolf and Sub-Zero appliances, spa-style primary suites, walk-in pantries, and generous terraces for indoor-outdoor living along the water.
Architect: Spina O'Rourke + Partners, Susurrus, Decorators Unlimited

Title: 346 Madison Avenue
Description: A 46-story, all-electric office tower rising a block from Grand Central and across from One Vanderbilt, with column-free floors, terrace and loggia levels, and a two-floor amenity collection including a 215-seat auditorium, a Daniel Boulud épicerie, and an indoor padel court.
DescriptionLong: A new 46-story, all-electric office tower planned for one of the best development sites in midtown Manhattan — a block from Grand Central Terminal and directly across from One Vanderbilt, on the site of the former Brooks Brothers flagship. The roughly 850,000-square-foot tower trades a center core for a side core, opening up column-free floorplates with ceilings from 15 to 22 feet, nine floors with private terraces, and nine more wrapped in loggias and oversized glass, targeting the highest tiers of sustainability and WELL certification. A two-floor amenity collection includes a 215-seat auditorium, a tenant lounge anchored by an épicerie from Michelin-starred chef Daniel Boulud, and a landscaped terrace over Madison Avenue, alongside a luxury wellness center with a tenant-only fitness club, spa-quality locker rooms, a regulation-size indoor padel court billed as the only one inside an office building, and a world-class restaurant planned at the ground floor.

Title: Nauka
Description: A private 920-acre community on Mexico's Pacific coast built around a Tom Fazio championship golf course with three oceanfront holes you can swim mid-round, anchored by a Ritz-Carlton Reserve and limited to just 395 residences.
DescriptionLong: A private, low-density community spanning 920 acres along the Riviera Nayarit coast, 45 minutes north of Puerto Vallarta, built around one of the most ambitious golf experiences in the Americas. At its heart is Nauka Golf Club, a Tom Fazio par-72, 7,600-yard championship course that threads through four distinct worlds — old-growth jungle, volcanic cliffs, a mangrove estuary, and more than four miles of Pacific coastline — with five sets of tees and a signature run of three consecutive oceanfront holes where players are invited to go barefoot and wade into the surf mid-round. On-course dining anchors the round at The Nest, a cliffside comfort station high above the Pacific, while a lighted nine-hole par-3 short course, The Booby Trap, is built for night play and social rounds. The wider community reaches far past golf: a beach club with a 197-foot infinity pool, a marina village and yacht club, a 15-acre jungle wellness sanctuary, ocean and racquet clubs, an organic farm driving the culinary program, and an adventure roster from surfing and spearfishing to zip lines and climbing. Just 395 residences — Beach Estates with direct Pacific access, elevated Cliff Estates, and four-bedroom Golf Villas along the 18th fairway — keep it intensely private, with a Ritz-Carlton Reserve anchoring the resort core.

Title: MIRAI Design District
Description: MIRAI Design District is a boutique mixed-use development in Miami's Design District and the first U.S. mixed-use project by Pritzker laureate Japanese architect Kengo Kuma. Conceived as a 'floating lantern' with a transparent ground floor, a layered façade-screening system and a lush central garden, the low-rise building pairs roughly 41,000 square feet of Class A boutique office space with about 16,000 square feet of luxury high-street retail and hospitality along NE 2nd Avenue. Developed by Leviathan Development, Lionheart Capital, WellDuo and The Lane Organization, MIRAI blends architecture, nature and craft into an intimate, experiential extension of the neighborhood.
DescriptionLong: MIRAI Design District is the first mixed-use development in the United States from internationally acclaimed Japanese architect Kengo Kuma and Associates, rising at 4218 NE 2nd Avenue in Miami's Design District. The project introduces approximately 41,000 square feet of Class A boutique office space — positioned for family offices, small and mid-sized funds, and design-led businesses — alongside 16,000 square feet of luxury high-street retail and hospitality space, all conceived as a "floating lantern" with a transparent ground floor, layered façade screening system, lush central garden, and hospitality-driven public spaces. Landscape architecture is led by Island Planning Corporation under Nathan Browning, with celebrated Italian lighting firm Viabizzuno designing a dynamic façade lighting program. Developed by a consortium of Leviathan Development (led by founder Allison Greenfield), Lionheart Capital (led by founder and CEO Ophir Sternberg), Well Duo, and The Lane Organization, with WD Capital Group advising on the capitalization. Ground broke in June 2026 following the closing of an $85 million construction loan from Monroe Capital LLC; completion is anticipated in 2028.
Developer: Leviathan Development, Lionheart Capital, WellDuo, The Lane Organization

Title: Flow at Aventura Corporate Center
Description: Flow at Aventura Corporate Center is a two-tower residential development under the Flow lifestyle brand, rising on a reimagined Biscayne Boulevard office campus in Aventura. The project pairs two 32-story rental towers with ground-floor retail, shared lifestyle amenities and a community fleet of cars and bikes, bringing Flow's branded, amenity-rich rental living to one of South Florida's most established suburban submarkets.
DescriptionLong: A pair of 32-story towers, rising to 367 feet, planned on the 8.6-acre Aventura Corporate Center along Biscayne Boulevard, adding 720 residences to a site that keeps its three existing office buildings in place. One tower would hold condominiums and the other rentals, both carrying the Flow residential brand, connected by a five-story podium with four levels of parking and ground-floor retail and amenities. Homes would range from roughly 550 to 1,494 square feet across one- to four-bedroom layouts, with a mix of 298 one-bedrooms, 271 two-bedrooms, 105 three-bedrooms, and 46 four-bedrooms. The plan folds in 20 units of workforce "hero housing" for city employees, first responders, and teachers, a shared fleet of 25 community cars and 50 bikes for residents, and a commitment to bar short-term rentals — an office-park-to-residential conversion of the kind reshaping Miami-Dade.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Aventura (per project name)

Title: Pine Crest School West Palm Beach
Description: Pine Crest School West Palm Beach is the planned third South Florida campus of Pine Crest School, the prestigious independent college-preparatory institution founded in Fort Lauderdale with an additional campus in Boca Raton. Established through a landmark philanthropic commitment from the Stephen M. Ross Foundation, the West Palm Beach expansion extends Pine Crest's nationally recognized academics, arts, and athletics programs into Palm Beach County, one of the fastest-growing centers of wealth and enterprise in the country. The new campus is positioned to serve the region's expanding population of families drawn to West Palm Beach's emergence as a financial and entrepreneurial hub.
DescriptionLong: Pine Crest School West Palm Beach is the planned third South Florida campus of Pine Crest School, the prestigious independent college-preparatory institution founded in Fort Lauderdale with an additional campus in Boca Raton. Title: The Brook Tennis and Pickleball
Description: The Brook Tennis and Pickleball is an approximately 79,000-square-foot indoor and outdoor racquet sports club in suburban Boynton Beach, Florida, developed and operated by Glenbrook Racquet Sports. The members-focused complex features five indoor and two outdoor tennis courts alongside fourteen indoor and six outdoor pickleball courts, all climate-controlled with professional lighting. Amenities include a pro shop, locker rooms, spectator viewing areas, and an on-site cafe and sports bar, supporting year-round leagues, clinics, youth development programs, tournaments, and social events for its founding membership community.

Title: Atelier Residences Miami
Description: Atelier Residences Miami is a boutique 10-story, 148-unit mixed-use condominium in The Roads, a historic tree-lined neighborhood just south of Brickell and Coral Way. Developed by BLDG Ventures and designed in collaboration with Gensler, the building is purpose-built for flexible short-term-rental ownership, offering fully finished, turnkey studio-to-three-bedroom residences with smart-home technology and curated interiors. Residents enjoy a rooftop pool deck, a spa and fitness center, a lobby restaurant and lounge, and the CAYA wellness members' club, anchoring a design-forward hospitality experience in one of Miami's most centrally located neighborhoods.

Title: Aston Martin Residences Daytona Beach Shores
Description: An oceanfront Aston Martin–branded residential tower on the Atlantic coast, with 86 residences crowned by double-height penthouses, fine dining, an artisan bakery, and direct access to one of the world's most famous beaches.
DescriptionLong: An ultra-luxury oceanfront tower carrying the Aston Martin name, rising on the Atlantic coast just minutes from the Daytona International Speedway and overlooking the sweep of Daytona Beach Shores. The 18-floor, roughly 320,000-square-foot building holds 86 residences shaped by the British marque's design language — the highly crafted, beautifully proportioned forms and cutting-edge detailing drawn from its automotive models — and is topped by two penthouse floors with eight double-height residences. Every home draws on a world-class amenity package, while the mixed-use base brings fine dining and an artisan bakery, and the beachfront setting offers residents direct access to the sand. Two parking floors anchor the lower levels of the tower.

Title: 1 Iris Lane, The Residences at Belle Meade Village
Description: A pair of boutique residential buildings at the gateway to Belle Meade — 92 one- to four-bedroom condominiums averaging about 3,200 square feet, wrapped by a five-acre private park and 16,000 square feet of amenities.
DescriptionLong: The only for-sale residential offering within the 15.5-acre Belle Meade Village development, 1 Iris Lane comprises two boutique buildings holding 92 condominiums in one- to four-bedroom layouts averaging roughly 3,200 square feet, with marble finishes, terraces framing views from the retail village to Richland Creek, 24-hour concierge, and full lifestyle staff. Residents have access to 16,000 square feet of amenity space — a library, piano room, screening and listening rooms, a golf simulator, a Pilates studio, a private pool and spa, an on-site salon, wine, whiskey, and cigar rooms, and entertainment spaces from intimate lounges to private mahjong and poker rooms. The buildings are surrounded by the five-acre Treetops Park & Gardens, with landscaped paths connecting to the Town Square, the cosmopolitan retail village, and the restored Richland Creek greenway.
Architect: Hartshorne Plunkard Architecture, Hastings Architecture, Meyer Davis

Title: Dolly Parton's SongTeller Hotel
Description: Dolly Parton's 12-story downtown Nashville hotel, with 245 music-themed rooms and suites, two live-music venues, a Listening Lobby, and a 20,000-square-foot museum of her life and career.
DescriptionLong: A 12-story downtown Nashville hotel just off Lower Broadway, conceived as Dolly Parton's love letter to Music City and marked by a guitar-shaped entryway. The 245 music-themed rooms and suites range from Signature Rooms to Acoustic Suites, the six-guest Six Sisters Suites honoring Dolly's sisters, and a two-story SongTeller Suite, with signature touches like in-room guitars and record players. A rainbow-hued Listening Lobby with gilded ceilings plays Dolly's songs every hour and hosts songwriting sessions, while two venues — Parton's Live, a performance space for songwriter sessions and rising-star showcases, and Jolene's, a piano lounge — bring live music under the same roof alongside craft cocktails and elevated fare. The hotel's third floor houses Dolly's Life of Many Colors Museum, a 20,000-plus-square-foot exhibit billed as the largest celebration of her life and career in the world, steps from the Ryman Auditorium, Bridgestone Arena, and the heart of Lower Broadway.

Title: Belle Meade Village
Description: A cosmopolitan shopping and residential village at the gateway to Belle Meade, with 40-plus storefronts, a members' club, a boutique hotel, dining, and wellness arranged around a courtyard anchored by an antique carousel.
DescriptionLong: A mixed-use shopping and residential village transforming a 15.5-acre site at the gateway to Belle Meade, at the entrance to one of Nashville's most established residential corridors. Comprised of seven two- and three-story buildings arranged around a central courtyard anchored by an antique carousel, the village brings together more than 40 storefronts, a members' club, a boutique hotel, health and wellness, cafés, and world-class restaurants. Green space, public walkways, and parks make up roughly 60% of the site, including the activation of Richland Creek and a connection to its namesake greenway that links to the pedestrian-friendly Sylvan Park neighborhood and McCabe Park.

Title: NoBe PARC Miami Beach
Description: A 15-story North Beach tower of 232 fully furnished, short-term-rental-friendly residences a short walk from the sand, with resort-style amenities and turnkey homes ready to live in or lease from day one.
DescriptionLong: NoBe PARC is a 15-story, 232-residence condominium in Miami Beach's fast-rising North Beach (NoBe) neighborhood, a short walk from the sand at 7140 Abbott Avenue. Developed by Robert Finvarb Companies with design architecture by Arquitectonica and architecture of record by The Architects Group, the tower is purpose-built for flexible ownership: studio to three-bedroom homes delivered fully furnished by Crate & Barrel with interiors by V Starr, ready to live in or lease short-term from day one. Amenities span a fifth-floor pool deck with sauna, steam room, and cold plunge, a rooftop yoga and cabana lounge, co-working lounges, a fitness center, and an on-site beach club — a turnkey, income-ready residence in one of Miami Beach's most dynamic submarkets.

Title: Coleman Park
Description: A mixed-income community hub rising along the Tamarind corridor with townhomes, rental apartments, commercial space, a fresh-market grocery, and a cultural gathering yard anchored by a library.
DescriptionLong: Coleman Park is a community-driven revitalization of a historic Black neighborhood along the Tamarind Avenue corridor in West Palm Beach, Florida. Led by Quantum Foundation's Palm Beach Venture Philanthropy with Neighborhood Renaissance, RISE Coleman Park and Purpose Built Communities, the mixed-income initiative rebuilds a cluster of Tamarind Avenue properties into for-sale and rental homes alongside a fresh-market grocery, shared and nonprofit office space, a community library, and a 'Culture Yard' gathering space. Designed by Spina O'Rourke + Partners, the plan weaves affordable, workforce and market-rate housing together with neighborhood services and cultural amenities to reshape the corridor into a walkable, equitable hub for longtime residents and newcomers alike.
Developer: Palm Beach Venture Philanthropy, Quantum Foundation, Neighborhood Renaissance

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Coleman Park (per project name + our WPB coverage)

Title: The Breakers Family Entertainment Center
Description: A 46,800-square-foot family entertainment center rising at The Breakers Palm Beach, nearly quadrupling the resort's current facility with indoor and outdoor attractions, casual dining, and immersive programming for guests and club members.
DescriptionLong: The Breakers Family Entertainment Center is a major new family-recreation venue rising on the oceanfront campus of The Breakers Palm Beach, the legendary Flagler-era resort on Florida's Palm Beach island. Designed by Hart Howerton and self-developed by the resort, the multi-level center nearly quadruples The Breakers' existing family facility, pairing indoor and outdoor attractions with casual dining and immersive, all-ages programming for resort guests and club members. The expansion deepens The Breakers' standing as one of America's premier luxury family destinations along the Atlantic coast.

StatusHistory.note: The Breakers began demolition to replace its 12,100 sf family facility with a 46,800 sf entertainment center; opening targeted spring 2028

Title: Meliá Residences Miami
Description: Meliá Residences Miami is Meliá Hotels & Resorts' first hotel-branded residential concept in North America, an eight-story, fully furnished turnkey condominium on the western edge of Brickell. Developed by Urban Network Capital Group with Meliá Hotels & Resorts and Concepto Capital Group, the project pairs a condo-hotel tower with a private-residence tower, with interiors and amenity spaces by Adriana Hoyos Design Studio and architecture by DTI Architects. Residents receive Meliá's signature European hospitality service alongside resort-style amenities in one of Miami's most connected luxury corridors.
DescriptionLong: Meliá Residences Miami marks Meliá Hotels & Resorts' return to Miami and its first hotel-branded residential concept in North America, rising at 1120 SW 3rd Avenue on the western edge of Brickell. Each residence is conceived as a turnkey home — delivered fully furnished and move-in ready — with rooms designed by Concepto Taller de Arquitectura and residential interiors and amenity spaces by Adriana Hoyos Design Studio, working in a Mediterranean-inspired palette of natural materials and organic textures that nods to the brand's heritage. The amenity program leans into Meliá's global lifestyle platform: wellness and relaxation spaces, Mediterranean-inspired fitness facilities, pools and outdoor terraces, social lounges, coworking areas, and hospitality-driven resident and guest services. Developed by Urban Network Capital Group (UNCG) in partnership with Meliá Hotels & Resorts and Concepto Capital Group, with a $30 million construction loan arranged by Concord Summit Capital.
Architect: Adriana Hoyos Design Studio, Concepto Taller De Arquitectura, DTI Architects
Developer: Urban Network Capital Group, Melia Hotels Resorts, Concepto Capital Group

Title: 120 S. Dixie Highway
Description: 120 S. Dixie Highway is a 25-story mixed-use mass-timber residential tower planned for downtown West Palm Beach, Florida, developed by billionaire Jeff Greene. Designed by Carlo Ratti Associati with Nexus Systems, the prefabricated timber high-rise is positioned to become Florida's tallest mass-timber building. Pursued under Florida's Live Local Act, the tower pairs market-rate apartments with a substantial share of workforce housing reserved for households at or below 120% of area median income, spanning studio to three-bedroom layouts above ground-floor retail. The design folds a turn-of-the-century firehouse on the site into the building's frontage, preserving the historic masonry landmark within a contemporary structure engineered to cut construction time and embodied carbon.
DescriptionLong: 120 S. Dixie Highway is a 25-story mixed-use mass-timber residential tower planned for downtown West Palm Beach, Florida, developed by billionaire Jeff Greene. Designed by Carlo Ratti Associati with Nexus Systems, the prefabricated timber high-rise is positioned to become Florida's tallest mass-timber building. Pursued under Florida's Live Local Act, the tower pairs market-rate apartments with a substantial share of workforce housing reserved for households at or below 120% of area median income, spanning studio to three-bedroom layouts above ground-floor retail. The design folds a turn-of-the-century firehouse on the site into the building's frontage, preserving the historic masonry landmark within a contemporary structure engineered to cut construction time and embodied carbon.

Title: 400 Lake Shore Drive
Description: 400 Lake Shore Drive is a two-tower luxury residential development by Related Midwest rising on the former Chicago Spire site, where the Chicago River meets Lake Michigan at the edge of downtown Chicago. Anchored by an 858-foot, 72-story north tower designed by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill with interiors by MAWD, the development pairs for-sale and rental residences with a curated amenity collection and a reserved share of affordable homes, alongside a new public lakefront DuSable Park. A planned south tower would complete one of the most prominent waterfront parcels on Chicago's skyline.
DescriptionLong: 400 Lake Shore Drive is a two-tower luxury residential development by Related Midwest rising on the former Chicago Spire site, where the Chicago River meets Lake Michigan at the edge of downtown Chicago. Anchored by an 858-foot, 72-story north tower designed by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill with interiors by MAWD, the development pairs for-sale and rental residences with a curated amenity collection and a reserved share of affordable homes, alongside a new public lakefront DuSable Park. A planned south tower would complete one of the most prominent waterfront parcels on Chicago's skyline.

Title: Nude Miami
DescriptionLong: Miami's first boutique organic grocer and café, open at 1100 Brickell Bay Drive on the ground floor of Panorama Tower in Brickell. Founded by three Miami locals — Charles Amine, Harry Miller, and Sebastian Lezcano — the 4,720-square-foot space is built around a strict ingredient-first standard: no GMOs, no seed oils, and no artificial colors, flavors, or preservatives. The flagship features Miami's first organic, seed-oil-free hot bar with grass-fed steak, wild-caught salmon, and pasture-raised chicken, alongside a full café serving smoothies, matcha, cold-pressed juices, and pastries from local bakeries. Operations run on the Vori grocery-management platform, built to support a multi-location rollout, with additional South Florida locations already in development.

Title: The Carnelian
Description: The Carnelian is a five-star boutique hotel in North Naples, Florida, on Tamiami Trail directly across from the Waterside Shops near Pine Ridge Road. Developed by the McCabe family's hospitality enterprise and designed by Smallwood, the six-story property pairs 70 generously scaled suites with Sterling's — a 70,000-square-foot private members' club spanning multiple levels and inspired by London's storied social clubs. Its Renaissance Italian villa-influenced design wraps a grand arrival court, a ground-floor public restaurant, an expansive marble-clad wellness and spa floor, and a rooftop crowned by twin pools and an open-air bar, extending the McCabe legacy of Naples boutique hospitality into an exclusive club-and-hotel landmark on the U.S. 41 corridor.
DescriptionLong: The Carnelian is a five-star boutique hotel now under construction in the heart of Naples, positioned steps from Waterside Shops and minutes from Seagate Beach. The building is anchored by Sterling's Club, the city's first hospitality-led private members' club, occupying the second, sixth, and rooftop floors with more than 70,000 square feet of member-only experiences opening in late 2027. Sterling's brings a fully realized lifestyle ecosystem under one roof: a 23,000-square-foot Wellness Club with rooftop lap pool, hydrotherapy zone, infrared sauna, and hammam steam room; a collection of dining venues including Riva (rooftop pool restaurant), The Dining Room, The Champagne Bar, The Club Room, The Drawing Room library retreat, Jetlag speakeasy, and Boulevardier cigar lounge; plus nightly entertainment programming developed in partnership with Artis–Naples and Gulfshore Playhouse. Pre-construction memberships are active, with founding members already shaping the club's culture ahead of opening.

Title: The Galleria at Fort Lauderdale
Description: A phased redevelopment of the 31.5-acre Galleria mall near East Sunrise Boulevard into a mixed-use district of up to eight 342-foot towers with 3,144 apartments, a 170-room hotel, and 1M+ square feet of commercial space.
DescriptionLong: The Galleria at Fort Lauderdale is a phased redevelopment of the 31.5-acre Galleria mall near East Sunrise Boulevard in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, into a mixed-use district of up to eight 342-foot, roughly 30-story towers. Pursued under Florida's Live Local Act by a GFO Investments and InSite Group joint venture and designed by Arquitectonica, the project pairs 3,144 rental apartments — split between workforce and market-rate homes — with a 170-room hotel, more than one million square feet of retail, office and restaurant space, a Wayfair anchor store, and over 4,700 parking spaces. The plan reimagines one of Fort Lauderdale's best-known shopping destinations as a walkable, transit-oriented urban neighborhood blending residences, hospitality and commerce along the East Sunrise corridor.
Developer: GFO Investments, Insite Group, Atlas Hill Real Estate, Prime Finance

StatusHistory.note: Redevelopment includes a 170-room hotel alongside 3,144 units in eight 342-ft towers; FAA determinations issued May 2026 per Florida YIMBY.

Wayfair signed as anchor tenant April 7 2026 — its first large-format Florida store (~94,000 sf over two levels). FAA cleared eight 342-ft towers (May 2026). 170-room hotel component. Status held at Announced.

Title: YMCA of the Palm Beaches
Description: A $75 million, 55,000-square-foot flagship YMCA community campus rising at Lake Lytal Park, anchoring an Olympic-style skate park and a new Palm Beach County aquatic center along Southern Boulevard.
DescriptionLong: The new YMCA of the Palm Beaches flagship is a 55,000-square-foot, two-story community center under construction at Lake Lytal Park, along the highly visible Southern Boulevard corridor in central Palm Beach County near Palm Beach International Airport. Delivered through a public-private partnership between the YMCA of the Palm Beaches and Palm Beach County Parks & Recreation — enabled by a 50-year county ground lease — the facility triples the size of the organization's current branch and is projected to serve an estimated 18,000 residents annually. The community center is designed to function well beyond a traditional gym, with advanced wellness facilities, a teen Youth Engagement and Resource Center, STEM and robotics labs, a music/recording studio, art studio, teaching kitchen, Y PreSchool Academy, and flexible childcare and after-school spaces. It anchors a broader $75 million, roughly 13-acre campus that also includes an Olympic-style skate park designed by Team Pain and Palm Beach County's new aquatic center — featuring a 70-meter competition pool with a 13-foot dive well and a 6,975-square-foot aquatic play zone with three slides. A Phase II expansion will add a basketball gym, indoor track, and additional youth-focused amenities. Designed by PBK Architects with Hedrick Brothers Construction as general contractor; completion targeted for late 2026 into 2027.

Title: Inscription
Description: A new boutique-style luxury rental community on Presidential Way overlooking the 10th tee of a Jack Nicklaus–designed championship golf course, now leasing.
DescriptionLong: A rental community set between I-95 and Lake Mangonia, 1.5 miles from downtown West Palm Beach. Apartments span one-, two-, and three-bedroom layouts from approximately 760 to 1,485 square feet, finished with quartz countertops, stainless steel appliances, and chef-inspired kitchens. The boutique-style community overlooks the 10th tee of a renowned Jack Nicklaus–designed championship golf course, Dutchman's Pipe. Amenities include a resort-style pool, a well-equipped fitness center, and a rooftop terrace with a pizza oven, within a gated, pet-friendly setting. Now open and actively leasing.

Title: The Belgrove Resort & Spa
Description: A British Caribbean–inspired Marriott Autograph Collection resort on a former mango grove along Lake Mangonia, anchored by a spa, three pools, and a Jack Nicklaus Signature golf experience.
DescriptionLong: The Belgrove Resort & Spa, West Palm Beach, Autograph Collection is a luxury resort blending British Caribbean charm and West Indies–inspired architecture with modern sophistication minutes from downtown West Palm Beach. Guest rooms and suites feature private balconies, bespoke decor, and pillow-top bedding, while the resort's culinary program spans Society 48 (an American brasserie), a poolside retreat, a grab-and-go café, and in-room dining. Leisure amenities include three outdoor pools with striped cabanas, a full-service spa drawing on Caribbean healing traditions and the property's mango heritage, a 24-hour fitness center, a free beach shuttle, and 21,000 square feet of indoor-outdoor event space. The resort connects to a broader lifestyle offering: a new private members' club, Dutchman's Pipe, featuring a 7,300-yard Jack Nicklaus Signature golf course, PGA-level instruction academy, and a racquet center with tennis, pickleball, and padel.

Title: Amanvari
Description: Aman's first resort in Mexico, set on Baja California Sur's secluded East Cape within the 1,500-acre Costa Palmas community near Los Cabos, with three miles of swimmable Sea of Cortez shoreline.
DescriptionLong: Amanvari is the debut Aman resort in Mexico, rising on the tranquil East Cape of Baja California Sur within Costa Palmas, a 1,500-acre master-planned coastal community roughly 45 minutes from Los Cabos International Airport. Designed by Elastic Architects and developed by Irongate, the low-slung retreat comprises 18 contemporary casitas framing sea and mountain views. Guests move between three signature restaurants — Sesui for Japanese, Arva for Italian, and Luma for coastal open-fire cooking — and a lounge for cocktails, while the Aman Spa centers on six treatment rooms, two Hydro Houses with banya and hammam, a 24-hour fitness centre, an open-air yoga pavilion, and a traditional temazcal sweat lodge. Set against the swimmable waters of the Sea of Cortez, Amanvari pairs Aman's hallmark serenity with the surrounding Costa Palmas marina, golf, and farm-to-table living, offering snorkeling, diving, boating, hiking, and horseback riding along one of Baja's most pristine stretches of coast.

Title: Southbank Residences
Description: Southbank Residences is a luxury riverfront rental development on Jacksonville's Southbank, the first new high-rise to rise along the St. Johns River downtown in over a decade. Developed by Miami-based Related Group, the project pairs the 25-story Icon Southbank tower with the eight-story Manor Southbank for a combined 395 residences, with concept design by internationally renowned architect Carlos Ott — known for the Opera Bastille in Paris — alongside MSA Architects and interiors by ID & Design International. Ott's signature sail-like, curving glass walls frame sweeping views of the river and downtown skyline. The waterfront community offers resort-caliber amenities, a marina component, and ground-floor retail, anchoring a new chapter of upscale urban living on the river's south bank.
DescriptionLong:
Southbank Residences is a $200 million, landmark mixed-use development that broke ground on May 20, 2026 along Jacksonville's St. Johns River — the city's first new luxury high-rise on the downtown riverfront in more than a decade. Developed by Related Group and designed by internationally acclaimed architect Carlos Ott (Paris's Opéra Bastille), with MSA Architects as record architect and interiors by ID & Design International, the project delivers 395 rental residences across two connected towers: the 25-story Icon Southbank and the eight-story Manor Southbank. The development is anchored by a 4,500-square-foot waterfront restaurant activating the Riverwalk and Friendship Park, a 29-slip marina, a public boat ramp, and 601 structured parking spaces. Resort-style amenities include a spa and wellness center with cold plunge and saunas, a waterfront pool, speakeasy, library, golf simulator, private theater, fitness center, sky lounge, and a penthouse-reserved rooftop clubroom and terrace. Residents have direct access to the 1.25-mile Southbank Riverwalk. Rents start at $2,000/month for a studio to over $7,000 for a three-bedroom. Completion is expected in 2028–29.</description_long>

Title: Water Street Tampa Entertainment District
Description: The Water Street Tampa Entertainment District is a walkable downtown destination anchored by a 3,500-capacity live music and entertainment venue, planned by Strategic Property Partners within Tampa's 56-acre Water Street master-planned neighborhood. Designed by global architecture firm Gensler, the flexible "open room" venue accommodates a 2,800-person general-admission floor, a 700-seat mezzanine, a 6,000-square-foot VIP lounge, and an 8,000-square-foot exterior terrace overlooking Channelside Drive. Surrounding it are a 250-key hotel, roughly 80,000 square feet of entertainment-driven retail, dining, and hospitality, and 25,000 square feet of public gathering space framing a central plaza facing Benchmark International Arena. The venue is programmed in partnership with Vinik Sports Group, extending Water Street's blend of sports, culture, and nightlife along Tampa's revitalized downtown waterfront.
DescriptionLong:
Strategic Property Partners (SPP) has revealed a new entertainment district at Water Street Tampa, formally entering the City of Tampa's development review process. Anchored by a 3,500-capacity live music and entertainment venue operated in partnership with Vinik Sports Group and a 250-key hotel, the district extends the Water Street Tampa model into entertainment-focused placemaking. The venue features a 2,800-person general admission floor, a 700-person mezzanine, 8,000+ sq ft of exterior terrace overlooking Channelside Drive, and a 6,000 sq ft VIP lounge, operating on an independent "open room" booking model. The integrated hotel adds a 12,000 sq ft amenity and pool deck with direct VIP connectivity to the venue. The district also includes roughly 80,000 sq ft of entertainment-driven retail and dining across two levels, more than 25,000 sq ft of activated public realm, a central plaza facing Benchmark International Arena, and approximately 1,000 parking spaces. Designed by Gensler, with Moss Construction and Barton Malow as joint venture general contractors. Groundbreaking is anticipated in early 2027.</description_long>

Title: The Society of the Four Arts
Description: A $250 million, 18-month renovation and expansion of two historic Mizner-era buildings on the Society of the Four Arts' 10-acre cultural campus along Royal Palm Way.
DescriptionLong: The Society of the Four Arts has begun a $250 million renovation and expansion of its 10-acre cultural campus in Midtown Palm Beach, focused on two historic buildings along Royal Palm Way just east of the Royal Park Bridge. The 18-month project overhauls the Esther B. O'Keeffe Gallery Building — originally Addison Mizner's Embassy Club and later reworked by John Volk — expanding its footprint from roughly 20,000 to nearly 31,000 square feet, enlarging and modernizing the galleries, and uncovering and restoring the original pecky cypress ceiling from Mizner's design. The adjacent Gubelmann Auditorium will gain improved acoustics, a larger stage, expanded backstage and dressing rooms, a catering area, and a loading dock, trading a modest seat reduction (700 to 650) for performance capability. The Rovensky Administrative Building — another Mizner design, built as the Embassy Apartment Building — receives a 10,356-square-foot, three-story addition, a relocated and expanded Children's Library with its own first-floor entrance and dedicated exhibition space (including works donated by Steve Wynn), new offices, and a 1,400-square-foot boardroom opening onto a 1,629-square-foot terrace. More than half of each building will be demolished while preserving their landmark facades. West Palm Beach–based Spina O'Rourke + Partners is the local architect of record, with Conkling & Lewis Construction handling the build. The King Library, Dixon Education Building, and Philip Hulitar Sculpture Garden remain open throughout. Funded entirely through trustee and member donations; completion targeted for late fall 2027, ahead of the 2027–28 season.

Title: Phoenix Arts & Innovation District
Description: Phoenix Arts & Innovation District (PHXJAX) is a mixed-use creative district in Jacksonville's North Springfield neighborhood, developed by Future of Cities. Spanning roughly 8.3 acres across a cluster of repurposed industrial warehouses and ground-up new construction, the district weaves together artist studios, creative offices, event and gathering spaces, retail, restaurants, and a planned mix of affordable, workforce, and market-rate housing. Anchored by adaptive-reuse landmarks such as Emerald Station, PHXJAX is conceived as a walkable hub for artists, makers, entrepreneurs, and small businesses, blending Jacksonville's industrial heritage with a forward-looking arts-and-innovation identity.
DescriptionLong: Phoenix Arts & Innovation District (PHXJAX) is a mixed-use creative district in Jacksonville's North Springfield neighborhood, developed by Future of Cities. Spanning roughly 8.3 acres across a cluster of repurposed industrial warehouses and ground-up new construction, the district weaves together artist studios, creative offices, event and gathering spaces, retail, restaurants, and a planned mix of affordable, workforce, and market-rate housing. Anchored by adaptive-reuse landmarks such as Emerald Station, PHXJAX is conceived as a walkable hub for artists, makers, entrepreneurs, and small businesses, blending Jacksonville's industrial heritage with a forward-looking arts-and-innovation identity.

Title: One West Twelve Residences
Description: A 23-story, 372-unit residential tower in Historic Overtown anchored by Vibe BBQ — a new restaurant and lounge concept from James Beard Award–winning chef Marcus Samuelsson.
DescriptionLong: One West Twelve Residences (ONE W12) is a 23-story, 372-unit residential tower rising on Northwest Eleventh Terrace in Miami's Historic Overtown. Developed by Property Markets Group with Lion Development Group and LNDMRK Development, and designed by CUBE 3 with interiors by Cotofana Designs, the short-term-rental-friendly building offers fully furnished studios, one-bedrooms, and one- to three-bedroom townhomes. Residents share a rooftop garden and pool deck, a fitness center, curated art installations, and a smart-building system. The ground floor is anchored by Vibe BBQ, a restaurant and lounge concept from James Beard Award-winning chef Marcus Samuelsson. The tower is built by general contractor John Moriarty & Associates.
Developer: Property Markets Group, Lion Development Group, LNDMRK Development

Title: Palm Tree Residences Miami
Description: Palm Tree Residences Miami is the first branded residential tower from Palm Tree Crew, the lifestyle and music brand led by global DJ Kygo and manager Myles Shear. Rising in the Park West district of Downtown Miami, the 37-story tower is developed by Property Markets Group alongside Lion Development Group, Eden Residential, and Sterling Equities, with architecture and interiors by Kobi Karp Architecture & Interior Design. Its fully furnished, short-term-rental-friendly residences span studios to three bedrooms and are paired with a resort-style amenity program -- pool, fitness center, padel court, spa, golf simulator, and juice bar -- plus a signature Palm Tree Crew dining and nightlife venue on the ground floor.

Title: Jean-Georges Miami Tropic Residences
Description: A 49-story, 338-unit luxury tower at the edge of the Miami Design District with culinary and lifestyle programming by two-Michelin-starred chef Jean-Georges Vongerichten.
DescriptionLong: Jean-Georges Miami Tropic Residences is a 49-story, 338-unit luxury condominium tower at the edge of the Miami Design District and Midtown. Developed by Terra and Lion Development Group and designed by Arquitectonica, the building features interiors by Yabu Pushelberg and landscape design by Clad, with culinary and lifestyle programming led by Michelin-starred chef Jean-Georges Vongerichten in his first branded-residential project. Homes range from one- to four-bedroom layouts up to a penthouse collection, paired with more than 41,000 square feet of indoor and outdoor wellness and entertainment amenities and 27,500 square feet of ground-floor retail anchored by Vongerichten's abc kitchens concept.

Title: 1250 West Avenue
Description: A 330-foot waterfront condominium tower on West Avenue in South Beach, replacing a 1964 building with 106 boutique-scale residences averaging nearly 3,500 square feet.
DescriptionLong: 1250 West Avenue is a 330-foot, 28-story waterfront condominium tower on West Avenue in Miami Beach's South Beach, developed by Terra alongside RG Development Group and GV Development and designed by ODP Architecture and Design. The boutique-scale project comprises 106 generously sized residences — floorplans ranging from roughly 1,700 to more than 5,000 square feet and averaging nearly 3,500 — crowned by two penthouses with private rooftop pools. It replaces the former Bay Garden Manor, a mid-century 238-unit condominium, on a bayfront parcel along Biscayne Bay. As part of its approvals, the development funds a new segment of the Miami Beach Baywalk and adds publicly accessible green space, trading the site's former density for a taller, lower-density design.
Architect: Odp Architecture and Design, ODP Architects

Title: Fulton Bond
Description: A full-block luxury condominium development in Fulton Market, designed by KPF with ParkFowler Plus and interiors by MAWD, planned across two phased towers rising to 33 stories.
DescriptionLong: Fulton Bond is a full-block condominium development at 1325 West Fulton Street in Chicago's Fulton Market District, bounded by North Ada Street to the west and North Elizabeth Street to the east. Designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox (KPF) in collaboration with ParkFowler Plus, the architecture interprets Fulton Market's industrial heritage through hand-set and precast brick arches, articulated podiums, and inverted bay windows expressed on the exterior as masonry arches — a contextual response rooted in the neighborhood's warehouse character. Interiors by MAWD / March and White Design layer warmth, curvature, and tonal richness across residences and shared spaces conceived to evoke a private home rather than a conventional condominium. Latest revisions (February 2026) reshape the project into two phased towers above a shared podium: a first-phase eastern tower of approximately 25 stories with a corrugated crown, followed by a 33-story western tower of approximately 500 feet clad in dark gray brick — together delivering roughly 149 to 240 residences across two- to four-bedroom layouts and penthouses, ranging from about 2,400 to over 4,500 square feet. Pricing guidance starts at $1.5M and runs into the mid-$7M range for penthouses. Shared amenities include a residents lounge with bar, fitness and wellness center, interactive golf simulator, outdoor pool with landscaped terrace, dog run, and 24-hour concierge. Developed by Sulo Development, which received City Council approval in early 2025 and completed an on-site sales center in 2025; presales are underway with delivery anticipated 2027–2029.
Architect: Kohn Pedersen Fox, Parkfowler Plus, Mawd

Title: DUS Padel
DescriptionLong: DUS Padel is the first Florida location for hospitality-driven padel brand DUS Padel, an approximately 26,000-square-foot indoor racquet club at 2031 Okeechobee Boulevard in West Palm Beach, just off I-95 near downtown. Founded by Philippe Urreiztieta, the club centers on five fully enclosed, climate-controlled indoor courts for year-round play, paired with an organic cafe, a curated wellness and retail lounge featuring the first official NOX Pro Shop in the United States, and men's and women's locker rooms with saunas, cold plunges and a spa-style recovery program led by licensed health professionals. DUS Padel positions itself at the intersection of sport, wellness and community in Palm Beach County.

Description: A 255-unit, four-story garden-style rental community on a 15.8-acre lakefront site along North Military Trail in Riviera Beach by The Estate Companies.
DescriptionLong: Soleste on the Trail is a 255-unit, four-story garden-style apartment community spread across roughly 15.8 acres along North Military Trail in Riviera Beach, Florida, developed by The Estate Companies under its prolific South Florida 'Soleste' multifamily brand. The market-rate community pairs one- to three-bedroom residences with quartz counters and stainless appliances around a two-acre lake ringed by a walking trail. Resort-style amenities include a pool, fitness center and spa, coworking lounge, dog park and children's playground, positioning it as a nature-oriented rental enclave northwest of West Palm Beach.

Title: The Berkeley
Description: A 25-story Arquitectonica-designed condominium overlooking Clear Lake, with 193 residences including four penthouses and interiors by founding principal Bernardo Fort-Brescia.
DescriptionLong: The Berkeley Palm Beach is a 25-story residential tower rising at 550 South Australian Avenue in downtown West Palm Beach's Clear Lake District, set just west of the Intracoastal and the CityPlace open-air complex. The building will introduce 193 luxury residences — including four penthouses — ranging from two to five bedrooms across roughly 2,500 to 4,800 square feet, with 9- to 11-foot ceilings, east- and west-facing flow-through layouts capturing sunrise and sunset exposures, oversized private terraces, Italian-crafted Snaidero kitchens, spa-style bathrooms, and integrated smart-home systems. Amenities are distributed across multiple levels: a 7th-floor family deck with pool, spa, fitness center, teen lounge, and children's play area, and a rooftop retreat with adult pool and jacuzzi, private dining, cigar lounge, nightclub, and panoramic views toward Palm Beach Island. Additional building features include wellness studios, a golf simulator, co-working spaces, indoor lounges, a gourmet resident market, full-time concierge and valet, UPLIN Health's in-home health services, and a building-wide emergency standby generator. Architecture is by Arquitectonica with interiors by founding principal Bernardo Fort-Brescia. The project is developed by Australian Property Group — led by Al Adelson, the developer behind The Bristol, the 2019 building widely credited with establishing West Palm Beach's modern luxury high-rise market — in partnership with Sympatico Real Estate, with exclusive sales by Douglas Elliman Development Marketing. Pricing runs from approximately $2 million to $10 million, with an average price of about $3.5 million, and the project has surpassed $100 million in pre-construction sales. Groundbreaking is anticipated for June 2026, with delivery targeted for 2028.

Title: Shorecrest Flagler Drive
Description: A boutique 28-story waterfront condominium by Related Ross on North Flagler Drive in West Palm Beach, with 98 water-facing residences and a rooftop amenity deck overlooking the Lake Worth Lagoon.
DescriptionLong: Shorecrest is a boutique waterfront condominium tower by Related Ross on North Flagler Drive in West Palm Beach — the 'Billionaire's Corridor,' on the former Temple Israel parcel — overlooking the Lake Worth Lagoon and Palm Beach. Designed by Roger Ferris + Partners with interiors by Rottet Studio, it holds 98 water-facing residences at four homes per floor, served by an 18,355-square-foot amenity program anchored by an Equinox-curated fitness and wellness suite, plus a rooftop deck with a 75-foot lap pool, cold plunge and spa, a golf simulator, and private dining.

StatusHistory.note: Related Ross broke ground April 2, 2026 ($157M loan); demolition/site prep just commencing with vertical "to follow" — recorded as construction, likely belongs at breaking-ground.

Title: Mr. C Residences West Palm Beach
Description: Hospitality‑focused hotel and residences, bringing the Mr. C brand’s European service to West Palm Beach for the first time.
DescriptionLong: Mr. C Hotel & Residences West Palm Beach broke ground in August 2025 as the first Mr. C property in Palm Beach County, rising in the heart of downtown West Palm Beach as a 27-story tower combining 110 hotel suites and 146 branded condominium residences. Designed by Arquitectonica with interiors by Meyer Davis, the building draws on the Italian Riviera of the 1950s and the city's sailing culture, with refined finishes, expansive layouts, and large windows framing views of the Intracoastal Waterway and downtown skyline; select residences include corner exposures and generous terraces. The amenity program centers on a garden-level lap pool with private cabanas and a poolside bar and lounge, alongside a fitness center with dedicated rooms for yoga, pilates, and Peloton workouts, and a spa with steam and sauna facilities. Residents also share a library, business lounge, children's play area, curated art installations, and a private members' lounge, all supported by a custom Mr. C Hotel & Residences digital app for building services and concierge requests. The project is developed by Miami-based Terra in partnership with Sympatico Real Estate, with exclusive sales by Douglas Elliman Florida; pre-sales had crossed 70 percent at groundbreaking. The Mr. C brand was founded by the Cipriani family in 2011 with its first hotel in Beverly Hills, later expanding to New York, Miami, and Dubai, and West Palm Beach extends that European-inspired hospitality program into Palm Beach County for the first time.

StatusHistory.note: 26-story Cipriani-branded condo/hotel tower per David Siddons Group

Title: Mandarin Oriental Residences, West Palm Beach
Description: The brand’s first standalone residential project in South Florida — rising 31 stories along North Flagler Drive and featuring 87 private residences ranging from two to four bedrooms.
DescriptionLong: Mandarin Oriental Residences, West Palm Beach is a 31-story, 87-unit luxury condominium tower on a 2.5-acre Intracoastal waterfront site at 5400 North Flagler Drive in West Palm Beach's North End. Developed by Toronto-based Great Gulf under the Mandarin Oriental flag, it stands as the brand's first standalone branded residences in South Florida. The tower is designed by Safdie Architects, led by Moshe Safdie, with interiors by Studio Munge and landscape design by Enea Landscape Architecture. The residences range from two- to four-bedroom layouts to multi-level villas and a full-floor penthouse, complemented by Mandarin Oriental's signature service and waterfront amenities.
Developer: Great Gulf, Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group

Title: Apogee Residences
Description: Boutique ultra‑luxury condo tower in South of Fifth known for privacy, deep terraces, and hotel‑caliber amenities and services.
DescriptionLong: Apogee Residences is an ultra-luxury waterfront condominium planned at 4906 North Flagler Drive in the North End of West Palm Beach, along the Intracoastal Waterway. Developed by The Related Group and designed by Arquitectonica, the 21-story tower offers 39 residences with reduced street-level massing, screened parking, new sidewalks and public art, two pools and a sun deck, and direct marina and waterfront access in one of West Palm Beach's fastest-growing residential corridors.

Title: Viceroy Aventura
Description: Planned luxury hotel and residential development delivering Viceroy service, rooftop leisure spaces, and destination dining.
DescriptionLong: Viceroy Residences Aventura is a 28-story, 274-unit luxury condominium tower at 2999 NE 191st Street in Aventura, Florida, developed as a joint venture between The Related Group and BH Group, which also bases its headquarters in the building. Carrying the Viceroy hospitality flag, the mixed-use tower combines branded residences with a roughly 105,000-square-foot office component and ground-floor retail. Architecture is by Arquitectonica with interiors by DesignAgency, and residents enjoy roughly 36,000 square feet of amenities including a pool and spa, fitness center, rock-climbing wall, basketball half-court, pickleball courts, a pet spa, children's and teen lounges, and a rooftop bar and terrace.

Title: The Residences at Mandarin Oriental, Miami
Description: Swire's two-tower, ~$1.3B-presold ultra-luxury Mandarin Oriental residential and hotel redevelopment at the tip of Brickell Key, with the original hotel imploded in April 2026 to clear the site.
DescriptionLong: The Residences at Mandarin Oriental, Miami is a two-tower ultra-luxury development at the southern tip of Brickell Key, replacing the original Mandarin Oriental hotel that Swire Properties imploded in a controlled demolition on April 12, 2026. Designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox, the 66-story South Tower holds 228 private residences and the 34-story North Tower combines 70 private residences, 28 turnkey Hotel Collection residences, and a new 121-key Mandarin Oriental hotel. The project has logged roughly $1.3 billion in residential sales, with completion targeted for 2030.
Developer: Swire Properties, Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group

Title: Ziggurat Coconut Grove
Description: Conceptual mixed‑use project with terraced, gardened architecture envisioned for luxury residences and boutique retail/office.
DescriptionLong:
A sculptural mixed-use sanctuary in the heart of Coconut Grove, organized around 19 boutique residences, 100,000 square feet of Class-A office space, and 40,000 square feet of curated retail. The building's stepped, oolite-clad silhouette draws inspiration from ancient ziggurats and rises organically from the Grove's tree canopy — weaving native plantings, warm woods, and living greenery through every floor. Residences feature 11-foot ceilings, private elevators with double-door entry, 12-foot-deep terraces with full summer kitchens, European oak floors, travertine throughout, and chef's kitchens with Italian stone counters and a Wolf and Sub-Zero appliance package. Primary suites are finished with Dornbracht fixtures, deep soaking tubs, and dual-function rain showers, while integrated smart-home technology runs throughout. 

The rooftop is reserved as a private wellness sanctuary anchored by a hydrotherapy circuit, resort-style pool, private cabanas, a chef's grilling station, and a MICHELIN-starred restaurant with panoramic views of Biscayne Bay. At street level, a landscaped paseo threads couture ateliers, an artisanal café, a global gourmet market, and additional MICHELIN-starred dining — extending the wellness experience outward toward Kirk Munroe Park, set for a $6.3 million renovation directly across the street.

Title: Okan Tower
Description: Okan Tower is a 72-story, tulip-shaped supertall in downtown Miami's Park West district, delivering a Hilton-branded hotel, condo-hotel and private Sky Residences, and Class-A office space in one of the city's tallest towers.
DescriptionLong: Okan Tower is a 902-foot, 72-story mixed-use supertall rising at 555 North Miami Avenue in the Park West district of downtown Miami, developed by Turkish-owned Okan Group and designed by Behar Font & Partners. Its tulip-inspired silhouette — a nod to Turkey's national flower — houses a 316-key Hilton Miami Bayfront hotel, 236 Hilton-managed condo-hotel residences, 163 private Sky Residences and Class-A office floors. Residents and guests share more than 80,000 square feet of amenities, including pools, a spa and wellness center, restaurants and panoramic skyline and Biscayne Bay views. One of the tallest towers on Miami's skyline, Okan Tower anchors the rapidly densifying downtown core.

Title: Ponce Park
Description: An 11-story, 58-residence European-inspired mixed-use luxury condominium in Coral Gables by The Allen Morris Company, with interiors by Meyer Davis and 25,000 sf of retail.
DescriptionLong: Ponce Park is an 11-story, European-inspired mixed-use luxury condominium in Coral Gables, Florida, at 3000 Ponce de Leon Boulevard. Developed by The Allen Morris Company, with architecture by John Cunningham in collaboration with Zyscovich and interiors by Meyer Davis, the building holds 58 residences ranging from roughly 1,900 to more than 6,500 square feet, including two-to-five-bedroom homes and penthouses. A rooftop pool, fitness studio and sky bar crown the project, while about 25,000 square feet of ground-floor retail activates one of Coral Gables' premier boulevards.

Title: Lake Eola Expansion
Description: Orlando's beloved Lake Eola Park is undergoing a $30 million expansion project, expected to be completed in phases. The first phase, which includes a new playground and interactive fountain, opened in 2023. Future phases will see the addition of a pedestrian bridge, a restaurant, and an event pavilion.
DescriptionLong: Orlando's beloved Lake Eola Park is undergoing a $30 million expansion project, expected to be completed in phases. The first phase, which includes a new playground and interactive fountain, opened in 2023. Future phases will see the addition of a pedestrian bridge, a restaurant, and an event pavilion.

StatusHistory.note: Orlando approved a $3.9M pre-construction deal with Turner for the Lake Eola Gateway + pocket park; first-phase construction expected to start summer 2026

Title: Westcourt Orlando
Description: The Orlando Magic are investing hundreds of millions of dollars into a new entertainment district surrounding the Amway Center, bringing a new hotel, office tower, and entertainment venues.
DescriptionLong: Westcourt is a roughly $500 million mixed-use sports and entertainment district on 8.4 acres just north of the Kia Center in downtown Orlando, developed by a joint venture of JMA Ventures, the Machete Group and the DeVos family. The development brings together Orlando's first Kimpton hotel, luxury residences, Class-A office, retail and dining, a Live Nation live-music venue, structured parking and a 1.5-acre central green — an “urban living room” — creating a year-round destination at the heart of Orlando's downtown entertainment core.

Title: Hotel Ora + Private Residences
Description: A new Class A+ commercial anchor integrating a full city block with retail, a pedestrian paseo, and a rooftop amenity deck connected by a skybridge—complete with green space, café, and gathering areas overlooking downtown and the Intracoastal.
DescriptionLong: Hotel Ora + Private Residences is a 39-story condo-hotel tower planned at 1101 East Jackson Street near Tampa's Water Street district, developed by ARC Realty Group with Prosper Group. Designed by Adache Group Architects, the roughly $675 million project pairs 627 flexible-ownership hotel suites with 31 private residences and penthouses on its upper floors, featuring high ceilings, private terraces and private elevator access. Pitched as Tampa's first true condo-hotel, it offers owners income-generating flexibility in one of downtown Tampa's fastest-rising corridors.
Developer: ARC Realty Group, Prosper Group, Sunwest Construction

Title: 10 CityPlace
Description: A Class A office tower in downtown West Palm Beach by Related Ross, designed by KPF, anchored by ServiceNow — part of the ~1M sf 10 & 15 CityPlace expansion.
DescriptionLong: 10 CityPlace is a Class A office tower in downtown West Palm Beach, Florida, part of Related Ross's roughly one-million-square-foot 10 & 15 CityPlace office expansion in the CityPlace district. Developed by Related Ross (Stephen Ross) and designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox, the tower delivers roughly 470,000 square feet of premium workspace with ground-floor retail, wellness-focused amenities, private terraces and a top-floor sky garden. Technology company ServiceNow has signed on as anchor tenant for a West Palm innovation hub and AI institute, reinforcing the city's rapid emergence as a corporate-relocation destination in South Florida.

StatusHistory.note: Related Ross closed a record $772M construction loan covering 10 and 15 CityPlace — the largest in Florida history

Title: West Palm Point
Description: A new Class A+ commercial anchor that integrates a full city block with retail, a pedestrian paseo, and a rooftop amenity deck connected by a skybridge—complete with green space, café, and gathering areas overlooking downtown and the Intracoastal.
DescriptionLong: West Palm Point is a Class A speculative office tower rising on a full downtown city block at Okeechobee and Quadrille boulevards in West Palm Beach, developed by Cohen Brothers Realty Corporation. Designed by Pelli Clarke & Partners with Nichols Brosch Wurst Wolf, the 26-story, roughly 400,000-square-foot tower offers column-free floor plates of about 15,000 to 22,000 square feet beneath soaring lobbies. Amenities include a private landscaped rooftop park, a fitness center, conference rooms, and ground-floor retail, served by an adjoining 10-story parking garage. CBRE serves as exclusive leasing agent for one of downtown West Palm Beach's premier new office addresses.

Title: The Marketplace of Wellington
Description: Planned lifestyle retail redevelopment introducing open‑air shopping, dining, and gathering spaces for the Wellington community.
DescriptionLong: The Marketplace of Wellington is a walkable mixed-use town center planned at 13421 South Shore Boulevard in Wellington, Florida, developed by Wellington Lifestyle Partners. Styled after destinations like Worth Avenue and Greenwich Avenue, the roughly 17.9-acre district pairs an 80-key luxury boutique hotel with 89 residences, about 117,000 square feet of Class-A office and commercial space, and shops, restaurants and landscaped common areas — an everyday luxury anchor for the equestrian community surrounding it.

Title: OLIN Palm Beach
Description: Placeholder name for an ultra‑luxury island condominium focused on large floorplans, classic architecture, and concierge services.
DescriptionLong: OLIN Palm Beach is an ultra-luxury oceanfront condominium planned along the South End's “Condominium Row” at 2720 and 2730 South Ocean Boulevard in Palm Beach, developed by OKO Group and Cain International. Designed by OMA, the project comprises three low-rise, five-story buildings with roughly 41 expansive residences on a 4.9-acre oceanfront site — among the first new-construction condominiums on the island in two decades — offering direct beach access and resort-caliber amenities in one of the most exclusive enclaves in South Florida.

Title: Safe Harbor Rybovich District
Description: Marine‑oriented mixed‑use district around a super‑yacht marina adding luxury residences, hotel, promenades, and maritime retail.
DescriptionLong: Safe Harbor Rybovich District is a $2 billion waterfront mixed-use destination on North Flagler Drive in West Palm Beach, Florida, rising around the iconic Safe Harbor Rybovich superyacht marina on the Intracoastal Waterway. Master-planned by Kohn Pedersen Fox (KPF) for developers Huizenga Holdings and Integra Investments, the roughly 19-acre district weaves luxury condominium towers, a boutique hotel, Class A office space, and curated waterfront retail and dining around a members-only marina club and a public Intracoastal promenade. Positioned at the heart of West Palm Beach's booming waterfront, the development pairs world-class marine and yachting amenities with resort-style residential living, reinforcing the city's emergence as a premier South Florida destination for boating, business, and waterfront lifestyle.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: North End (per our WPB coverage)

Title: Cleveland Clinic Hospital
Description: Regional hospital expansion delivering advanced cancer care, surgical suites, and patient‑centered design across new facilities.
DescriptionLong: Cleveland Clinic Hospital is a new full-service hospital planned for the heart of downtown West Palm Beach, Florida, establishing the world-renowned Cleveland Clinic health system's flagship Palm Beach County presence. The roughly 200-bed acute-care hospital will deliver advanced emergency, surgical, and specialty care, complemented by a companion 120,000-square-foot outpatient and ambulatory surgery center within the nearby 15 CityPlace tower. Backed by a $500 million philanthropic campaign, the campus anchors West Palm Beach's fast-growing medical and innovation district, expanding access to nationally ranked specialty medicine for one of Florida's wealthiest and fastest-growing regions.

StatusHistory.note: Site demolition/prep slated Q3 2026, hospital construction early 2027, opening end of 2029 (start corrected from 2025)
Hospital construction to begin early 2027; completion targeted December 2029 (site demo/prep Q3 2026)

Title: Glazer Hall

Title: Vanderbilt University
Description: Prestigious university’s planned graduate campus expansion with modern classrooms, labs, and executive‑education spaces.
DescriptionLong: Vanderbilt University West Palm Beach is the Nashville-based university's planned downtown graduate campus in West Palm Beach, Florida, designed by Elkus Manfredi Architects. The campus will serve roughly 1,000 graduate students with programs spanning finance, management, engineering, computing, and space and defense technology, weaving artificial intelligence and data science throughout its curriculum. Assembled from a combination of city and county land and a major downtown parcel donation, the urban campus is positioned to feed talent into West Palm Beach's surging finance and technology corridor, cementing the city's reputation as a national hub for business, innovation, and higher education.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Downtown West Palm Beach (per our WPB coverage)

Title: Bath and Racquet Residences
Description: A 256-residence redevelopment of Sarasota's historic Bath & Racquet Club, combining condominiums with a 43,500-square-foot private racquet, fitness and wellness club.
DescriptionLong: Bath and Racquet Residences is a 256-residence, four-story luxury redevelopment of the historic Bath & Racquet Club site in Sarasota, Florida, developed by Silver Sky Global Capital with Federal Finance Group. Built in phases with concrete construction and hurricane-rated glazing, the community revives the club's heritage through a 43,500-square-foot private racquet, fitness and wellness club at its heart. Residents enjoy resort-style amenities alongside tennis and pickleball courts and spa facilities, blending Sarasota's mid-century recreational legacy with contemporary condominium living.
Developer: Silver Sky Global Capital, Federal Finance Group

Title: Ritz-Carlton Residences, South Beach
Description: An ultra-luxury oceanfront condominium in South Beach, Miami Beach by Sobe Sky Development, designed by Kobi Karp — 30 custom 'beach house' residences with José Andrés dining.
DescriptionLong: The Ritz-Carlton Residences, South Beach is an ultra-luxury oceanfront condominium in Miami Beach, tied to the Sagamore and Ritz-Carlton hospitality at the heart of South Beach near Lincoln Road. Developed by Sobe Sky Development — a partnership of Flag Luxury Group, Ben-Josef Group and Lionstone Development — with architecture by Kobi Karp and interiors by Studio Munge, the boutique tower offers 30 custom 'beach house' residences, including two duplex penthouses with private rooftop pools. More than 50,000 square feet of amenities and dining curated with Michelin-starred chef José Andrés frame a rarefied beachfront lifestyle in one of the world's most recognized resort destinations.
Developer: Lionstone Development, Flag Luxury Group, Ben-Josef Group

Title: The Alton Miami Beach
Description: Contemporary mixed‑use project adding offices, retail, and residences with sustainable features to Miami Beach’s urban core.
DescriptionLong: Planned for the 1656–1680 Alton Road site in Miami Beach's West Avenue corridor, this 14-story, 184-residence mixed-use development from Infinity Collective brings new luxury living to the South Beach gateway. Designed by PBDW Architects, the project pairs its residences with lifestyle retail and office space, structured parking, and resort-style amenities including a rooftop pool and a padel court. Its walkable Alton Road location places residents minutes from Sunset Harbour, Lincoln Road, and the shoreline of Biscayne Bay.

Title: Continuum Waterfront District
Description: Phase Two will introduce a second Continuum-branded condominium tower, a boutique hotel with a restaurant, and a reimagined marina, alongside outdoor wellness areas, civic gathering spaces, and ground-floor retail and cafés.
DescriptionLong: Continuum Waterfront District is a luxury waterfront condominium development on the JFK Causeway in North Bay Village, Florida, from the Continuum Company — the Eichner family's celebrated brand behind South Beach's Continuum towers. Its first phase rises as a 36-story tower of 236 residences, framed by a waterfront restaurant and direct marina access on a roughly 3.7-acre Biscayne Bay assemblage. Part of a broader master plan that envisions additional residences, a hotel and a private marina, the district is positioned as a transformative bayfront address midway between Miami Beach and the mainland, reshaping North Bay Village's skyline and waterfront.

Title: LivWrk Towers
Description: LivWrk Wynwood is an ambitious three-tower residential community proposed for the heart of Miami's Wynwood Arts District.
DescriptionLong: LivWrk Wynwood is an ambitious three-tower residential community proposed for the heart of Miami's Wynwood Arts District, occupying a five-lot assemblage at North Miami Avenue and Northwest 24th Street. Developed by Brooklyn-based LivWrk under Florida's Live Local Act, the roughly 1.8-million-square-foot project would introduce three 45-story towers delivering 1,363 apartments, of which about 40 percent are reserved as workforce housing. Designed by MKDA, the towers pair studio-to-three-bedroom residences with ground-floor retail, a shared parking podium, ninth-floor amenity decks, and resident sky lounges. The development weaves art-forward street-level design into Wynwood's creative fabric while addressing Miami's acute demand for attainable urban housing.

StatusHistory.note: LivWrk plans massive three-tower, 1,363-unit apartment project in Wynwood Arts District

Title: Jungle Island
Description: Urban eco‑park and attraction undergoing reinvention with new adventure, event, and hospitality components.
DescriptionLong: Jungle Island is a master-planned waterfront redevelopment of the eco-adventure park on Watson Island in Miami, a joint venture of Terra and ESJ Capital Partners. The plan pairs two residential towers of up to 48 stories — roughly 600 condominiums — with a reimagined eco-adventure attraction and hotel, anchored by a roughly 13-acre public Biscayne Bay park and a bayfront promenade. Minutes from Downtown Miami and South Beach across the MacArthur Causeway, it is positioned as a new public-realm destination linking the city to the bay.

Title: Royal Caribbean: Terminal G
Description: New cruise terminal delivering streamlined passenger processing, lounge spaces, and next‑gen boarding technology at PortMiami.
DescriptionLong: Terminal G is a $345 million dedicated cruise terminal for Royal Caribbean Group at PortMiami in Miami-Dade County, Florida. Designed by Perez & Perez Architects & Planners and delivered design-build by the Lemartec–NV2A Joint Venture, the LEED-targeted complex is engineered to berth the world's largest Icon-class cruise ships and to process up to 7,000 passengers. It pairs a multi-level parking garage with an intermodal ground-transportation facility, reinforcing PortMiami's standing as the 'Cruise Capital of the World' through a public-private partnership between Miami-Dade County and Royal Caribbean Group.
Developer: Royal Caribbean Group, Port of Miami
