Title: Viceroy Residences Fort Lauderdale
Description: A 45-story tower bringing Viceroy-hospitality with 370 residences and 30,000+ sq ft of amenities in Flagler Village, between Las Olas and the arts district.
DescriptionLong:
A 45-story, 500-foot Viceroy-branded condominium tower rising in Flagler Village between Las Olas Boulevard and the city's creative arts district, with 370 studio to four-bedroom residences including four crown penthouses.
 
 More than 30,000 square feet of Viceroy-curated amenities span 35-plus offerings across the 11th and 46th floors — two resort-style pools with cabana and poolside service, a full-service spa with sauna and steam rooms, a health club with yoga and boxing studios, indoor-outdoor coworking with private conference rooms, a 46th-floor sky lounge, juice bar, garden lounge, and outdoor grilling pavilions — all anchored by signature Viceroy services including a 24-hour attended lobby, full-service concierge, valet, and bespoke private service scheduling.

Title: 633 SE Third Avenue
Description: Downtown office or mixed‑use tower adding modern workspaces, parking, and street‑level retail near the riverfront.
DescriptionLong: 633 SE Third Avenue is a planned 47-story residential rental tower in downtown Fort Lauderdale, Florida, rising near the Broward County courthouse and the New River. Developed by Dependable Equities with the Rabsky Group and designed by New York architecture firm ODA, the sculptural high-rise stacks shifting, tubular glass volumes into one of the tallest forms on the Fort Lauderdale skyline. The project pairs roughly 830 apartments with ground-floor retail and a multi-level parking structure, bringing a landmark architectural statement and a major new supply of rental housing to the city's fast-growing downtown core.

Description: Mixed‑use residential tower number on Broadway delivering apartments/condos with amenities and ground‑floor retail.
DescriptionLong: 1117 Broadway is a 508-unit, for-sale condominium development in downtown Riviera Beach, Florida, developed by SobelCo on a former boatyard across from the Port of Palm Beach. Designed by GarciaStromberg, the roughly 1.23-million-square-foot project comprises four towers — two of 20 stories and two of 13 stories — with one- and two-bedroom residences, ground-floor retail and structured parking, bringing attainable waterfront-adjacent condominium living to a fast-redeveloping stretch of Florida's Treasure Coast.

Title: Wellington Sports Academy
Description: Youth‑focused elite sports academy campus with professional‑grade fields, indoor training, dorms, and sports‑science facilities.
DescriptionLong: Wellington Sports Academy is a roughly 115,000-square-foot indoor-and-outdoor multi-sport training complex in Wellington, Florida, founded by NFL veteran Jon Bostic through his Wellington Athletics venture in a public-private partnership with the Village of Wellington at Village Park. Built to support a dozen sports — from football and basketball to soccer, baseball and beyond — the academy delivers professional-grade training facilities, courts and fields for youth and elite athletes alike. Designed by Birse Thomas with construction by Verdex, the complex brings a major athletic-development hub to Palm Beach County, hosting tournaments, camps and year-round training that position Wellington Sports Academy as a premier destination for competitive youth and amateur sports in South Florida.

Title: Alhambra Parc
Description: Planned mixed‑use village introducing townhomes, greenways, and neighborhood retail in a Mediterranean‑inspired plan.
DescriptionLong: Alhambra Parc is a landmark mixed-use development at 33 Alhambra Circle in downtown Coral Gables, Florida, and MG Developer's tenth project in the city. The eight-story building pairs 74 luxury residences with roughly 13,000 square feet of office space and about 18,000 square feet of ground-floor retail, bringing a refined live-work-shop environment to the heart of the Gables. Developed by MG Developer with Vertical Developments, Alhambra Parc reflects the Mediterranean-influenced character of its surroundings while delivering contemporary boutique condominium living in one of South Florida's most prestigious downtowns.

Title: Summit Brickell
Description: A planned ~65-story, ~500-unit residential tower in Miami's Brickell, FAA-cleared to ~765 feet, by Lore Development Group and Corwil Architects.
DescriptionLong: Summit Brickell is a planned ultra-tall mixed-use residential tower in Miami's Brickell financial district, at 1015 SW 1st Avenue, cleared by the FAA to rise about 765 feet. Developed by Lore Development Group — a venture tied to Daniel Dantas's Opportunity and the Leste Group — and designed by Corwil Architects, the roughly 65-story skyscraper would deliver around 500 residences above structured parking and ground-floor commercial space. Positioned among Brickell's tallest towers, Summit Brickell is designed to capitalize on sweeping bay and skyline views in one of the densest, most walkable urban cores in the United States.

Title: EDITION Residences Miami Edgewater
Description: A 55-story EDITION-branded luxury condominium on Biscayne Bay in Miami's Edgewater by Two Roads Development, designed by Arquitectonica — 185 waterfront residences.
DescriptionLong: EDITION Residences, Miami Edgewater is a 55-story branded luxury condominium on Biscayne Bay in Miami's Edgewater neighborhood, at 2121 North Bayshore Drive on the former Biscayne 21 site. Developed by Two Roads Development, with architecture by Arquitectonica and interiors by Studio Munge, the tower carries the EDITION hospitality brand across 185 waterfront residences ranging from one to four bedrooms, roughly 1,950 to 3,815 square feet. Private elevator entry, deep bayfront terraces, floor-to-ceiling glass and EDITION-caliber wellness, lounge and pool-deck amenities define a hotel-serviced lifestyle on one of Miami's most coveted stretches of waterfront.

Title: Wingrove Academy
Description: Academy campus plan with classrooms, labs, athletic facilities, and community programming for lifelong learning.
DescriptionLong: Wingrove Academy is a not-for-profit private school planned for the K-Park site in Wellington, Florida, backed by developer Stephen Ross and his Related Ross firm and operated by the West Palm Beach–based education group ElevatED. Designed by Elkus Manfredi Architects, the PreK–12 campus is envisioned for up to 1,700 students at full build-out, opening first for its younger grades before expanding to a full high school program. Wingrove pairs a rigorous, STEM-forward academic model with a strong emphasis on athletics, complemented by a scholarship program aimed at broadening access for local Wellington families. Positioned on one of Wellington's most prominent remaining parcels, Wingrove Academy is set to become a marquee educational institution in Palm Beach County.

Title: La Baia North
Description: A boutique eight-story, 57-residence waterfront condominium in Bay Harbor Islands by The Continuum Company, designed by Luis Revuelta — the final phase of the La Baia enclave.
DescriptionLong: La Baia North is a boutique waterfront luxury condominium in Bay Harbor Islands, Florida, at 9481 East Bay Harbor Drive, the second and final phase of the La Baia enclave following the sellout of La Baia South. Developed by The Continuum Company with architecture by Luis Revuelta and interiors by Durukan Design, the eight-story building holds 57 residences in two-to-four-bedroom layouts of roughly 1,400 to 2,100 square feet. More than 20,000 square feet of amenities and five-star services — including yacht-club access on Biscayne Bay — anchor a refined bayfront lifestyle minutes from Bal Harbour and Miami Beach.

Title: Edgeworth
Description: An ultra-luxury two-tower waterfront condominium in downtown West Palm Beach by Related Ross, designed by KPF — 168 residences with 90,000+ sf of amenities.
DescriptionLong: Edgeworth is an ultra-luxury waterfront condominium in downtown West Palm Beach, Florida, rising as two curved towers along South Flagler Drive overlooking the Intracoastal Waterway and Palm Beach. Developed by Related Ross (Stephen Ross) with architecture by Kohn Pedersen Fox and interiors by March and White Design, the project comprises 168 residences, including nine penthouses, in one-to-five-bedroom layouts. More than 90,000 square feet of Riviera-inspired amenities anchor a resort-caliber wellness, spa and fitness program, positioning Edgeworth among the marquee additions to West Palm Beach's fast-rising luxury waterfront.

Title: Banyan Tree Residences
Description: Banyan Tree is officially making its United States debut with its first-ever residential development in downtown West Palm Beach.
DescriptionLong: Banyan Tree Residences is the Banyan Group's first U.S. residential project, an 88-unit luxury condominium rising at 400 Hibiscus Street in downtown West Palm Beach, Florida, steps from CityPlace and the Intracoastal waterfront. Designed by the renowned Office for Metropolitan Architecture (OMA) with interiors by Yabu Pushelberg and landscaping by Enzo Enea, the sculptural dual-form tower is developed by Mast Capital and Curated JCZM Development under the globally celebrated Banyan Tree hospitality brand. Residences range from one-to-four bedrooms to penthouses and are paired with a signature wellness program -- including cryotherapy, a hammam, spa, and meditation spaces -- bringing Banyan Tree's Asian-inspired sanctuary living to the heart of West Palm Beach's surging luxury condominium market.
Developer: Mast Capital, Curated JCZM Development, Banyan Group

Title: The Wellington
Description: A 400-acre ultra-luxury residential and private club community, anchored by a David McLay Kidd-designed championship golf course and world-class equestrian facilities.
DescriptionLong: The Wellington is an ultra-luxury private residential club community in Wellington, Florida, developed by Wellington Lifestyle Partners and managed by NEXUS Luxury Collection. Spanning roughly 400 acres, it pairs an equestrian-led lifestyle with an 18-hole championship golf course by David McLay Kidd, a master plan and core amenities by Workshop/APD, and 253 residences across five home types — from custom estates and equestrian villas to townhomes, condominiums and farm estates. World-class equestrian facilities, golf, wellness and an adjacent walkable Marketplace town center round out one of South Florida's most exclusive new communities.
Architect: Workshop/APD, D. Stanley Dixon Architect, Morris Adjmi Architects, Squire & Partners

Title: Good Samaritan Medical District
Description: A massive, futuristic next-generation medical campus surrounded by housing, hospitality, retail, and longevity-driven innovation.
DescriptionLong: Good Samaritan Medical District is a $2-3 billion vertical medical and mixed-use redevelopment of the historic waterfront Good Samaritan campus on North Flagler Drive in West Palm Beach, Florida. Master-planned by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill (SOM) for developer Easton Street Capital with Tenet Healthcare's Palm Beach Health Network, the district reimagines the Intracoastal-front site as a next-generation center of medical excellence anchored by a replacement hospital, two roughly 25-story luxury condominium towers oriented toward longevity and independent living, workforce rental housing, a hotel, and wellness-driven retail. The project fuses world-class healthcare with resort-style waterfront residences, positioning West Palm Beach at the forefront of the booming longevity and medical-tourism economy.

StatusHistory.note: New hospital construction to start mid-2027 (~30-month build); $2–3B district w/ Easton Street Capital

Title: The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Fort Lauderdale Beach
Description: An oceanfront Ritz-Carlton-branded condominium in Fort Lauderdale Beach — two 13-story towers, 83 residences — by MICL Group and Admire Capital, designed by Garcia Stromberg.
DescriptionLong: The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Fort Lauderdale Beach is an oceanfront branded condominium at 551 Bayshore Drive, set on a barrier-island parcel between the Atlantic Ocean and the Intracoastal Waterway. Developed by MICL Group and Admire Capital, with architecture by Garcia Stromberg and interiors by Dan Fink Studio, the two 13-story towers hold 83 residences — including six penthouses — in two-and-three-bedroom-plus-den layouts of roughly 1,550 to 3,480 square feet. Ritz-Carlton service underpins an amenity suite of two pools, a zen garden, private cabanas, dock slips, a resident lounge, screening room, sports simulator and children's playroom, delivering full-service beachfront living in Fort Lauderdale.

StatusHistory.note: Fill spec 83 units/13 floors (dual-tower); completion now estimated 2029 per YIMBY, correcting delivery from 2027

Title: Bone Valley at Streamsong
Description: A major new chapter with the upcoming debut of its new luxury on-site Golf Cabins and a fifth championship golf course, Bone Valley, designed by David McLay Kidd.
DescriptionLong: One of the world’s best golf destinations has officially unveiled its bold new fifth championship golf course, Bone Valley. Designed by acclaimed architect David McLay Kidd, known for his work at world-class destinations like Bandon Dunes — Bone Valley will bring another elite track to Streamsong, a property already home to three Top 40 public courses in the United States. With Bone Valley, Kidd joins an extraordinary roster of modern architectural icons at Streamsong - Coore & Crenshaw, Tom Doak, and Hanse & Wagner - forming the “Mount Rushmore” of modern minimalist golf designers. Preview play begins October 30, 2026, ahead of a January 26, 2027 opening, alongside new lakeside Golf Cabins designed for group stays.

StatusHistory.note: Streamsong unveiled Bone Valley, its fifth championship golf course, designed by David McLay Kidd.

Title: Nora House
Description: The Nora District's first residential project, bringing 117 residences and ground-floor retail to the center of one of West Palm Beach’s fastest-growing neighborhoods.
DescriptionLong: Nora House is the first for-sale condominium in West Palm Beach's Nora District, an 11-story building of 117 residences developed by The Ronto Group with the district's master developers NDT Development, Place Projects and Wheelock Street Capital. Designed by Swedroe Architecture with interiors by Lillian Wu Studio, it offers two- to four-bedroom homes plus expansive terrace residences above nearly 19,000 square feet of ground-floor commercial space, served by two pools, a spa, a fitness center, a two-lane bowling alley, a golf simulator and pickleball courts.
Developer: The Ronto Group, NDT Development, Wheelock Street Capital

Title: Frida Kahlo Wynwood Residences
Description: The world's first Frida Kahlo-branded residential development in Miami's Wynwood, by PMG and LNDMRK, designed by Carlos Ott — two towers totaling 244 residences.
DescriptionLong: Frida Kahlo Wynwood Residences is the world's first residential development branded in tribute to the artist Frida Kahlo, rising in Miami's Wynwood Arts District. Developed by Property Markets Group (PMG) and LNDMRK Development, with design by Uruguayan architect Carlos Ott — designer of the Opéra Bastille — alongside Cube 3, the project pairs a 14-story tower with an eight-story companion building for a total of 244 residences. Color, craft and Kahlo's legacy run throughout, anchored by a large-scale mural, with creative-office and ground-floor commercial space woven into a walkable, art-immersed Wynwood setting.

Title: Delano Residences Miami
Description: The first-ever residential tower from the legendary Delano brand and marking PMG’s second supertall to rise over the city.
DescriptionLong:
A landmark 90-story supertall and the first-ever residential tower from the legendary Delano brand, marking PMG's second supertall to rise over Downtown Miami. The 421-residence project redefines urban luxury through a "vertical resort" model that integrates hotel-grade hospitality across two products: the Delano Collection for flexible short-term stays and Delano Residences for long-term living. 

The tower is anchored by South Florida's first public observation deck — featuring a cantilevered glass floor at 850 feet — alongside a sky pool perched at 800 feet and the legendary Rose Bar reimagined for a new skyline era. Residents tap into 24-hour concierge and valet, The Source by Delano wellness sanctuary, and private access to beach services at the original Delano Miami Beach.

StatusHistory.note: 90-story supertall, 421 residences per Robb Report / PROFILEmiami (sales launched 2026)

Title: BIBA
Description: A transformation of a historic property into a private, members-driven social and hospitality destination.
DescriptionLong: BIBA is a members-only boutique social club and hotel in West Palm Beach, occupying the restored 1941 Hotel Biba (the former Mount Vernon Motor Lodge) on Belvedere Road, a National Register of Historic Places property. Conceived by Elizabeth Elghanayan as a French Riviera-inspired retreat, it pairs 26 hotel rooms and suites with a swimming pool, a cocktail bar, a poolside lounge, and a rooftop cigar lounge. Its dining centerpiece is MayaBay, the Monaco-born Thai-Japanese restaurant making its U.S. debut at the club. The nearly one-acre compound blends modernist architecture with members-only culture and hospitality.

Developed by Kolter Urban and Perko Development Partners and designed by global firm 10 Design with interiors by Hirsch Bedner Associates and landscapes by EDSA, its Regency- and Mediterranean-inspired homes range from two to four bedrooms up to a roughly 10,000-square-foot penthouse, served by a spa, club lounge, private dining room, theater, salon and guest suites.

Title: Singer Island Gateway
Description: Continuum has proposed a 28-story, 298-unit luxury condominium along the Intracoastal near the Blue Heron Bridge, featuring public open space, a marina, and resort-style amenities.
DescriptionLong: Singer Island Gateway is a proposed luxury waterfront high-rise on a roughly three-acre Intracoastal assemblage at the eastern foot of the Blue Heron Boulevard bridge on Singer Island in Riviera Beach, Florida. Developed by The Continuum Company and designed by Arquitectonica, the project envisions a tower of condominium and townhouse residences above a waterfront restaurant and retail, a marina, and roughly 25,000 square feet of public open space, with amenities including a fitness center, lap pool, and padel and pickleball courts. As planned, it would create a landmark gateway between the mainland and Singer Island's barrier-island beaches.

Title: Pier Sixty-Six
Description: A 32-acre Fort Lauderdale waterfront destination from Tavistock Development — anchored by a reopened 325-room resort, 88 private residences, a world-class marina, and a curated dining and retail collection — with a proposed Phase II expansion that would add four 23-story towers and 339 new residences.
DescriptionLong:
Pier Sixty-Six is a 32-acre waterfront mixed-use destination in Fort Lauderdale at 2301 SE 17th Street, anchored at the heart of the city's intracoastal corridor. The historic property's redevelopment concluded in January 2025 with the reopening of a 325-room resort, 88 private residences, a fully reimagined marina, and an integrated district of restaurants, retail, and public waterfront amenities. The Phase I redevelopment — designed by Arquitectonica and developed by Tavistock Development Company — restored the landmark observation tower at the project's center and introduced the resort's dining program, members-only spaces, and resort-level amenities across the campus.

In May 2026, Tavistock unveiled Phase II of the development: a proposed eight-acre mixed-use expansion that would add four 23-story towers rising 270 feet, distributing 339 additional residences across the four buildings. Building A, on a 1.4-acre north parcel, would deliver 101 residences; Buildings B, C, and D, on a 6.6-acre south parcel, would deliver 90, 75, and 73 residences respectively. The Phase II program adds ground-floor retail and restaurant space, second-level office space across all four towers, structured parking, and expanded marina facilities, alongside an approximately one-acre new water basin on the south side of the property that would extend marina access while increasing publicly accessible waterfront frontage.

The Phase II proposal also calls for additional grocery, retail, and dining space, mobility improvements, future public art installations throughout the property, and a new community gathering space beneath the 17th Street Causeway Bridge — planned in partnership with the City of Fort Lauderdale and the Florida Department of Transportation — that would activate an underutilized area beneath the bridge as a new public amenity linking both sides of the waterfront development.
ProjectType: Residences, Entertainment, Park, Retail, Hotel, Marina

DescriptionLong: Waldorf Astoria Residences Sarasota is the city's first Waldorf Astoria-branded residential address, an 18-story tower of 86 homes at Main Street and Pineapple Avenue in downtown Sarasota, developed by Jebcore Companies and WMG Development with Hilton. Designed by ODP Architecture & Design with interiors by Workshop/APD and landscape by DWY, the one- to five-bedroom residences feature 14-foot ceilings, floor-to-ceiling glass and curved private terraces. Steps from Sarasota Bay and the city's largest marina, the tower brings Waldorf Astoria's signature service-driven lifestyle to Florida's Gulf Coast.

Title: The George
Description: A 13-residence ultra luxury boutique project marking the culmination of Biltmore Square, MG Developer’s master-planned project under the lush canopy of Coral Gables.
DescriptionLong: The George is an ultra-luxury townhome enclave in Coral Gables, Florida, conceived as the final residential phase of the landmark Biltmore Square community. Developed by MG Developer and designed by De la Guardia Victoria Architects & Urbanists, The George comprises 13 Mediterranean-inspired residences, each spanning more than 5,000 square feet, blending timeless Coral Gables architecture with contemporary luxury finishes. Set within one of South Florida's most prestigious neighborhoods near the historic Biltmore Hotel, the boutique low-density collection offers bespoke craftsmanship and refined privacy, positioning The George as a rare new-construction opportunity in the heart of the City Beautiful.

Description: A six-story, ~126,000 sf Class A+ waterfront office building in Bay Harbor Islands by Taubco and Landau Properties, with private yacht dockage and dock-to-desk access.
DescriptionLong: One Kane is a boutique Class A+ waterfront office building in Bay Harbor Islands, Florida, at 9551 East Bay Harbor Drive, steps from Bal Harbour Shops. Developed by Taubco and Landau Properties, the six-story, roughly 126,000-square-foot trophy office is the area's first and only purpose-built office address, conceived for a yacht-owning clientele with eight private dock slips, boat valet and dock-to-desk access. Private elevator lobbies, concealed parking and a ground-floor and rooftop restaurant by BondSt round out a waterfront workplace designed to feel like a private club on Biscayne Bay.

Title: Live Nation Gasworx
Description: KETTLER has partnered with Live Nation to deliver a major new live music venue in the heart of Ybor City as part of the rapidly evolving Gasworx District
DescriptionLong: Live Nation Gasworx is a 4,300-capacity live-music venue planned in the Gasworx district adjacent to Ybor City in Tampa, Florida, operated by Live Nation. Sited near the historic Seventh Avenue entertainment corridor, the Centro Ybor garage and a future TECO streetcar stop, the mid-size, theater-style hall draws on classic music-hall design — a vintage exposed-bulb marquee, a brick facade, a second-level balcony and a proscenium-framed stage. Developed within the Gasworx joint venture of KETTLER, Ybor City developer Darryl Shaw and PPF Real Estate, the venue anchors a new chapter of nightlife and culture in one of Tampa's most storied districts.

StatusHistory.note: Live Nation 4,300-cap venue (KETTLER/Gasworx); opening slated late 2028 (was 2027); groundbreaking pending oak-tree variance

Title: CORA Merrick Park
Description: A 13-story, 74-residence wellness-focused luxury condominium in Coral Gables by Constellation Group and The Boschetti Group, designed by Arquitectonica, pursuing WELL certification.
DescriptionLong: CORA Merrick Park is a wellness-focused luxury condominium in Coral Gables, Florida, at 4241 Aurora Street near Shops at Merrick Park. Developed by Constellation Group and The Boschetti Group, with architecture by Arquitectonica and interiors by Urban Robot, the 13-story tower holds 74 residences and is positioned as Coral Gables' first residences designed around health and longevity, pursuing WELL residential certification. Amenities center on biohacking and recovery — advanced air and water purification, a fitness and recovery zone, a spa with infrared sauna, steam, contrast hydrotherapy and red-light therapy, plus a rooftop padel court, with ground-floor retail and office space rounding out the mixed-use base.

Title: Seventeen Gables
Description: A boutique collection of attainable luxury residences, and one of the tallest residential buildings in the East Gables market.
DescriptionLong: Seventeen Gables is a boutique eight-story, 117-residence luxury condominium rising on the Douglas Road corridor in Coral Gables, Florida, moments from downtown Coral Gables and Miami's Brickell financial district. Developed by BAM Development and Ascendra Capital with architecture by Caymares Martin A&E Design and interiors by Asprea Studio, the building delivers a rare collection of attainably priced new-construction Coral Gables condominiums, with most residences positioned below the seven-figure mark. Residents enjoy a resort-style pool, fitness center, sauna, a co-working resident lounge, and ground-floor retail, pairing Mediterranean-inflected architecture with walkable, amenity-rich city living.

Title: Pagani Residences
Description: Pagani Residences is the world's first branded residential project from Italian hypercar maker Pagani, a 30-story, 70-unit waterfront condominium on Harbor Island in North Bay Village, Miami.
DescriptionLong: Pagani Residences is the world's first branded residential project from Italian hypercar maker Pagani, rising as a 30-story, 70-unit luxury waterfront condominium tower at 7940 West Drive on Harbor Island in North Bay Village, Miami. Developed by Riviera Horizons in collaboration with Pagani and the Pagani Arte design studio, with architecture by A++, the boutique tower offers only four all-corner residences per floor, each framing panoramic views of Biscayne Bay and the Atlantic. Interiors carry Pagani's signature blend of artisan craftsmanship and aerospace-grade detailing, complemented by expansive private terraces and double-height penthouses. Amenities include a rooftop pool with 360-degree views, a top-floor wellness and spa level, a private marina, a 240-foot waterfront boardwalk, and yacht- and beach-club access, with an automotive concierge program rounding out a lifestyle aimed squarely at collectors.

StatusHistory.note: World's first Pagani Residences launches in North Bay Village — 70 units across 28 stories, from $2.4M

Title: 123 Ocean
Description: An exclusive six-story oceanfront boutique residential collection with 27 thoughtfully designed 1–3 bedroom beach homes.
DescriptionLong: 123 Ocean is a boutique oceanfront condominium at 123 Ocean Avenue in Palm Beach Shores, on the northern tip of Singer Island, Florida. The six-story building holds just 27 beach-house-style residences with direct private beach access, designed by Arquitectonica with interiors by Asthetique and landscape by Naturalficial. Developed by Lionstone Development, the intimate enclave pairs one- to three-bedroom homes with a resort pool and sun deck, a wellness suite featuring an infrared sauna and ice bath, a fitness studio, a residents' lounge, a media room and a children's play space — a relaxed luxury retreat steps from the Atlantic on one of Palm Beach County's most coveted barrier islands.

Title: Mr. C Residences Boca Raton
Description: Boca Raton's first newly announced branded residential tower in more than a decade is a 12-story boutique tower that brings the Cipriani family’s hospitality-led Mr. C brand to one of South Florida’s most established luxury markets.
DescriptionLong: Mr. C Residences Boca Raton is a 12-story luxury condominium of 133 residences at 41 SE 4th Street in Downtown Boca Raton, the first new branded residential tower in the city in over a decade. Carrying the Cipriani family's Mr. C hospitality brand, the European-inspired tower is developed by Key International and Wexford Real Estate Investors, designed by Arquitectonica with interiors by 1508 London. Residents enjoy a hotel-caliber service model alongside a rooftop infinity pool with private cabanas, an open-air cinema, a full spa with hammam and infrared sauna, a Technogym fitness center, yoga studio, lap pool, and padel and pickleball courts, with the ground-floor Bellini Restaurant and a residents-only rooftop Mr. C Bar & Lounge rounding out the experience.
Developer: Key International, Wexford Real Estate Investors

Title: Continuum 12000 Sport & Wellness Residences
Description: Located on the waterfront in North Miami, and bringing 262 private residences including one- to three-bedroom homes, waterfront villas, and penthouses.
DescriptionLong: Continuum 12000 Sport & Wellness Residences is a waterfront luxury condominium at 12000 North Bayshore Drive in North Miami, the Continuum Company's wellness- and water-sports-themed evolution of its acclaimed Continuum brand. Rising on a 4.32-acre bayfront site overlooking Biscayne Bay, the 20-story tower offers 262 residences ranging from one- to three-bedroom layouts to penthouses and townhomes, designed by Kobi Karp Architecture with interiors by Paul Duesing Partners. Its signature Mermaid Club anchors a 20-slip private marina with floating pools, paddleboards and kayaks, complemented by a spa, fitness center, indoor pickleball, sports simulators and putting greens — positioning Continuum 12000 as North Miami's premier sport-and-wellness address on the bay.

Title: Kempinski Residences Miami Design District
Description: Rising across two towers, the collection includes 132 private residences, six townhomes and 17 guest suites reserved exclusively for residents
DescriptionLong: Kempinski Residences Miami Design District marks the storied European hospitality brand Kempinski's first branded residential project in the United States, rising at 3801 and 3883 Biscayne Boulevard at the gateway to Miami's Design District. Developed by DaGrosa Capital Development Partners with architecture by Arquitectonica and interiors by Rockwell Group, the development pairs two 20-story towers — 132 residences in all, just four homes per floor — connected by an elevated third-floor amenity bridge, alongside six townhomes and resident-only guest suites. The wellness-forward amenity program spans a spa and recovery suite, lap pool, cold plunge, infrared and steam saunas, an outdoor training terrace, a restaurant with terrace dining, a library with wine and game lounges, a screening room, and golf and Formula 1 simulators, with landscape design by Enea.

Title: HQ Residences Miami
Description: HQ Hotels & Residences introduces its first residential tower, 229 one- to three-bedroom residences.
DescriptionLong: HQ Residences Miami is a 35-story condominium tower in Miami's Edgewater neighborhood, the first residential project from Sam Nazarian's HQ Hotels & Residences by sbe, with artist Marc Anthony among its creative collaborators. Designed by Arquitectonica with interiors by DesignAgency and landscape by Enzo Enea, the tower delivers 229 fully serviced residences and penthouses. Its roughly 15,000-square-foot amenity program spans four pillars — wellness, entertainment, community and art — including a contrast-therapy circuit, hammam, sauna and plunge pools, an open-air fitness terrace, a chef's kitchen, a podcast studio, a library and 'Marc's,' a speakeasy curated by Marc Anthony — bringing hospitality-driven living to one of Miami's most dynamic bayfront-adjacent districts.

Title: Nobu Hotel Nashville
Description: Nobu is bringing its globally celebrated brand to Nashville with a new Nobu Hotel and Restaurant within East Bank's visionary Oracle campus development.
DescriptionLong: Nobu Hotel Nashville is the first Nashville property from Nobu Hospitality, the global luxury lifestyle brand co-founded by chef Nobu Matsuhisa and Robert De Niro, planned within Oracle's riverfront campus in the River North District on the East Bank of the Cumberland River. Designed by Foster + Partners, the 120-room hotel pairs Nobu's signature restaurant and lobby cafe with a spa, fitness center and an infinity-edge rooftop pool framing downtown Nashville skyline views. Anchored in one of the city's most ambitious new mixed-use districts, Nobu Hotel Nashville brings the brand's refined Japanese-influenced hospitality to Music City's fast-emerging east riverfront.

Title: Firefly
Description: Firefly is a gated luxury golf community set across roughly 700 rolling acres in Spring Hill, Tennessee, about 30 miles south of Nashville. The private club community centers on two courses by an acclaimed golf architect: an 18-hole championship course and a family-friendly nine-hole short course. Beyond golf, members enjoy a full-service clubhouse, fitness center, family and adult pools, a bowling alley, golf simulators, and a racquet club with indoor pickleball and outdoor tennis. With several hundred custom homesites woven through the landscape, Firefly offers an exclusive, amenity-rich lifestyle in the hills of Middle Tennessee.
DescriptionLong: A highly anticipated debut course from renowned architect Andrew Green within a master-planned community.

Title: East Bank
Description: A ~70-acre master-planned riverfront district on Nashville's East Bank by The Fallon Company — affordable and market-rate housing, childcare, retail and public space by Nissan Stadium.
DescriptionLong: East Bank is a roughly 70-acre master-planned riverfront district rising on the East Bank of the Cumberland River in Nashville, Tennessee, directly across from downtown and adjacent to the new Nissan Stadium. Led by master developer The Fallon Company under an agreement with Metro Nashville, the walkable district blends affordable and market-rate housing, childcare, retail, hospitality and public open space. Its first residential building, Eastpoint Flats — co-developed with Elmington Capital — brings 323 all-affordable apartments, an 8,000-square-foot childcare facility and ground-floor retail, anchoring a new neighborhood that reconnects Nashville's growing core to its riverfront.

Title: Rosewood Residences West Palm Beach
Description: Rosewood Hotels & Resorts is adding one more property to its portfolio in South Florida.
DescriptionLong: Rosewood Residences West Palm Beach is a 27-story luxury waterfront condominium planned at 2001 North Flagler Drive in West Palm Beach, a joint venture of the Related Group and BH Group carrying the ultra-luxury Rosewood brand. Designed by Arquitectonica, the tower offers 90 residences averaging roughly 3,400 square feet, paired with more than 13,000 square feet of indoor amenities and a fifth-floor pool deck overlooking the Intracoastal Waterway. Part of the same development partnership behind the nearby Ritz-Carlton Residences on North Flagler, the project brings Rosewood's renowned service and residential design to one of West Palm Beach's most prestigious waterfront corridors.

Title: Currie Park Towers
Description: A two-tower luxury waterfront condominium across from Currie Park on West Palm Beach's northern Flagler Drive, designed by Pritzker Prize-winning architects Herzog & de Meuron.
DescriptionLong: Currie Park Towers is a landmark two-tower luxury condominium planned across from Currie Park at 2085 North Flagler Drive on West Palm Beach, Florida's emerging northern waterfront. Designed by Pritzker Prize-winning architects Herzog & de Meuron with Spina O'Rourke + Partners and landscaping by Enea Garden Design, the development comprises roughly 281 residences set within two approximately 31-story towers over a shared podium and underground parking. The sculptural waterfront towers emphasize walkability, public activation, and sweeping Intracoastal views, advancing a new chapter for West Palm Beach's northern downtown as one of South Florida's most ambitious luxury residential frontiers.

Title: Westgate Village (Palm Beach Kennel Club Redevelopment)
Description: A 42-acre redevelopment of The Palm Beach Kennel Club by Terra and Palm Beach-based Frisbie Group.
DescriptionLong: Westgate Village is a phased mixed-use redevelopment of the former Palm Beach Kennel Club, the historic greyhound track on roughly 42 to 47 acres at 1111 North Congress Avenue in the Westgate area of unincorporated Palm Beach County. A joint venture of Terra and the Frisbie Group, the walkable neighborhood is planned for more than a thousand residences — studio-to-three-bedroom apartments, including a workforce-housing component — alongside roughly 120,000 square feet of commercial, retail and dining space woven through a network of streets, plazas and open space. The redevelopment reimagines one of the county's best-known landmarks as a contemporary live-work-play district anchoring the fast-growing Congress Avenue corridor near West Palm Beach.

Title: Saks Fifth Redevelopment
Description: The redevelopment of the former Saks Fifth Avenue at The Esplanade on Worth Avenue in Palm Beach into a high-end retail, office and lifestyle destination.
DescriptionLong: The Saks Fifth Redevelopment reimagines the long-vacant, two-level former Saks Fifth Avenue anchor at The Esplanade on Worth Avenue in Palm Beach, owned and redeveloped by O'Connor Capital Partners with architecture by Fairfax & Sammons. The roughly 50,000-square-foot transformation introduces luxury retail, office and lifestyle space behind a refreshed, classically detailed facade in keeping with Worth Avenue's storied shopping district. Leasing is led by Odyssey Retail as the project re-establishes one of Palm Beach's premier luxury addresses.

Title: The Modern
Description: An eight-story Class AA office tower, with a rooftop tenant, a fitness center, conference facilities, ground-level retail and dining.
DescriptionLong: The Modern is an eight-story, Class AA office tower of roughly 220,000 square feet planned at 11200 RCA Center Drive in Palm Beach Gardens, developed by Gatsby Florida with Hedrick Brothers Construction as general contractor. Designed for financial, legal and Fortune 500 tenants drawn to Palm Beach County's booming business corridor, the building offers efficient 25,000-square-foot floor plates, a six-level parking garage with electric-vehicle charging, a rooftop tenant terrace and a roughly 10,000-square-foot fine-dining venue at its base. The Modern positions itself as a premier next-generation workplace in one of South Florida's most active office submarkets.

Title: The Nora Hotel
Description: A 201-room boutique hotel that will anchor the Nora District, featuring a rooftop pool, spa, fitness center, and multiple dining venues, including a Pastis by STARR Restaurants.
DescriptionLong: The Nora Hotel is a 201-key boutique hotel in the emerging Nora District of West Palm Beach, the first ground-up development outside New York for Richard Born's BD Hotels, in collaboration with hotelier Sean MacPherson, NDT Development, Place Projects, and Wheelock Street Capital. Designed by Stantec with interiors by Gachot Studios, it draws on Addison Mizner's Mediterranean and Spanish-colonial-revival language with arched loggias, sunlit courtyards, and layered tilework. Amenities include a rooftop pool and bar with Intracoastal and downtown views and a dedicated wellness spa, while dining is led by STARR Restaurants, anchored by a Parisian-style Pastis brasserie on the ground floor.
Developer: BD Hotels, Wheelock Street Capital, NDT Development, Place Projects

Title: The Estates at NoMar
Description: A limited collection of 100 contemporary single-family homes at the forefront of West Palm Beach’s rapidly transforming northern waterfront
DescriptionLong: The Estates at NoMar is a gated enclave of 100 contemporary luxury single-family homes in West Palm Beach's Northwood Marina Quarter, developed by GL Homes within the broader NoMar district master-planned by Huizenga Holdings. Set just west of Flagler Drive near the waterfront, the homes range from roughly 1,800 to 3,900 square feet with open-concept plans, soaring ceilings, flexible office and guest spaces, and upscale modern finishes. Residents sit minutes from Palm Beach Island, Northwood Village, downtown West Palm Beach, CityPlace, and the Nora District, while the wider NoMar vision adds future waterfront condominiums, walkable retail and dining, a community park, and a mega-yacht marina.

Title: Tampa Bay Rays Stadium
Description: The Rays’ vision for the proposed project, specifically the ballpark they view as their “forever home” and their hope to open in time for the 2029 season.
DescriptionLong: The Tampa Bay Rays' proposed new ballpark is a roughly $2.3 billion, approximately 31,000-seat domed stadium planned for the Hillsborough Community College Dale Mabry campus in Tampa, replacing the collapsed St. Petersburg Historic Gas Plant plan. Conceived as the centerpiece of a redeveloped campus district, the fixed-roof, climate-controlled ballpark is organized around four zones — the Champions Quarter housing the stadium itself, the Invention Edge rebuilding the college campus, The Row as a central pedestrian street, and The Canopy as new public parkland. Funded through a Rays-and-public partnership with state support for the college rebuild, the project is envisioned as a permanent, weather-protected home for Major League Baseball on Florida's Gulf Coast.

StatusHistory.note: Hillsborough County (5-2) and Tampa City Council (4-3) approve stadium MOU; Rays target a March 2029 opening

Title: Roche Bobois St. Pete Tower
Description: The first Roche Bobois-branded residential building in the U.S., bringing French luxury design to downtown St. Petersburg.
DescriptionLong: Roche Bobois St. Pete Tower is a 29-story, roughly 375-foot luxury condominium at 344 4th Street South in downtown St. Petersburg, Florida — the first designer-branded residential tower in the United States from the celebrated French furniture house Roche Bobois. Developed by Valor Real Estate Development and designed by Gómez Vázquez International with Niz + Chauvet Arquitectos, the tower places residences on floors five through twenty-nine, from studio lofts to penthouses, above a ground-floor restaurant and retail space. Amenities exceed 20,000 square feet, and the project contributes a 5,000-square-foot public arts plaza to the streetscape, bringing branded European design pedigree to one of Florida's fastest-rising downtown waterfront cities.

StatusHistory.note: 29-story, ~164 condos at 344 4th St S; construction expected 2026, completion 2028 per St Pete Rising

Title: Candyroot Lodge
Description: A 1,200-acre resort bringing four 18-hole courses, a par-3, on-site lodging, hot and cold therapy wellness facilities, fitness spaces, and an integrated trail network.
DescriptionLong: Candyroot Lodge is a wellness-focused public golf retreat taking shape across roughly 1,210 acres in the Carolina Sandhills of South Carolina, an emerging destination set between Charlotte and Columbia. Developed by brothers Aaron and Ethan Oberman with master planning by Hart Howerton, Candyroot is anchored by an 18-hole championship course — the solo design debut of architect Mike Koprowski — with plans for as many as four walkable championship courses plus a lighted par-3, all conceived as a rare daily-fee, walking-only experience open to the public. The retreat blends the rolling, sandy terrain and native vegetation of the Sandhills with a restorative, wellness-driven approach to golf and hospitality, positioning Candyroot Lodge as one of the most anticipated new public golf destinations in the American Southeast.

StatusHistory.note: Preview play Nov 2026, grand opening Spring 2027 (stored 2026) per Post and Courier

Title: NOKOSI Golf Club
Neighborhood: Former Sherbrooke Golf & Country Club site
Description: A new Nicklaus Family-owned private destination centered around an 18-hole course, wellness, longevity, and learning — blending Old Florida-inspired, Spina O'Rourke + Partners-designed architecture.
DescriptionLong: NOKOSI is a strictly private, invitation-only Nicklaus Family Club rising on the 153-acre site of the long-shuttered Sherbrooke Golf & Country Club in Palm Beach County, Florida, roughly twenty minutes from Palm Beach International Airport. Designed by Jack Nicklaus and his son Gary through Nicklaus Design and developed by the Nicklaus family with TWG Global, the club centers on a completely rebuilt, walking-friendly championship golf course emphasizing strategic shot values. Beyond golf, NOKOSI is planned around a chef-driven clubhouse and a Health, Wellness and Longevity Center offering physician-led diagnostics, preventative screening, and regenerative therapies.

Title: Old Shores
Description: The team behind Bandon Dunes, Sand Valley, Rodeo Dunes, and Wild Spring Dunes are bringing two championship 18-hole courses, a 9-hole course, a par-3, and a walkable village.
DescriptionLong: Old Shores is an ambitious destination golf resort rising in the Florida Panhandle, set on a dramatic sand-based site roughly 30 minutes inland from Panama City Beach with as much as 40 feet of natural elevation change, deep sinkholes and a 50-acre lake. Developed by Michael Keiser — the visionary behind Bandon Dunes and the Dream Golf portfolio — Old Shores marks another collaboration with celebrated golf architect Tom Doak, whose first course at the resort is joined by plans for a second 18-hole layout and a short course. Beyond the golf, the development is envisioned as a complete retreat with cottage and estate homesites, a hotel and restaurants. With its rare inland-Panhandle geology and a marquee design pedigree, Old Shores is positioned to become one of Florida's premier walking-golf destinations.

Title: Coral Mountain Desert Club
Description: A 400-acre private club community bringing an 18-hole David McLay Kidd-designed course and the largest pneumatic surf lagoon in the United States.
DescriptionLong: Coral Mountain Desert Club is a 400-acre private residential and recreation club in La Quinta, California, in the greater Palm Springs area, developed by Meriwether Companies as an experience-driven desert retreat. The club is anchored by an 18-hole championship golf course — the first Coachella Valley desert design from acclaimed architect David McLay Kidd — alongside a 48-chamber pneumatic surf lagoon billed as the largest wave basin of its kind in the United States. A wellness- and sport-focused amenity program rounds out the community with padel, pickleball and tennis courts, a clubhouse, pools, a pump track, a recreational lake and trails connecting to tens of thousands of acres of adjacent public land. Set against the striking backdrop of Coral Mountain, the club fuses championship golf, world-class surf and fitness into a distinctive Southern California desert lifestyle destination.

StatusHistory.note: Broke ground Apr 9 2026; first amenities/golf/wave basin complete 2028 (stored 2026) per Meriwether/PRNewswire
Coral Mountain Desert Club is a 400-acre club in La Quinta (per Meriwether groundbreaking release)

Title: Marcella Club
Description: Reef Capital Partners are bringing Tiger Wood's first-ever mountain golf course to the new Marcella Club in Utah.
DescriptionLong: Marcella Club is a private 36-hole golf and ski-village community in the Deer Valley East Village / Jordanelle Ridge area of Park City, Utah, developed by Reef Capital Partners. Its centerpiece is “Skyline,” an 18-hole mountain course — the first by Tiger Woods' TGR Design — complemented by a second 18-hole “Ambush” course from King Collins Dormer, with a clubhouse by Olson Kundig. With 500 founding memberships, the club blends championship mountain golf, ski-village access and luxury real estate against the backdrop of Utah's Wasatch Range.

StatusHistory.note: Members-only soft opening of the Tiger Woods/TGR Design course set for summer 2026; full grand opening in 2027

Title: Gallery at Marina Village
Description: Gallery at Marina Village is a market-rate apartment development rising along the Broadway corridor in Riviera Beach's emerging Marina Village waterfront district. Developed by Related Urban Development Group with partners BH Group and Tezral Partners, its twin 20-story towers pair studio-to-three-bedroom residences with ground-floor retail and office space and a multi-level parking structure, plus resident amenities including a fitness center, coworking lounge and pool deck. Set steps from the Riviera Beach marina and Peanut Island, it anchors a broader transformation of the city's downtown waterfront.
DescriptionLong: A market-rate rental complex with two, 20-story residential towers and a nine-level parking structure with 652 parking spaces with retail and office space.

Title: HB Capital Group Tower
Description: A 37-story, 425-foot mixed-use tower proposed across from Lake Eola Park in downtown Orlando, pairing 252 condominiums with a 221-room hotel and ground-floor retail and dining.
DescriptionLong: HB Capital Group Tower is a proposed 37-story, roughly 425-foot mixed-use high-rise on a prominent corner across from Lake Eola Park in downtown Orlando, developed by HB Capital Group and designed by Kobi Karp Architecture & Interior Design. The tower would combine up to 252 condominium residences with a 221-room hotel, about 35,000 square feet of commercial space and a park-facing ground-floor restaurant, served by a multi-level parking podium and a hotel pool deck overlooking the lake. Sitting on one of the last marquee Lake Eola-fronting sites, the project would bring a new luxury residential-and-hospitality landmark to Orlando's urban core.

Title: District at Northwood
Description: A mixed-use luxury rental community in Northwood Village, West Palm Beach by Immocorp Ventures, designed by Kobi Karp — 382 residences across three buildings with Class A retail.
DescriptionLong: District at Northwood is a mixed-use luxury rental community in the Northwood Village neighborhood of West Palm Beach, Florida, at 2501 Pinewood Avenue on a former CRA 'Anchor Site.' Developed by Immocorp Ventures — a joint venture of Immocorp Capital and Beachwold Residential — and designed by Kobi Karp, the project spans three buildings, led by a nine-story tower, with 382 residences including 38 workforce units. The community pairs roughly 60,000 square feet of Class A retail and a grocery anchor with a rooftop pool and spa, fitness center and structured parking, catalyzing the revitalization of historic Northwood Village.

Title: Tennessee Performing Arts Center
Description: A new 307,000-square-foot Tennessee Performing Arts Center campus on Nashville's East Bank, designed by Bjarke Ingels Group with four performance venues under one sculptural roof.
DescriptionLong: The Tennessee Performing Arts Center is a new 307,000-square-foot cultural campus planned for Nashville's East Bank along the Cumberland River, designed by Bjarke Ingels Group (BIG) with William Rawn Associates and Hastings Architecture. The building gathers four distinct performance venues — a grand Broadway hall, a dedicated dance and opera theater, a flexible black-box space and an intimate cabaret — beneath a reflective facade of aluminum tubes whose arches lift like a theater curtain to reveal the activity within. Conceived as the cultural anchor of Nashville's East Bank redevelopment near Nissan Stadium, the riverfront campus replaces TPAC's longtime downtown home and is funded through a public-private partnership.

Title: Jacksonville Jaguars Stadium
Description: A $1.4 billion transformation of EverBank Stadium, delivering a near-complete reinvention of the Jaguars’ home into a next-generation venue set to debut for the 2028 NFL season.
DescriptionLong: The Jacksonville Jaguars' "Stadium of the Future" is a comprehensive, more-than-$1.4-billion reinvention of the team's downtown Jacksonville home on the St. Johns River, delivered as a public-private partnership between the City of Jacksonville and the Jaguars. Designed by global sports architect HOK and built by a Hunt Construction–Barton Malow joint venture, the project reconstructs the open-air bowl beneath a signature shade canopy and a dramatic steel-truss roof structure engineered to cool the seating areas in Florida's climate. The phased renovation reshapes concourses, premium clubs and fan amenities while the team continues to play on site, transforming the aging venue into a modern, year-round entertainment destination capable of hosting marquee events. The Stadium of the Future is the centerpiece of a broader effort to anchor sports, tourism and development in downtown Jacksonville.

Title: Panther National
Description: Palm Beach County’s first new golf community in nearly two decades with a Nicklaus-Thomas course.
DescriptionLong:
A 400-acre private golf and luxury residential community, designed around an 18-hole championship golf course created by golf legends Jack Nicklaus and Justin Thomas. Located within the Avenir master-planned community and bordering more than 2,400 acres of protected conservation land, Panther National combines contemporary architecture, expansive homesites, and a club-focused lifestyle in one of South Florida’s fastest-growing luxury markets.

The community features approximately 218 custom and signature estate homes, many designed by acclaimed architect Max Strang, with modern architecture that stands apart from traditional South Florida golf communities. Amenities include a private golf club, lifestyle club, racquet facilities, wellness offerings, resort-style pools, and a new clubhouse currently under construction that is expected to become a centerpiece of the development.

Title: The Ritz-Carlton Residences Houston
Description: The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Houston is a luxury tower at 2120 Post Oak Boulevard in Houston's Uptown-Galleria district, the brand's first co-located hotel-and-residential property in the city.
DescriptionLong: The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Houston is a luxury tower rising at 2120 Post Oak Boulevard in Houston's Uptown-Galleria district, steps from the River Oaks District shops and The Galleria. Developed by Deiso Moss with Cleary Interests in collaboration with Marriott International, it is the Ritz-Carlton brand's first co-located hotel-and-residential property in Houston, pairing a 154-key hotel with 114 private branded residences. The tower is designed by global architecture firm Pickard Chilton with Houston-based Ziegler Cooper Architects, interiors by Rottet Studio, and construction by Harvey Cleary. Residences begin at roughly 2,200 square feet and are served by about 27,000 square feet of owner-exclusive amenities plus access to the hotel's facilities, a private motor court, dedicated lobby and elevators, and panoramic Uptown views.

Title: One Twenty Brickell Signature Residences
Description: A 40-story, 467-residence condominium in Miami's Brickell by PMG, designed by Sieger Suarez — each home paired with a deeded private office suite.
DescriptionLong: One Twenty Brickell Signature Residences is a 40-story, 467-residence condominium in Miami's Brickell district, at 120 SW 8th Street. Developed by PMG and designed by Sieger Suarez Architects, the tower introduces a work-from-home concept in which every fully furnished residence is paired with a deeded private office suite. A roughly 50,000-square-foot amenity program anchored by a Signature Owners' Club serves studios through three-bedroom homes, with general contractor Juneau Construction building the tower in one of the densest, most connected urban cores in the United States.

StatusHistory.note: 40-story PMG tower, 467 residences w/ deeded office suites; completion early 2028 per The Real Deal (Apr 2026)

Title: Marina Village
Description: A multi-phase, 12-acre mixed-use waterfront redevelopment aimed at transforming the Broadway corridor into a premier tourist destination and residential hub.
DescriptionLong: Marina Village is an eight-story, 149-unit mixed-income residential development in Riviera Beach's waterfront marina district, across from Peanut Island. Developed by Related Urban with Tezral Partners as a centerpiece of the Riviera Beach CRA's waterfront redevelopment, the Arquitectonica-designed building offers one- and two-bedroom apartments at a range of income levels, ground-floor retail and structured parking, helping anchor a revitalized public waterfront on Florida's Treasure Coast.
ProjectType: Residences, Hotel, Park, Marina, Entertainment, Retail
Developer: City of Riviera Beach, Developer Partner

Title: King Salman Park
Description: King Salman Park is the centerpiece of Riyadh's Vision 2030 transformation — a roughly 16-square-kilometer green district billed as the largest urban park in the world.
DescriptionLong: King Salman Park is the centerpiece of Riyadh's Vision 2030 transformation: a roughly sixteen-square-kilometer green district rising on the site of the former Riyadh Air Base, billed as the largest urban park in the world and dwarfing London's Hyde Park and New York's Central Park. Master-planned by Danish firm Henning Larsen with Saudi lead consultant Omrania and delivered by the King Salman Park Foundation, it is one of four flagship quality-of-life megaprojects reshaping the Saudi capital. The park weaves together cultural anchors including a Royal Arts Complex by Ricardo Bofill Taller de Arquitectura and a visitor pavilion by David Adjaye, alongside gardens, a wadi cascade, museums, residential and hospitality districts, and tens of kilometers of cycling and walking trails. Conceived as the green lung of Riyadh, it aims to set a new global benchmark for sustainable, art-infused urban placemaking.

Title: Dubai Creek Tower
Description: A needle-like observation tower planned to surpass the Burj Khalifa, rising from Dubai Creek Harbour with a cantilevered observation deck inspired by a lily pad.
DescriptionLong: Dubai Creek Tower is a landmark observation tower planned as the centerpiece of Emaar Properties' Dubai Creek Harbour, an expansive waterfront master-development set around the historic Dubai Creek and the Ras Al Khor wildlife sanctuary. Conceived as a soaring, cable-stayed structure — originally designed by Spanish-Swiss architect Santiago Calatrava and inspired by the slender form of a lily and the minarets of Islamic architecture — the tower is intended to anchor a new district of residences, retail and public realm, crowned by observation decks offering panoramic views across the city. As one of Emaar's signature undertakings, Dubai Creek Tower is envisioned as a defining vertical icon of Dubai's skyline and a global tourism draw.

StatusHistory.note: Recorded "construction" but vertical never started — foundation (145 barrette piles) complete 2018 then stalled; Emaar relaunching with NEW design, construction tender to be issued ~2026, vertical begins once contractor appointed. Proposing correction to breaking-ground

Title: The Avenues
Description: The Avenues – Riyadh is a landmark mixed-use retail and lifestyle destination in the heart of Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.
DescriptionLong: The Avenues – Riyadh is a landmark mixed-use retail and lifestyle destination rising in the heart of Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. Developed by Shomoul Holding Company — a joint venture of Kuwait's Mabanee, the Al Fozan Group, and the Alshaya Group — and designed by regional architecture and engineering firm Pace, the project spans roughly 390,000 square meters and centers on a vast enclosed mall of approximately 370,000 square meters of leasable space. The design draws on Salmani architecture native to the Najd region, anchoring five towers that house hotels, residences, and offices alongside the retail core. Conceived as one of the Middle East's premier shopping and entertainment hubs, it extends Mabanee's flagship Avenues brand from Kuwait into the Saudi capital.

StatusHistory.note: On track to open in 2026 (most recent reports point to late 2026); the $4.58B mega-mall is past 44.5% complete with 13,000 workers on three shifts.

Title: Hudson Yards West
Description: The ~$2B second phase of Hudson Yards over the Western Rail Yards — ~4,000 homes, 2.4M sf of office, a hotel, school and 6.6-acre park — by Related Companies and Oxford Properties.
DescriptionLong: Hudson Yards West is the second phase of Manhattan's Hudson Yards, a roughly $2 billion mixed-use megaproject rising on a platform over the Western Rail Yards along the High Line on the Far West Side. Developed by Related Companies and Oxford Properties with master design led by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill, the plan calls for around 4,000 homes — a large share of them affordable — across multiple towers, plus 2.4 million square feet of office space, a hotel, a public school and a 6.6-acre park. As the western completion of the largest private real-estate development in U.S. history, it is conceived to extend Hudson Yards' skyline of glass towers and elevated public space toward the Hudson River.

Title: Penn Station Redevelopment
Description: A federally led transformation of New York's Penn Station into a modern, single-level transit hub with a soaring new train hall, expanded concourses and a new Eighth Avenue entrance.
DescriptionLong: The Penn Station Redevelopment is a federally led overhaul of New York's Penn Station — the busiest transit hub in the Western Hemisphere — directed by the U.S. Department of Transportation with Amtrak and the Federal Railroad Administration. Master-developed by Penn Transformation Partners, a joint venture of Halmar International and Skanska, with design by Practice for Architecture and Urbanism (PAU), the plan replaces the cramped warren beneath Madison Square Garden with a single-level concourse, ceilings rising above 50 feet, stone and bronze finishes, expanded retail and a grand new Eighth Avenue entrance. Drawing on the Beaux-Arts and Art Deco grandeur of New York's great civic landmarks, the project aims to dramatically improve capacity and the rider experience for Amtrak, NJ Transit and the LIRR.

Title: Shenzhen Bay Super Headquarters Base
Description: The Shenzhen Bay Super Headquarters Base is a flagship central business district on reclaimed land in Shenzhen's Nanshan District, on the shore of Shenzhen Bay facing Hong Kong.
DescriptionLong: The Shenzhen Bay Super Headquarters Base is a flagship central business district rising on reclaimed land in Shenzhen's Nanshan District, on the shore of Shenzhen Bay facing Hong Kong. Spanning roughly 117 hectares with about 5.2 million square metres of planned floor area across nine development units, it is conceived as a dense cluster of corporate headquarters, cultural venues, a transport interchange, and coastal public space serving the Guangdong–Hong Kong–Macau Greater Bay Area. Its skyline centrepiece is Zaha Hadid Architects' Tower C, a pair of naturally lit supertall towers near 400 metres joined by sky terraces, alongside the China Merchants Bank Global Headquarters and other landmark office towers. Master-planned and delivered under municipal state-owned Shenzhen Investment Holdings, the district is designed to host hundreds of thousands of workers daily and form one of China's most concentrated groupings of supertall skyscrapers.

Title: Brent Cross Town
Description: Brent Cross Town is one of the UK's largest regeneration projects, transforming 180 acres of brownfield land in Brent Cross and Cricklewood into a new park town for north-west London.
DescriptionLong: Brent Cross Town is one of the UK's largest regeneration projects, transforming 180 acres of brownfield land in Brent Cross and Cricklewood, in the London Borough of Barnet, into a new park town for north-west London. Delivered by Related Argent — a joint venture between Related and Argent — in partnership with Barnet Council, the masterplan sets out up to 6,700 new homes across for-sale, build-to-rent, affordable, student, co-living, and retirement tenures around 50 acres of parks and playing fields. The mixed-use scheme also brings around 3 million square feet of offices and innovation space, new schools, indoor sports facilities, a new high street, and its own mainline railway station, Brent Cross West. Designed as a sustainable, walkable neighbourhood with a landscape-led masterplan, it is anchored by tenants including a new Sheffield Hallam University campus.

Title: Lincoln Yards
Description: Lincoln Yards is a major mixed-use redevelopment of a former industrial corridor along the Chicago River between Lincoln Park and Bucktown on Chicago's North Side.
DescriptionLong: Lincoln Yards is a major mixed-use redevelopment of a former industrial corridor along the Chicago River between Lincoln Park and Bucktown on Chicago's North Side. Originally envisioned by developer Sterling Bay as a multibillion-dollar campus of housing, offices, and public space, the project stalled amid financing and ownership turmoil. The northern 31-acre parcel has since passed to JDL Development and Kayne Anderson Real Estate, who are advancing a recalibrated, residential-led plan rebranded as Foundry Park, while the southern riverfront section moved to Novak Construction. The site remains one of the largest urban infill development opportunities in the United States.

StatusHistory.note: Original Lincoln Yards megaproject stalled/failed; site bought by JDL+Kayne Anderson, rebranded "Foundry Park," City Council approved Feb 18 2026, construction expected to begin Oct 2026, ~7yr buildout. Title: Shibuya Upper West Project
Description: The Shibuya Upper West Project is a landmark mixed-use redevelopment on the former Tokyu Department Store and Bunkamura site in Tokyo's Shibuya district.
DescriptionLong: The Shibuya Upper West Project is a landmark mixed-use redevelopment rising on the former Tokyu Department Store flagship and Bunkamura site in Tokyo's Shibuya district. Developed by Tokyu Corporation, L Catterton Real Estate, and Tokyu Department Store, the roughly 119,000-square-meter tower is the largest Japanese project to date by Norwegian architecture firm Snøhetta. Its ceramic-and-glass façade, broken by cascading green terraces, wraps retail at street level, a contemporary luxury hotel by Swire Hotels' House Collective in the middle floors, and rental residences above. Signature spaces include The Hive, a lively central atrium, and The Sanctuary, a rooftop garden, alongside the relocated and expanded Bunkamura Museum of Art, bringing Shibuya one of its first true luxury hotels.

Title: Madrid Nuevo Norte – BBVA Tower & Towers
Description: Madrid Nuevo Norte is one of Europe's largest urban regeneration projects, transforming railway land around Chamartín station in northern Madrid's Castellana Norte corridor.
DescriptionLong: Madrid Nuevo Norte is one of Europe's largest urban regeneration projects, transforming roughly 2.3 million square metres of former railway land around Chamartín station in northern Madrid's Castellana Norte corridor. Led by developer Crea Madrid Nuevo Norte — backed by BBVA, Merlin Properties, and Grupo San José — the masterplan stitches together four districts: Las Tablas Oeste, Malmea-San Roque-Tres Olivos, the Chamartín Business Center, and a remodelled Chamartín station. At its heart rises a new financial skyline crowned by Torre Madrid Nuevo Norte 1, a roughly 330-metre, 70-storey landmark set to become the tallest building in Spain and Western Europe. The mixed-use scheme also delivers around 10,500 homes, an expanded high-speed rail hub, new Metro connections, and a large central park, channeling decades of planning into a sustainability-driven, transit-oriented district.

Title: Litibú Bay Club
Description: Litibú Bay Club is a private, gated beachfront community on a 21-acre stretch of coast in Punta de Mita, in Mexico's Riviera Nayarit.
DescriptionLong: Litibú Bay Club is a private, gated beachfront community on a 21-acre stretch of coast in Punta de Mita, in Mexico's Riviera Nayarit. Developed by Contact Development Company with Mexico City-based GBM Real Estate and designed by architecture studio TALLER ADG, the community comprises 171 villas and estates set between Litibú Beach and the Greg Norman-designed Higuera Golf Club. Its collection spans ocean villas, golf villas, beach houses, and beachfront estates, with golf-villa ownership including membership to the adjacent championship course. Positioned as a new benchmark for coastal luxury north of Puerto Vallarta, the club pairs beach and golf living with resort-grade amenities in one of the Pacific coast's most exclusive enclaves.

Description: A five-story luxury watch-and-jewelry flagship and mixed-use building planned for 1117 East Las Olas Boulevard in downtown Fort Lauderdale, developed by Weston Jewelers and anchored by Rolex.
DescriptionLong: The Weston Jewelers Las Olas Flagship is a five-story, roughly 32,000-square-foot luxury retail and office building planned for 1117 East Las Olas Boulevard in downtown Fort Lauderdale, developed by the family-owned South Florida jeweler Weston Jewelers. Anchored by Rolex and joined by brands including Cartier, Omega, Breitling and Chopard, the building stacks at least three floors of curated watch and jewelry retail beneath a corporate office level and rooftop hospitality space. With a prominent Las Olas Boulevard address, it is positioned to become one of the largest multi-brand luxury watch and jewelry destinations in the United States.

Title: Marina Towers by Tatweer Misr
Description: A premium phase within the IL Monte Galala resort in Ain Sokhna featuring nine high-rise towers.
DescriptionLong: Marina Towers is the marina-district centerpiece of Tatweer Misr's Il Monte Galala resort, set on the Galala Mountain above Ain Sokhna on Egypt's Red Sea. Master-planned by Gianluca Peluffo & Partners, the roughly ten-tower waterfront complex combines thousands of residences with a Marriott Autograph Collection hotel, a 154-berth IGY-operated marina, a large convention center, and extensive retail and dining, creating a flagship Red Sea leisure and yachting destination within one of Egypt's premier mountain-and-sea resorts.

Title: Nobu Residences Miami
Description: A 75-story, 296-residence Nobu-branded waterfront tower at 619 Brickell in Miami, designed by Foster + Partners with a $25 million longevity-focused wellness program.
DescriptionLong: Nobu Residences Miami is a 75-story, roughly 860-foot branded condominium tower planned for 619 Brickell Avenue on the Biscayne Bay waterfront in Miami's Brickell district, developed by 13th Floor Investments and Key International. Designed by Foster + Partners with Sieger Suarez Architects as architect of record, the tower delivers 296 one- to five-bedroom residences, sky villas and penthouses ranging from about 1,160 to 5,500 square feet. Its 90,000-plus square feet of amenities center on a roughly $25 million Nobu Wellness and Longevity Spa, four pools including an 86-foot sunset pool, ground-floor and upper-level Nobu restaurants and padel courts — Miami's first Nobu-branded residential tower.

Title: The Power District
Description: The Power District is a roughly 100-acre mixed-use megaproject on Salt Lake City's west side, transforming the former Rocky Mountain Power industrial site along the Jordan River.
DescriptionLong: The Power District is a roughly 100-acre mixed-use megaproject rising on Salt Lake City's west side, transforming the former Rocky Mountain Power industrial site along the Jordan River between downtown and Salt Lake City International Airport. Developed by the Larry H. Miller Company around a master plan by Sasaki, the district is anchored by a new all-electric Rocky Mountain Power corporate headquarters designed by SOM with Architectural Nexus and built by Okland Construction, plus a restored riverfront led by James Corner Field Operations. At full buildout it is planned to deliver about 4,700 homes, roughly 1.3 million square feet of office, around 300 hotel keys, and some 320,000 square feet of retail, alongside parks, trails, and entertainment uses. The Power District also holds the shovel-ready site at the center of Utah's bid to land a Major League Baseball expansion franchise.
ProjectType: Mixed-Use, Residences, Office, Hotel, Retail, Entertainment, Park

StatusHistory.note: Vertical construction underway on the 10-story, 300,000 sq ft Rocky Mountain Power HQ, the first structure rising in the 100-acre district.

Title: Twenty Sixth & 2nd Wynwood Residences
Description: An eight-story, 233-residence live-work condominium in Miami's Wynwood by PMG and LNDMRK Development, designed by CUBE 3, with short-term-rental flexibility.
DescriptionLong: Twenty Sixth & 2nd Wynwood Residences is an eight-story, 233-residence condominium in Miami's Wynwood Arts District, at 201 NW 26th Street. Developed by PMG and LNDMRK Development, with architecture by CUBE 3 and interiors by BAMO, the project delivers furnished live-work residences with full short-term-rental flexibility, paired with about 26,000 square feet of retail and 32,000 square feet of amenities across a roughly 250,000-square-foot building. General contractor John Moriarty & Associates of Florida is building the project in the heart of one of Miami's most walkable, art-driven neighborhoods.

Title: The Setai, St. Maarten
Description: An ultra-luxury beachfront resort and branded residences in Indigo Bay, marking The Setai's Caribbean debut
DescriptionLong: The Setai St. Maarten is an ultra-luxury beachfront resort and collection of branded residences in Indigo Bay, developed by Altree Developments in partnership with The Setai Hotels (Nakash Family). Set along over 1,000 feet of pristine shoreline with views toward Saba and St. Barths, the property will feature a 205-key hotel plus 280 private residences ranging from approximately 620 to over 2,000 sq ft, with dual-key options up to 6,000 sq ft. Designed by HKS Architects with interiors by Studio Munge, amenities include a beach club with cabanas and butler service, three pools (including an adults-only oasis), a 30,000 sq ft destination spa, tennis and pickleball courts, five restaurant concepts, and a Les Clefs d'Or concierge team. It will be St. Maarten's first Leading Hotels of the World property. Construction is underway with completion expected in 2028.

StatusHistory.note: 205 keys plus branded residences/villas; opening 2028 (unchanged) per Luxury Travel Magazine

Description: A private, invitation-only sports and wellness campus, combining elite tennis and multi-sport training with mentorship-focused programming, featuring courts, wellness facilities, and creative spaces.
DescriptionLong: A private, invitation-only sports and wellness campus in Wellington led by Betsy McCormack and Rodrigo Nascimento, combining elite tennis and multi-sport training with mentorship-focused programming, featuring courts, wellness facilities, and creative spaces, with design by Spina O'Rourke + Partners.

Title: Arbor Coconut Grove
Description: A boutique 45-residence, 5-story building in the heart of Coconut Grove, steps from CocoWalk
DescriptionLong: Arbor Coconut Grove is a boutique 5-story, 45-residence building developed by Muse Groupe in the Village of Coconut Grove, Miami. Designed by Miami-based Behar Font & Partners, the low-density building offers spacious 2-, 3-, and 4-bedroom layouts with flexible den spaces, floor-to-ceiling impact-resistant windows, and nature-inspired modernist design. Located at 3034 Oak Avenue, the building is steps from CocoWalk, parks, marinas, top-rated schools, restaurants, and shops. Amenities include a pool, rooftop, lobby, pet-friendly community spaces, electric car charging stations, and ample resident parking. Move-in ready Q1 2026.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Coconut Grove (per project name + official site)

Title: DELUX WeHo
Description: A 134-unit boutique wellness-focused apartment community in Wedgewood-Houston with saltwater pool, red light saunas, cold plunge, and Technogym fitness center.
DescriptionLong: A 134-unit boutique apartment community in Nashville's Wedgewood-Houston neighborhood, developed by Tinsley Company, Big Plan Holdings, and The Bradley Projects. Following the same wellness-driven approach as the team's earlier project The Lanes, DELUX WeHo prioritizes natural light, livability, and intentional living over scale. Residences range from approximately 500 to 1,500 square feet across studio, one-, two-, and three-bedroom layouts, including loft-style floor plans that separate living and private spaces, with oversized windows and private balconies throughout. Wellness and recovery amenities include a saltwater pool, two red light saunas, a cold plunge, a dedicated recovery studio, and a Technogym fitness center. The property also features 5,000 square feet of street-level retail and sits within two blocks of Soho House Nashville, Michelin-starred Bastion, Hermès Nashville, and Pastis.
Developer: The Bradley Projects, Tinsley Co., Big Plan Holdings

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Wedgewood-Houston (WeHo), Nashville (per official site)
