Title: Highland Park Miami
Description: A $1 billion, 7-acre transit-oriented master plan from Black Salmon and The Allen Morris Company directly south of the Miami Health District, designed to add 2,000 residences, 250,000 square feet of medical office, a 150-key hotel, and a curated retail core.
DescriptionLong: Highland Park Miami is a transformative 7-acre transit-oriented master plan rising at 800 NW 14th Street, directly south of the Miami Health District — Miami-Dade's second-largest employment hub and one of the largest medical districts in the United States. The $1 billion development from Black Salmon (led by founder Camilo Lopez) and The Allen Morris Company will deliver more than 2,000 residential units across approximately 1.5 million square feet, 250,000 square feet of medical office, a 150-key hotel, and 75,000 square feet of curated retail anchored by health-focused tenants — fitness studios, juice bars, and healthy dining concepts — filling a category gap that has long defined the surrounding district. The project is anchored by more than an acre of public green space and a pedestrian promenade designed to link the medical district to greater Miami, with up to six buildings rising as tall as 22 stories across a multi-phase build planned over the coming decade. The master plan is by Arquitectonica under Design Principal Bernardo Fort-Brescia, with the residential component designed by Oppenheim Architecture and landscape architecture by Naturalficial. Site location is unusually well-positioned: just 400 feet from the Culmer Metrorail Station and immediately adjacent to State Road 836, the east-west transit artery linking downtown Miami, the international airport, and the western suburbs. Black Salmon assembled the 7-acre site over roughly a decade, acquiring approximately 50 properties across four city blocks for around $60 million before unveiling the master plan in September 2023; the project leverages Miami's transit-oriented development zoning to substantially increase density. Phase 1 calls for roughly 250 to 300 apartments and 250,000 square feet of medical office, with Phases 2 through 6 — the current Allen Morris portfolio scope — adding the remaining residential, hotel, retail, and public-realm components.
Developer: Black Salmon, The Allen Morris Company

StatusHistory.note: Highland Park Miami expands the Miami Health District (Civic Center).

Title: The Spruce
Description: An eight-story, 270-unit mixed-income mid-rise from Affiliated Development in the heart of Northwood Village, with 117 workforce and affordable units, a rooftop pool over the parking garage with Atlantic Ocean and downtown views, and ground-floor retail.
DescriptionLong: The Spruce is an eight-story, 270-unit mixed-income apartment building delivered by Affiliated Development at 2501 Spruce Avenue, occupying a 1.41-acre block between 24th and 25th Streets in the heart of West Palm Beach's Northwood Village. The mid-rise opens with one-, two-, and three-bedroom apartments, 117 of which are reserved as income-restricted housing: 109 workforce units priced for residents earning 80 to 140 percent of area median income, and 8 affordable units reserved for those at 60 percent AMI or below. The remaining 153 apartments lease at market rate. The building is anchored by a rooftop pool atop the parking garage with unobstructed views of the Atlantic Ocean and the downtown West Palm Beach skyline, alongside a resident dog park and 2,330 square feet of ground-floor retail intended to bring foot traffic to Northwood Village's existing dining, boutique, and nightlife corridor. Designed by A+R — the in-house architecture firm of West Palm Beach-based general contractor Verdex Construction — with renderings by Milwaukee-based multifamily specialist Rinka+, The Spruce emerged from a multi-year public-private collaboration with the West Palm Beach Community Redevelopment Agency and the City of West Palm Beach, both of which awarded the project funding incentives and tax-increment financing in spring 2023 to bridge the affordable-housing funding gap. The project broke ground in August 2024 and opened for leasing in early 2026. Affiliated Development, the Fort Lauderdale-based firm founded by Nick Rojo and Jeff Burns and also behind The Grand in West Palm Beach and The Pierce in Boynton Beach, designed The Spruce to deliver enough new daytime and evening residents to support Northwood Village's locally-owned businesses.

Title: The Pierce
Description: An 8-story, 300-unit mixed-income downtown Boynton Beach development from Affiliated Development, with 17,000 square feet of restaurant, retail, and office space, a 150-space public parking garage, and the relocated Hurricane Alley restaurant.
DescriptionLong: The Pierce is a $73 million mixed-use, mixed-income redevelopment rising on a 3.04-acre downtown Boynton Beach site at 115 North Federal Highway, between Boynton Beach Boulevard and Ocean Avenue, one block from the Intracoastal Waterway. The eight-story project will deliver 300 residential apartments — 50 percent rented at 80–120 percent of Area Median Income for 15 years, then 30 percent for a further 15 years, then 10 percent in perpetuity under the project's Tax Increment Revenue Financing Agreement (TIRFA) with the Boynton Beach Community Redevelopment Agency — alongside approximately 17,000 square feet of restaurant, retail, and office space and a public parking garage with 150 public parking spaces. The amenity program features a resort-style pool with private cabanas, a state-of-the-art fitness facility, pickleball courts, a co-working collective, and keyless Bluetooth door entry, with the entire complex built and certified to national green building standards. A defining feature of the project is the relocation of Hurricane Alley — the longtime Boynton Beach restaurant and bar founded by Kim Kelley — to a dedicated standalone building at the project's northwest corner, anchoring the dining program with one of the city's most established hospitality operators. Developed by Fort Lauderdale-based Affiliated Development, founded and led by Jeff Burns, the project emerged from a competitive 2021 RFP issued by the Boynton Beach CRA in which Affiliated outbid four other firms including Related Group, with site plan approval secured in March 2023 and a TIRFA amendment approved in June 2026. Demolition and utility relocation are underway with vertical construction slated for mid-2026.

StatusHistory.note: The Pierce sits at 115 N. Federal Highway in downtown Boynton Beach; demolition underway ahead of a summer 2026 vertical start.
Boynton Beach approves Affiliated Development's downtown project (originally 236 units, 118 workforce)
Downtown Boynton Beach (115 N. Federal Hwy): 300 apts (150 market-rate + 150 workforce), 8 stories, 17,015 sf retail (developer Affiliated Development).

Title: Tula Residences
Description: A 21-story, 54-residence Biscayne Bay condominium tower from Bayshore Grove Capital with architecture by M77R, interiors by Shannon Farrell Design, and a corner-only floor plate of just four residences per floor, plus six grand penthouses.
DescriptionLong: Tula Residences is a 21-story, 54-residence Biscayne Bay condominium tower rising at 7918 West Drive in North Bay Village — the three-island enclave between Miami Beach and the mainland — directly on the water with views spanning the Downtown Miami skyline and the Atlantic horizon. The tower's floor plate is unusually generous: corner-only residences with just four units per floor across the standard 7th through 19th floors, topped by an exclusive collection of six grand penthouses spanning the 20th through 22nd levels. Standard residences range from approximately 1,590 to 2,440 square feet across two-bedroom, two-bedroom-plus-den, and three-bedroom layouts; penthouses run roughly 4,880 to 5,370 square feet with five-bedroom-plus-den, five-and-a-half-bath layouts, expansive wraparound terraces, and unobstructed bay views. Every residence is delivered with 10-foot ceilings, oversize windows, decorative-glass terraces with iron railings, custom European flooring, Shannon Farrell Design kitchens with marble counters and a fully integrated Miele appliance suite, marble-clad bathrooms with fluted glass and champagne metal framing, walk-in Miele laundry rooms, and pre-wired fiber-optic connectivity. Amenities are anchored by a 6th-floor amenity level with a resort-style pool, landscaped sunrise/sunset deck and cabanas, a great room with a marble fireplace and floor-to-ceiling bay windows, a Skyline Club Room, a private dining room for twelve with a chef's warming kitchen, a 2,100-square-foot fitness center, a 24-hour-concierge-led lobby, a dedicated pet spa, surround-sound theater, an above-ground parking garage, and secure package room. Architecture is by Miami-based M77R, led by Juan Azulay and Sylvia Pawlowski; interiors by Shannon Farrell Design (AIA, formerly of Handel Architects, Rockwell Group, and Elkus Manfredi); landscape architecture by H.L. Martin Landscape Architect; and sales and marketing by Brown Harris Stevens. The project is developed by Bayshore Grove Capital, the Miami-based real estate development and private equity firm founded by Vivian Zumot Dimond, with pricing from approximately $1.995 million for standard residences to $8 million-plus for penthouses.
Architect: M77R, Shannon Farrell Design, H.L. Martin Landscape Architect

StatusHistory.note: Construction resumed at 7918 West Drive under new team after a 2023 stall; completion targeted Q4 2026 (multi-step advance from announced — flagged for review)
Original groundbreaking 2023; construction revived by Bayshore Grove Capital in 2025, delivery targeted 2026.
Original groundbreaking 2023; construction revived by Bayshore Grove Capital in 2025, delivery targeted 2026.

Title: The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Naples
Description: A six-acre waterfront Ritz-Carlton-branded condominium from STOCK Development between the Gulf of Mexico and Vanderbilt Lagoon, with 128 residences, a 22-slip marina, and over 50,000 square feet of amenities anchored by The Vanderbilt Club.
DescriptionLong: The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Naples is a six-acre waterfront branded condominium development rising at the corner of Vanderbilt Beach Road and Gulf Shore Drive, positioned between the Gulf of Mexico and Vanderbilt Lagoon. The project will deliver 128 residences across an offering that includes standard residences, penthouses, and grand penthouses — with the entire 11th-floor penthouse collection already sold out ahead of opening. Amenities total more than 50,000 square feet of indoor and outdoor space anchored by The Vanderbilt Club, a 28,000-square-foot residents' facility with hospitality programming tailored to the Ritz-Carlton standard, alongside a private 22-slip marina facing Vanderbilt Lagoon and a landscaped arrival sequence that culminates in "Vivacious," a 30-foot stainless steel sculpture by Arkansas-based artist Hunter Brown installed at the entrance plaza in May 2026 as one of the final exterior additions to the development. Architecture is by Naples-based Curts Gaines Hall Jones Architects (CGHJ), with interiors by Susurrus, landscape architecture by Booth Design Group, lighting design by JKM Lighting Design, and engineering by J.R. Evans Engineering; Suffolk Construction is the general contractor. The project is developed by STOCK Development and its luxury condominium division STOCK Residences. As of June 2026, exterior work is largely complete and crews are focused on final site improvements and closeout activities; opening is on track for fall 2026.
Architect: Curts Gaines Hall Jones Architects, Susurrus, Booth Design Group, JKM Lighting Design

Topped off — the two Tower Residences reached full height of 12 stories; GC Suffolk
Gulf-front Ritz-Carlton Residences enclave tops off, on track for 2026 completion

Title: One Lady Bird Lake
Description: An 18-story, 298-residence Lady Bird Lake-front tower from Related Texas — 101 condominiums and 197 rental apartments across 750 feet of waterfront frontage in Austin's South Central Waterfront District — with architecture by Kohn Pedersen Fox and two ground-floor restaurants.
DescriptionLong: One Lady Bird Lake is an 18-story, 298-residence waterfront tower rising at 151 South 1st Street on a 3.3-acre site at the southern shore of Austin's Lady Bird Lake — a parcel currently serving as the surface parking lot for the Hyatt Regency Austin. The project comprises 101 condominiums in the building's western wing closest to the lake shore and 197 rental apartments in the eastern wing, served by separate lobbies for the two ownership types, with units ranging from one to three bedrooms across approximately 494,000 square feet of multifamily program. The ground floor delivers 10,400 square feet of restaurant space across two waterfront restaurants and 3,200 square feet of retail, oriented to capture views of Lady Bird Lake and the downtown Austin skyline. Architecture is by New York-based Kohn Pedersen Fox (KPF) as architect of record, with interiors by Workshop/APD, landscape architecture by TBG Partners, and engineering by GarzaEMC. Developed by Related Texas — the Austin platform of New York-based Related Companies, led by Texas President Mike Iannacone — One Lady Bird Lake is part of Related's broader investment in Austin's South Central Waterfront District, which also includes the firm's nearby 500 South Congress PUD. The site plan was approved by the City of Austin on July 1, 2025; the project originally launched in 2022 as a 15-story Class-A office tower in partnership with Austin-based Christopher Investment Company before redesigning to its current residential program in 2023 amid Austin's elevated office vacancy. Move-ins are scheduled to begin in early 2028.

StatusHistory.note: Related's One Lady Bird Lake rises at 151 S. 1st Street in Austin's South Central Waterfront District.

Title: Hilton Miami Airport Blue Lagoon Expansion
Description: A 1,000-key expansion of the Hilton Miami Airport Blue Lagoon — two new 14-story limited-service business hotels with 500 keys each, sixth-floor pool decks overlooking Blue Lagoon, structured parking, and Behar Font & Partners architecture across a 20.6-acre waterfront site.
DescriptionLong: The Hilton Miami Airport Blue Lagoon Expansion is a proposed 1,000-key addition to the existing 1983-built Hilton at 5101 Blue Lagoon Drive in Miami, currently under rezoning review with Miami-Dade County. Submitted by M2 Miami Airport Blue Lagoon LLC — an affiliate of New York-based hotel owner and operator MCR, which acquired the property in 2023 — the proposal calls for two new 14-story limited-service business hotels on the northwestern and southwestern portions of the 20.6-acre waterfront site, each containing 500 guestrooms ranging from approximately 320 to 860 square feet. Both towers feature sixth-floor pool decks and amenity areas overlooking Blue Lagoon. The existing 14-story, 508-key Hilton would continue operating during construction, with the full property reaching 1,508 keys at completion. The expansion is paired with a substantial structured parking program: a seven-story self-parking garage containing 419 spaces, a five-story valet garage serving the existing hotel, and two six-story garages — one paired with each new tower — containing 297 spaces apiece. Together the new structured parking replaces large portions of the site's existing surface parking inventory. The project is designed by Behar Font & Partners, with landscape architecture by Walk Landscape Architecture and Urban Design and traffic engineering by Kimley-Horn. The current application seeks to modify Resolution No. 4-ZAB-55-82 (the original 1980s zoning approval) with a non-use variance increasing permitted building height from 154 feet to 164 feet for the new towers, alongside setback variances; the applicant has also requested rezoning from IU-2 (Industrial District, Heavy Manufacturing) to RU-4A as an alternative entitlement path. The current proposal is a substantial scale-up from the developer's 2024 plans, which called for two eight-story hotels totaling 400 keys and a single five-story parking garage. MCR — led by CEO Tyler Morse — owns and operates a portfolio of approximately 150 hotels across 37 states, including the TWA Hotel at JFK, The High Line Hotel in Manhattan, and the Pasadena Hotel & Pool in California.
Architect: Behar Font & Partners, Walk Landscape Architecture and Urban Design

StatusHistory.note: The Hilton Miami Airport hotel sits in the Blue Lagoon office park west of MIA.

Title: PORT 32 Palm Beach Gardens
Description: A 471-slip ground-up marina redevelopment from PORT 32 Marinas — Palm Beach County's only Category 5 hurricane-rated marina — with 451 enclosed dry storage slips, 20 wet slips for vessels up to 100 feet, and 31,000 square feet of marine services and boat sales.
DescriptionLong: PORT 32 Palm Beach Gardens is a ground-up marina redevelopment at 2385 PGA Boulevard that topped out on June 4, 2026, with all three buildings reaching structural completion ahead of a December 2026 opening. The 471-slip facility is engineered as the only Category 5 hurricane-rated marina in Palm Beach County, certified to withstand wind speeds up to 180 miles per hour and recognized by most insurance providers as an accepted hurricane dockage location. The slip program splits into 451 fully enclosed dry storage slips — sized for vessels up to 60 feet in length overall and 22 feet in height — and 20 wet slips designed to accommodate vessels between 50 and 100 feet in length. Marina operations include transient dockage, haul-out services, launch facilities, and provisioning services, supported by advanced marine infrastructure including marina forklifts with a 75,000-pound lifting capacity and elevating operator cabs engineered for precision, visibility, and safety during vessel handling. Beyond the slips, the property delivers approximately 31,296 square feet of commercial space for marine services and boat sales, including climate-controlled marine offices, retail showroom space, heated and cooled service units, and additional storage and flexible-use spaces. The project is developed by PORT 32 Marinas, LLC under CEO Austin Schell and Regional Vice President Mike Vinson, and was constructed by Miller Construction Company under CEO Brian Sudduth and Project Manager Harley R. Miller, with more than 150 construction workers and project personnel contributing through the topping-out milestone.

StatusHistory.note: Topped out June 4, 2026 — all three buildings structurally complete; $80M marina set to open December 2026
All three dry-stack buildings topped out at the $80M PORT 32 Palm Beach Gardens marina redevelopment; pre-leasing underway (per Marina Dock Age)

Title: Sweetens Cove Golf Club
Description: A property-wide expansion of golf's cult nine-hole Tennessee destination — adding a King Collins Dormer-designed 13-hole short course, multi-acre putting green, stay-and-play cabins, restaurant, and Sweetens Cove Spirits bottle shop and micro-distillery — led by Reef Capital Partners.
DescriptionLong:
Sweetens Cove Golf Club is undergoing a property-wide expansion that will transform the cult nine-hole inland links course in South Pittsburg, Tennessee — 30 miles west of Chattanooga — into what course architect Rob Collins calls "the ultimate 24-hour golf playground." Led by Reef Capital Partners (CEO Jared Lucero) in partnership with the Sweetens Cove ownership group, the multi-year expansion adds a King Collins Dormer-designed 13-hole short course (groundbreaking May 2026, opening summer 2027) that connects to and overlaps with the existing King Collins course, encouraging "cross-country" play. A two-acre winding putting green debuts spring 2027. The expansion delivers the property's first overnight stay: one-bedroom cabins with single or double occupancy, opening in phases starting late 2027 with completion in 2028. A 5,100-square-foot private full-service restaurant and bar — reserved for golf-package guests — and a 1,775-square-foot public-facing Sweetens Cove Spirits bottle shop and micro-distillery (opening fall 2027) round out the hospitality program, alongside three golf simulator bays with club fitting, expanded retail, a fishing dock near the sixth hole, a skeet shooting range, an Octagon Barn event space with storage for 60 whiskey barrels, and a Thermal Village debuting spring 2028. The hospitality program is designed by Pfeffer Torode Architecture; course work continues under King Collins Dormer, the Rob Collins and Tad King design partnership that crafted the original 2014 routing. The expansion preserves Sweetens Cove's distinct operating model: all-day passes, 8:30 a.m. shed gatherings for course conditions and starting-hole assignments, daily caps of 60 passes Tuesday through Thursday and 45 Friday through Sunday.

Story so far: Rob Collins and Ari Techner signed the original Sweetens Cove lease in May 2014; King Collins designed the nine-hole inland links course that opened later that year and quickly became a national pilgrimage site. In 2019, the ownership group expanded to include Peyton Manning, Andy Roddick, Tom Nolan, Skip Bronson, Drew Holcomb, and Chris Parsons (now Sweetens Cove Spirits CEO). Reef Capital Partners joined as expansion developer in early 2025. The King Collins Dormer-designed 13-hole short course broke ground in May 2026 — the first construction milestone in the property-wide expansion.

StatusHistory.note: Sweetens Cove just broke ground on its 13-hole short course — the next chapter for golf's cult Tennessee destination
Sweetens Cove unveils major expansion: short course, restaurant, distillery and lodging
New 13-hole short course (King Collins Dormer; developer Reef Capital Partners) opens summer 2027 — corrected from 2028; lodging/F&B follow into 2028.

Title: Bayside North
Description: The second phase of the waterfront Bayside community in downtown Sarasota — a seven-story, 96-residence building with a top-floor clubhouse over Sarasota Bay, a rooftop resort pool, a pickleball court, botanical courtyards, and a ground-floor restaurant.
DescriptionLong: Bayside North is the second phase of the Bayside waterfront community taking shape in downtown Sarasota's Rosemary District and Bayfront corridor. The seven-story building holds 96 luxury residences in a curated mix of one-, two-, and three-bedroom layouts averaging roughly 1,200 square feet, complemented by eight fully furnished guest suites and 15 workforce housing units. Designed around its waterfront setting, it is crowned by a top-floor clubhouse with sweeping west-facing views over Sarasota Bay, while the ground level brings neighborhood-activating amenities including a pickleball court and a 3,000-square-foot restaurant. As part of the broader Bayside masterplan, residents draw on more than 50,000 square feet of amenities, a rooftop resort-style pool, lush botanical courtyards, and integrated food-and-beverage programming, creating a walkable, design-forward alternative to traditional condo living.

StatusHistory.note: Allen Morris Co. closes $43M construction loan from Affinius and Axonic for Bayside North; vertical work to begin June 2026, first units late 2027
Allen Morris Co. closes $43M construction loan from Affinius and Axonic for Bayside North; vertical work to begin June 2026, first units late 2027
Construction commenced June 2026 in Sarasota's Rosemary District (per Sarasota Magazine); DB's 2025 start was incorrect

Title: The Residences at The St. Regis Los Cabos
Description: A St. Regis-branded oceanfront residential collection at Quivira Resort on the Pacific tip of the Baja Peninsula — 74 residences alongside a 120-room St. Regis hotel on 33 acres with 1,200 feet of beachfront, three Carlos Gaytan-led dining venues, the largest wellness center in Los Cabos, and direct access to the Jack Nicklaus-designed Quivira Golf Club.
DescriptionLong: The Residences at The St. Regis Los Cabos at Quivira is a 74-residence oceanfront collection co-located with a 120-room St. Regis-branded hotel on a 33-acre site with 1,200 feet of Pacific beachfront, set within the 1,850-acre Quivira Los Cabos master-planned community on the southern tip of the Baja Peninsula. The residential program covers three- and four-bedroom oceanview condominiums (starting around $3 million), four exclusive oceanview penthouses with private rooftop terraces and plunge pools (extending to over 6,300 interior square feet plus more than 7,000 square feet of exterior terrace), five-bedroom direct-oceanfront villas (6,740 to 6,871 interior square feet, with listings reaching nearly $15 million), and luxury townhomes. Hotel and resort amenities include three dining experiences led by Michelin-starred Chef Carlos Gaytan, the largest fitness and wellness center in Los Cabos, seven resort pools (five designed for families), an exclusive beach club, a St. Regis spa, and round-the-clock St. Regis butler service. Residents have direct access to the Jack Nicklaus Signature-designed Quivira Golf Club on the same property — named Golf Inc. and Golf Magazine's "Development of the Year" upon its opening — along with three miles of pristine Pacific beachfront across the broader Quivira community. The project is designed by SB Architects (with offices in San Francisco, Miami, and Hong Kong) and Gomez Vazquez International, with interiors by Hirsch Bedner Associates and land planning by EDSA. Co-developed by Olarena Hospitality Group (the team behind Hacienda del Mar) and Pueblo Bonito Group / Quivira under Ernesto Coppel, the project operates under license from Marriott International, which manages the St. Regis brand and provides residential staff under the St. Regis service standard. Construction began in January 2020, with delivery anticipated in 2026.
Architect: SB Architects, Gomez Vazquez International, Hirsch Bedner Associates
Developer: Olarena Hospitality Group, Pueblo Bonito Group

StatusHistory.note: The St. Regis Los Cabos and its residences sit within the Quivira community on the Pacific coast.
Vocabulary cleanup: removing the 'Spa' tag from the TMW type registry (spas are an amenity, not a primary use). Stripping Spa from types[] across all affected projects.

Title: Montage Cay
Description: A 53-acre private island resort in the Abacos, Bahamas — 47 branded residences across Cottages, Villas, and Estates, a 50-suite Montage Inn, a 46-slip deep-water marina for vessels up to 100 feet, Spa Montage, and seven white-sand beaches, all under Montage Hotels & Resorts management.
DescriptionLong: Montage Cay is a 53-acre private island resort and residential community in the Abacos chain of The Bahamas, located one mile offshore from Marsh Harbour on Great Abaco — roughly 170 miles due east of Palm Beach and a 60-minute flight from Miami. The development comprises 47 branded residences sited across seven enclaves overlooking the Sea of Abaco, organized into five typologies: Shore Cottages, Beach Villas, Seaside Villas, Marina Villas, and a limited collection of Ocean Estates. Residences range from two- to six-bedroom homes (with select Estate Residences scaling to four to eight bedrooms), spanning 3,100 to more than 7,000 square feet, and are sold turnkey and fully furnished from $6.9 million. The flagship Hibiscus Estate Residence runs over 8,500 square feet across five bedrooms and five-and-a-half baths. Resort-side amenities include the 50-suite Montage Inn (Montage Hotels & Resorts' first Bahamas property), Spa Montage, multiple dining venues, and full resort programming — alongside a 46-slip deep-water marina exclusive to owners and capable of accommodating vessels up to 100 feet. Architectural design is led by Robert C. Glazier and Gensler, with the entire island's site planning and landscape architecture by SKS Studio. The project is developed by Sterling Montage Cay Ltd., a subsidiary of Sterling Global Financial — a developer with over $10.5 billion in residential, mixed-use, hospitality, and marina projects across its portfolio — operating under license from Montage International. Residences are sold as fee-simple freehold property, with Bahamian permanent residency available to purchasers investing more than $1 million.
Architect: Robert C. Glazier, Gensler, SKS Studio
Developer: Sterling Global Financial, Sterling Montage Cay Ltd.

StatusHistory.note: Montage Cay's resort will offer 30 ocean-view hotel keys; private-island debut slated for 2026.
Montage Cay's resort will offer 30 ocean-view hotel keys; private-island debut slated for 2026.
Montage Cay's resort will feature 50 all-suite accommodations, including over-water bungalows.
Vocabulary cleanup: removing the 'Spa' tag from the TMW type registry (spas are an amenity, not a primary use). Stripping Spa from types[] across all affected projects.

Title: Thirty Six
Description: A boutique collection of 38 luxury condominium residences on Paradise Island near Atlantis and the Four Seasons Ocean Club — the first new multi-family residential development on Paradise Island in a decade when it delivered, built on the highest elevation on the island with underground parking and views over Nassau harbor.
DescriptionLong: Thirty Six is a boutique condominium development on Bayview Drive, Paradise Island, built on the highest elevation on the island at thirty feet above sea level — a strategic siting choice that also allowed for underground parking for cars and golf carts. The 38-residence project marked the first new multi-family residential development on Paradise Island in a decade when construction completed in December 2018, opening with two- and three-bedroom configurations alongside penthouse residences. Each residence features nine-and-a-half-foot ceilings, storm-rated glass, fine molding around all doors and windows, top-of-the-line appliances, lavish bathrooms, and large patios. Amenities include an infinity pool and pool deck, a fitness center, and walking access to the Atlantis Resort, the Four Seasons Ocean Club, the Ocean Club Golf Club, and the broader Paradise Island dining, shopping, and entertainment district. Ownership entitles residents to apply for golf membership at the Ocean Club, and qualifying purchasers may apply for Bahamian Economic Permanent Residency. Built in partnership between Sterling Global Financial and Aristo Development, the project has been operated with a professionally managed rental program for owners since delivery.

Title: Hyatt Centric Grand Cayman
Description: A 316-room boutique four-star Hyatt Centric on Seven Mile Beach with 150 studio, one-, and two-bedroom residences for individual ownership — interiors by Clodagh Design, with a rooftop bar, two pools, multiple dining venues, and pickleball courts, from the Sterling Global Financial and LASKA Development Group partnership behind the Grand Hyatt next door.
DescriptionLong: Hyatt Centric Grand Cayman is a 316-room boutique four-star resort and branded residence development on Seven Mile Beach, positioned as a design-focused, lifestyle-oriented counterpoint to its larger Grand Hyatt sibling next door. The project includes 316 keys total, of which 150 studio, one-, and two-bedroom residences are available for individual ownership, with select residential layouts featuring kitchenettes, in-unit washer/dryer units, and dedicated owner closets that support extended stays. Interiors are by Clodagh Design, the New York-based AD100 firm known for its biophilic, holistic approach to hospitality and residential design. Amenities include multiple food and beverage venues, a signature rooftop restaurant and bar, two pools and a jacuzzi, a fitness center, indoor meeting rooms, an outdoor event lawn, pickleball courts, and direct Seven Mile Beach access. The development is led by 269 West Bay Road Ltd. in collaboration with Sterling Global Financial and LASKA Development Group — the same partnership that delivered the 100% sold-out Grand Hyatt Grand Cayman next door — under a revocable license agreement from Hyatt Corporation. Residences are priced from the low $700,000s, with project completion targeted for 2028.
Developer: 269 West Bay Road Ltd., Sterling Global Financial, LASKA Development Group

StatusHistory.note: Sterling Global & LASKA's 10-storey Hyatt Centric rising on former Margaritaville site, Seven Mile Beach; 316 keys, 150 for sale
Sterling Global & LASKA's 10-storey Hyatt Centric rising on former Margaritaville site, Seven Mile Beach; 316 keys, 150 for sale
Pre-construction residence sales launched on the former Margaritaville site, Seven Mile Beach (developer Sterling Global + LASKA; ~160 hotel keys + for-sale residences).
Pre-construction residence sales launched on the former Margaritaville site, Seven Mile Beach (developer Sterling Global + LASKA; ~160 hotel keys + for-sale residences).

Title: Briland Club Residences & Marina
Description: The Bahamas' only bay-to-beach private club community on Harbour Island — 93 planned residences across 27 acres, a 50-slip megayacht marina for vessels up to 300 feet, the restored "Haunted House" Mansion Clubhouse, and Pink Sand Beach frontage, with interiors by Bunny Williams and hospitality by The Royal Portfolio.
DescriptionLong: Briland Club Residences & Marina is a private members' club community on Harbour Island, the famously pink-sand outer island of Eleuthera in The Bahamas — distinguished as the country's only bay-to-beach development, with direct access to both the protected harbor and the legendary Pink Sand Beach. Spanning 27 acres in Dunmore Town, the master plan calls for 93 residences across a range of formats: 2,100-square-foot Coral Cottages with interiors by AD100 Hall of Fame designer Bunny Williams (designed by CMA Design Studio), Club Cottages (3,300 to 3,700 square feet), Club Residences (1,200 square feet with terracotta tile and coral stone finishes), Garden Villas (3-bedroom with private pool, fire pit, and 6,500 total square feet of indoor/outdoor space), and custom Estate Homes on hillside homesites. Residences are priced from $4 million. The 50-slip megayacht marina accommodates vessels from 75 to 300 feet, designed to withstand Category 3 hurricanes, with an advanced floating dock system, in-slip high-speed fueling, 80,000-gallon diesel capacity, a crew lounge, and 24/7 security. The Mansion Clubhouse is set within the historic, fully-restored "Haunted House" estate, with three dining venues, a wellness center and spa, beach club, kids club, and the only resort in The Bahamas that provides 100 percent of its own electricity (3 megawatts) and water (on-site reverse osmosis). Owned by 4M Harbour Island Ltd. under chairman Michael Wiener, with Sterling Global Advisors serving as Development Manager and The Royal Portfolio (the family-owned South African hospitality group behind The Silo Hotel and Birkenhead House) managing all hospitality operations.
Developer: 4M Harbour Island Ltd., Sterling Global Advisors, The Royal Portfolio

StatusHistory.note: Vocabulary cleanup: removing the 'Spa' tag from the TMW type registry (spas are an amenity, not a primary use). Stripping Spa from types[] across all affected projects.

Title: 1000 Boulevard of the Arts
Description: A 20-story waterfront tower at the corner of Boulevard of the Arts and Quay Commons in downtown Sarasota, combining the 174-key Hyatt Centric Harborside hotel with 117 luxury condominium residences overlooking Sarasota Bay.
DescriptionLong: 1000 Boulevard of the Arts is a 20-story mixed-use waterfront tower in downtown Sarasota's Quay district, rising at the corner of Boulevard of the Arts and Quay Commons on the former Hyatt Regency Sarasota site. The tower houses two distinct components within a single structure: the 174-key Hyatt Centric Harborside hotel on levels 6 through 14, and 117 luxury condominium residences above. Hotel amenities include more than 7,000 square feet of flexible event space anchored by a ballroom, a ground-floor restaurant with indoor and outdoor seating, a resort-style swimming pool, and a pool bar. Hotel interiors are characterized by curved architectural forms, terrazzo and wood flooring, tropical landscaping elements, warm wood accents, intricate fixtures, decorative lighting, and a palette of natural materials reflective of Sarasota's waterfront setting. The residential collection ranges from one- to three-bedroom-plus-den layouts spanning approximately 1,200 to more than 3,000 square feet, with a 24-hour staffed lobby, valet and self-parking, and a collection of private amenity spaces. A separate residential tower is planned for the adjacent site along Boulevard of the Arts.

StatusHistory.note: Correction: former Hyatt Regency demolished Feb 2026 and site cleared, but Kolter's 20-story/117-unit tower is still awaiting permits — vertical construction not due to begin until Q3 2026
Correction: former Hyatt Regency demolished Feb 2026 and site cleared, but Kolter's 20-story/117-unit tower is still awaiting permits — vertical construction not due to begin until Q3 2026
Condo reservations converted to contracts; ~$40M in sales in first 45 days
174-key Hyatt Centric Harborside + 117 condos on Sarasota's Bayfront/Quay district

Title: Indigo Bay
Description: A boutique waterfront residential community on the eastern edge of reef-protected South Sound in Grand Cayman, with sea views, a private dock, and a stone coastline protected by a nine-foot seawall — currently accepting buyers for a second phase adding 23 units.
DescriptionLong: Indigo Bay is a boutique luxury residential development on the eastern edge of South Sound, a reef-protected stretch of Grand Cayman's southern coast — a quieter, calmer counterpoint to the high-density development along Seven Mile Beach. The site features its own private dock, a stone coastline reinforced with a nine-foot above-sea-level seawall, and views directly over the sound's turquoise waters. The first phase delivered an initial run of residences; a second phase currently accepting buyers will add 23 additional units, extending the community's scale while preserving its intimate footprint. Developed by Sterling Global Financial as part of the firm's continued investment in the Cayman Islands market alongside its Seven Mile Beach Grand Hyatt and Hyatt Centric projects.

Title: Hurricane Hole Superyacht Marina
Description: The Caribbean's most technologically advanced superyacht marina on Paradise Island — the first in The Bahamas to offer in-slip fueling, with a 240-foot turning basin, floating concrete docks up to 420 feet, and the Paradise Landing mixed-use development surrounding it with luxury residences, dining, retail, and a medical center.
DescriptionLong: Hurricane Hole Superyacht Marina is the centerpiece of Paradise Landing, a mixed-use redevelopment of one of Paradise Island's most historic dock locations, recreated as the Caribbean's most exclusive berth for megayachts. Following a comprehensive Sterling Global Financial-led redesign, the marina was the first in The Bahamas to introduce in-slip fueling — allowing vessels to be fueled directly at their berth — alongside floating concrete docks up to 420 feet in length and a 240-foot turning basin engineered to accommodate the world's largest yachts. The surrounding Paradise Landing development functions as a "Downtown Paradise Island," with a seven-story luxury condominium featuring two- and three-bedroom suites overlooking the marina, two high-end restaurants, retail and entertainment venues, executive office space, a wine and liquor store, and a medical center and pharmacy serving residents and the broader Paradise Island community. The marina has earned a reputation as the most coveted berth in the Caribbean among yacht captains and crews, supported by 24-hour security, concierge services, and direct access to Atlantis Resort, the Four Seasons Ocean Club, and the broader resort island ecosystem. Developed by Sterling Global Financial as a flagship project in their Bahamas portfolio.

Title: Grand Hyatt Grand Cayman
Description: A 381-key Grand Hyatt resort on Seven Mile Beach with 167 branded residences for sale, six restaurants, three pools, a 12,000-square-foot spa, and 25,000 square feet of event space — one of only two five-star hotels on Grand Cayman's most coveted shoreline, with residences 100% sold out ahead of opening.
DescriptionLong: Grand Hyatt Grand Cayman Resort & Residences occupies a premium 7.1-acre beachfront site at the southern end of West Bay Road on Seven Mile Beach, with 900 feet of direct beachfront and approximately one mile of separation from George Town's center. The 10-story property comprises 381 keys total (a mix of hotel guestrooms, condo-hotel guestrooms, and suites) alongside 167 individually owned branded residences featuring 10-foot ceilings, 9-foot windows, Thermador appliances, and full Grand Hyatt service. Resort amenities include six distinct food and beverage venues, three swimming pools (with a dramatic main resort pool, a family pool near Camp Hyatt, and fully-serviced beachfront cabanas), a 12,000-square-foot destination spa and fitness center, sound therapy and crystal grotto wellness programs, an oceanview vitality pool, and 25,000 square feet of versatile event space — the largest function facilities in the Cayman Islands. Owners may participate in a voluntary hotel rental program. Co-developed by Sterling Global Financial, LASKA Development Group, and Pageant Beach Hotel Ltd., the project was built by Americaribe (a subsidiary of Bouygues Bâtiment International) and operated under the Grand Hyatt brand by Hyatt Hotels Corporation. The residential component is 100% sold out as of January 2026, with the property opening in early 2026.
Developer: Sterling Global Financial, LASKA Development Group, Pageant Beach Hotel Ltd.

StatusHistory.note: Grand Hyatt Seven Mile Beach resort tracking to early-2026 completion; 382 guestrooms plus branded residences
Grand Hyatt Seven Mile Beach resort tracking to early-2026 completion; 382 guestrooms plus branded residences
Vocabulary cleanup: removing the 'Spa' tag from the TMW type registry (spas are an amenity, not a primary use). Stripping Spa from types[] across all affected projects.
Grand Hyatt Grand Cayman opened on Seven Mile Beach April 1, 2026; 382 keys per Caribbean Mag & Resort Flock

Title: The Wolseley Hotel New York
Description: The Wolseley Hotels' first property worldwide, a 76-room luxury hotel opening in early 2027 in the landmark 1905 building at 130 West 44th Street in Midtown Manhattan.
DescriptionLong: The Wolseley Hotel New York is the inaugural property and North American culinary flagship of The Wolseley Hotels, a Minor Hotels brand born from London's iconic Wolseley restaurant. Set in Midtown Manhattan steps from Bryant Park, the hotel occupies the landmark building at 130 West 44th Street — the former clubhouse of The Lambs Club, designed by McKim, Mead & White. Developed by Ben-Josef Group Holdings, the property pairs 76 guest rooms and suites with the brand's first U.S. outpost of The Wolseley restaurant and bar, a cellar-level speakeasy, and a redesigned wellness centre, blending grand European café heritage with contemporary New York luxury.

StatusHistory.note: Minor Hotels converting the former Lambs Club at 130 W 44th St into 76-key Wolseley flagship near Bryant Park
Minor Hotels converting the former Lambs Club at 130 W 44th St into 76-key Wolseley flagship near Bryant Park

Title: Ruby New York City
Description: An 187-room urban lifestyle hotel rising inside a converted 1930s building on Avenue of the Americas, anchored by a 24/7 lobby bar open to guests and locals.
DescriptionLong: A flagship conversion of an 18-story, 1930s-era building on Manhattan's Avenue of the Americas into 187 guest rooms built around a lean, design-forward "everything you need, nothing you don't" philosophy — signature beds, rainfall showers, and premium finishes in efficiently scaled spaces. The social heart of the property is a 24/7 lobby bar serving both overnight guests and neighborhood regulars, set steps from Herald Square, the Empire State Building, and Madison Square Garden.
Developer: Ac Developers, IHG Hotels & Resorts, Ruby Hotels, Aimbridge Hospitality

StatusHistory.note: IHG's Ruby converting an 18-story 1930s building on Sixth Ave near Herald Square; 187 keys, 2027 debut
IHG's Ruby converting an 18-story 1930s building on Sixth Ave near Herald Square; 187 keys, 2027 debut
IHG's Ruby converting an 18-story 1930s building on Sixth Ave near Herald Square; 187 keys, 2027 debut

Title: Mandarin Oriental, Desaru Coast
Description: A beachfront resort set within 128 acres of rainforest on the South China Sea, with 44 suites — each with a private plunge pool, courtyard and veranda — plus a four-bedroom pool villa.
DescriptionLong: A low-rise luxury beachfront resort folded into 128 acres of rainforest along the South China Sea. The property comprises 44 secluded suites, each with its own private plunge pool, courtyard and veranda framing views of either the sea or the canopy, anchored by a four-bedroom pool villa. Guests have a choice of five dining destinations, including a beach club and a coastal lounge bar, while a spa brings a yoga pavilion, fitness centre and beauty salon. Sporting facilities include tennis and padel courts, with championship golf nearby. A collection of branded residences is planned alongside the resort.
Developer: Desaru Development Holdings, Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group

StatusHistory.note: Vocabulary cleanup: removing the 'Spa' tag from the TMW type registry (spas are an amenity, not a primary use). Stripping Spa from types[] across all affected projects.
Restore Resort tag stripped by deprecated resort→Hotel worker synonym (fixed in worker/src/mcp.js, deployed 2026-06-17).

Title: Olana Naples
Description: A six-story Gulf-front enclave of just 12 estate-sized residences on Gulf Shore Boulevard North, anchored by two Beachfront Villas priced from $35 million.
DescriptionLong: Olana Naples introduces a first-of-its-kind residential model on the Gulf Coast at 1121 Gulf Shore Boulevard North — just 12 estate-sized homes, each exceeding 10,000 square feet, set within a six-story enclave. The development's recently debuted Beachfront Villas anchor the second floor with two grand-scale residences priced from $35 million, each spanning over 11,500 square feet with five bedrooms, 2,000-plus-square-foot terraces, eighty linear feet of Gulf frontage, and private pools set directly above the beach. Architecture by Randall Stofft Architects and SB Architects with interiors by Champalimaud Design balances Gulf-facing scale with multigenerational programming: presentation kitchens paired with chef's kitchens, climate-controlled wine rooms, soundproof theaters, dedicated wellness suites with steam room, sauna, and cold plunge. Developed by Kolter Urban and BH Group, with residences pricing from $30 million.
Architect: Randall Stofft Architects, SB Architects, Champalimaud Design

StatusHistory.note: Kolter Urban is pre-selling Olana's 12 beachfront residences; groundbreaking is scheduled for 2026, not yet underway.
Kolter Urban is pre-selling Olana's 12 beachfront residences; groundbreaking is scheduled for 2026, not yet underway.
Sales launched for Olana — Kolter Urban's ultra-luxury Naples beachfront residences
Sales launched for Olana — Kolter Urban's ultra-luxury Naples beachfront residences

Title: Miramar Cove
Description: Miramar Cove is a $1 billion, 125-acre master-planned mixed-use community in Miramar, Florida, centered on a 5.5-acre crystal-blue lagoon.
DescriptionLong: Miramar Cove is a $1 billion, 125-acre master-planned mixed-use community rising in Miramar, Florida, at Miramar Parkway and Red Road in west Broward County. Developed by Sunbeam Properties and Stiles and designed by architecture firm Cooper Carry, the walkable district centers on a 5.5-acre crystal-blue lagoon ringed by restaurants, retail, and waterfront promenades. The community will deliver 2,874 homes alongside roughly 400,000 square feet of shops, a 35,000-square-foot grocer, 125,000 square feet of Class A offices, and a 185-key hotel. Resident amenities include a private beach club, about five miles of walking paths, and connections to acres of parks and natural trails.

StatusHistory.note: Sunbeam Properties and Stiles broke ground on the $1B, 125-acre Miramar Cove on May 27, 2026; plan includes 2,874 homes and a 185-room hotel, with grand opening targeted Q4 2028.
Miramar approves $1B, 125-acre mixed-use Miramar Cove (Sunbeam Properties + Stiles): 2,874 homes, 185-key hotel, 400k sf retail, 125k sf office around a 5.5-acre lagoon.

Title: Saravela
Description: A two-tower luxury condominium development rising on North Tamiami Trail near Sarasota Bay — downtown Sarasota's first new high-rise designed for short-term rental freedom, with 282 fully furnished residences, 11 townhomes, and more than 47,000 square feet of amenities across six dedicated levels.
DescriptionLong: Saravela is a two-tower luxury condominium development rising on North Tamiami Trail at the northern gateway to downtown Sarasota, distinguished as the city's first new high-rise designed for short-term rental freedom — letting owners rent their fully furnished residences for stays as short as three days through a professionally managed program. The 1.7-acre site straddles two zoning districts, with one tower rising 18 stories in the Downtown Bayfront and the other stepping down to 11 stories in the Downtown Core. Together the towers deliver 282 condominiums and penthouses, 11 two-story townhomes, 40 attainable rental units, and more than 11,000 square feet of ground-floor retail. Inside each residence, 12-foot ceilings, floor-to-ceiling glass, expansive terraces, Italian cabinetry, Sub-Zero and Wolf appliances, and furnishings curated by Steven G. create a layered, modern coastal aesthetic. The amenity program spans more than 47,000 square feet across six dedicated levels — two rooftop pools overlooking Sarasota Bay, two spas, club lounges, wine vaults, fitness studios, saunas, hot and cold plunge pools, a golf simulator, putting green, bocce court, an attended Kids' Club with duckpin bowling and splash pool, game lounges, co-working spaces, pet amenities, and rooftop firepits, all backed by concierge, valet, optional housekeeping, grocery delivery, 24-hour security, and a complimentary one-year sports membership at TPC Prestancia. The development sits within walking distance of The Quay, The Bay Park, waterfront dining, galleries, marinas, performing arts venues, and St. Armands Circle.
Architect: Kimmich Smith Architecture, ID & Design International

StatusHistory.note: GSP Development unveils Saravela, a two-tower luxury condominium with a flexible short-term-rental model in downtown Sarasota.
GSP Development unveils Saravela, a two-tower luxury condominium with a flexible short-term-rental model in downtown Sarasota.
GSP Development unveils Saravela, a two-tower luxury condominium with a flexible short-term-rental model in downtown Sarasota.
Confirmed Residences: 282 condos plus townhomes (ancillary ground-floor retail) in downtown Sarasota.
Reservation phase / sales gallery launched Dec 2025 with Premier Sotheby's per Tampa Bay 100 & TBBW

Title: Aria Reserve
Description: Twin 62-story glass towers rising along five acres of Biscayne Bay waterfront in Edgewater — set to become the tallest waterfront residential twin towers in the United States, with 782 condominium residences across two acres of indoor and outdoor amenities.
DescriptionLong: Aria Reserve is a twin-tower luxury condominium development on five acres of Biscayne Bay waterfront in Miami's Edgewater neighborhood — twin 62-story glass towers rising approximately 649 feet, set to become the tallest waterfront residential twin towers in the United States. Together the towers deliver 782 condominium residences across 547 linear feet of water frontage, behind an iconic zig-zag facade pattern designed to read as twin sinuous curves rising from bay to sky. The South Tower opened in 2025 and the North Tower follows in 2026. Floor categorizations include Panoramic Residences on floors 3–50 (10-foot ceilings), Skyview Residences on floors 51–56 (11-foot ceilings), Penthouses on floors 57–59, and three Tri-Level Penthouse Mansions on floors 60–62 — seven-bedroom three-story residences ranging from 7,790 to 8,385 square feet, each with a private rooftop terrace, summer kitchen, and private pool. Every residence features two private elevators, a dedicated office or bonus room, flow-through layouts with east- and west-facing waterfront views, and 11-foot-deep outdoor terraces. The amenity program spans more than two acres of indoor and outdoor space — a semi-Olympic pool, full-service spa, fitness and yoga studios, tennis and basketball courts, children's play zones, screening rooms, co-working lounges, a private marina component, a luxury bayview restaurant, a dog park, meditation deck, and podium and bayfront decks tied into the Miami Baywalk, anchored by a residents-only sky lobby with wine cellar and business center.
Architect: Arquitectonica, MORADA Haute Furniture Boutique, ArquitectonicaGEO

StatusHistory.note: Aria Reserve's North Tower surpassed the halfway mark in construction.
Aria Reserve South Tower delivered and ready for occupancy in Edgewater
Aria Reserve North Tower tops off in Edgewater
South Tower received its TCO — first Edgewater tower delivered, with closings underway. (North Tower topped out 2025-08-19 and is finishing.)

Title: White Elephant Aspen
Description: A 54-key boutique hotel on Main Street in Aspen's historic West End, with mountain-contemporary architecture in wood, stone, and brass — anchored by Lola 41 in a glass-enclosed pool pavilion and a 125-piece art collection threaded through the public spaces and guest rooms.
DescriptionLong: White Elephant Aspen is a 54-key boutique hotel on Main Street in Aspen's historic West End, blending mountain-contemporary architecture with an interior signature of warm rust, cognac, charcoal, and warm-white tones layered over leather, wood, and stone. The room program runs from King Rooms to Luxury Hideaways, mountain- and town-view King Suites, the Highlands Suite, and the West End Suite, with the Ajax Penthouse at the top — a three-bedroom, 1,560-square-foot retreat with a chef's kitchen, a log fireplace, and a private mountain-view terrace with a fire pit overlooking Aspen Mountain. Flexible adjoining layouts let families and groups connect rooms into multi-bedroom suites. Public spaces include a glass-enclosed pool pavilion that houses Lola 41, the property's seafood and sushi restaurant, and the 41 Below private dining space. Brass elephant details, a brass doorknocker on every guest-room door, and a 125-piece curated art collection thread throughout the property, complemented by a chauffeured BMW fleet and an interior palette designed to harmonize with the alpine landscape rather than dominate it.

Title: Magic City Innovation District
Description: A 17-acre mixed-use master plan in Little Haiti spanning 17 parcels and 7.8 million square feet of office, residential, hotel, and retail — programmed around a proposed Brightline transit station with more than 2,600 residential units, 432 hotel rooms, and roughly 2 million square feet of office space across phased delivery.
DescriptionLong: Magic City Innovation District is a 17-acre, 17-parcel mixed-use master plan in Little Haiti, just north of Wynwood and the Design District and adjacent to the MiMo district. The Special Area Plan, approved by the Miami City Commission in 2019 and revived in June 2026, calls for 7.8 million square feet of programmed development including more than 2,600 residential units, 432 hotel rooms, approximately 2 million square feet of office space, and 344,548 square feet of commercial-retail. Roughly 165,528 square feet — about 21% of the total lot area — is reserved for open space. The site is generally bounded by Northeast 2nd Avenue, Northeast 4th Court, Northeast 59th Terrace, and Northeast 64th Terrace, centered around a proposed Little Haiti Brightline station. The signature project Station M, modeled on Paris's Station F, will deliver a two-tower innovation center with 215,000 square feet of incubator and accelerator space across more than 500,000 square feet of office, retail, and restaurant uses. Phase 1 anchors the district with an office campus and a 25-story residential tower carrying 349 rental apartments and 10,000 square feet of ground-floor retail. The phased master plan integrates adaptive reuse of existing factories and warehouses with new construction, and includes a $31 million commitment to the Little Haiti Revitalization Trust supporting affordable housing, education, small-business development, and public parks.

StatusHistory.note: Magic City Innovation District, a ~$1B master-planned mixed-use development, advanced in Miami's Little Haiti.

Title: Waterline
Description: At 1,025 feet and 74 stories, Waterline is Texas' tallest building — a mixed-use tower beside Waterloo Greenway combining 352 luxury apartments, a 1 Hotel, Class AA offices and retail, with residences opening fall 2026.
DescriptionLong: Waterline is a 74-story, 1,025-foot mixed-use supertall at 98 Red River Street in Austin's Rainey Street district, now the tallest building in Texas. Developed by Lincoln Property Company and Kairoi Residential and designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox, the tower stacks 352 luxury apartments across its top 33 floors above roughly 703,000 square feet of Class AA office space, a 252-key 1 Hotel occupying 13 floors, and 24,000 square feet of ground-level retail and restaurants. The 3-plus-acre campus integrates with the Waterloo Greenway park system along Waller Creek. Waterline broke ground in September 2022 and topped out in August 2025; the office and hotel are targeted to open in spring 2026 with the residences following in fall 2026.

StatusHistory.note: Construction began on Waterline, set to become Texas' tallest tower at 1,025 ft / 74 stories, on Austin's Rainey Street.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: 352 residences + 251-key 1 Hotel Austin + 700,000 sf office + retail in Texas' tallest tower.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: 352 residences + 251-key 1 Hotel Austin + 700,000 sf office + retail in Texas' tallest tower.
Waterline topped out at 1,025 ft / 74 stories, officially becoming Texas' tallest tower.
1 Hotel Austin opened reservations ahead of its August 2026 opening within the Waterline tower.
Waterline topped out Aug 1, 2025 at 1,025 ft — tallest tower in Texas — per Community Impact / KPF / Lincoln; phased openings fall 2026.

Title: The Motley
Description: A three-tower, ~1,200-home mixed-use development rising on a former dairy-factory site in Midtown Nashville, with the 26-story Phase 1 north tower under construction since early 2025.
DescriptionLong: The Motley is a three-tower mixed-use development by IPB Properties transforming a former dairy-factory site at 1401 Church Street into a new gateway to Midtown Nashville. Designed by Nashville-based HASTINGS Architecture with construction by Axiom Builders, the full project will bring nearly 1,200 rental homes and more than 70,000 square feet of locally inspired commercial space, arranged around a central landscaped plaza with 'dancing' arches and a grand stair-ramp 'stramp.' Industrial heritage is echoed in precast facade panels imprinted with silo impressions. The Phase 1 north tower — which officially began construction in February 2025 — rises 26 floors and roughly 300 feet with 326 residential units, 27,000 square feet of amenities and 14,000 square feet of retail; the two later phases add a 32-story tower and a third tower of undetermined height. Phase 1 completion is anticipated in 2027.

StatusHistory.note: IPB Properties (Bosa) advanced The Motley, a multi-tower residential community on the former Dairy Farmers plant in Midtown Nashville.
The Motley officially started construction on its 26-story Phase 1 tower in Midtown Nashville.

Title: Flats Flagler Gateway
Description: A 12-story, 215-unit Class A Opportunity Zone apartment development in Fort Lauderdale's Flagler Village blending market-rate and workforce housing, which broke ground in April 2026.
DescriptionLong: Flats Flagler Gateway is a 12-story, 215-unit Class A multifamily development by CEDARst Companies at 745 N Andrews Avenue in Fort Lauderdale's Flagler Village, sitting in a federally designated Opportunity Zone. Spanning about 350,000 square feet on 1.46 acres and capitalized at roughly $100 million, it mixes market-rate and workforce studio through three-bedroom units with structured parking, a fitness center, resort-style pool and coworking space. CEDARst held its groundbreaking on April 28, 2026, with construction starting in May 2026 and completion targeted for mid-2028.

StatusHistory.note: CEDARst Companies unveiled Flats Flagler Gateway, a 215-unit Opportunity Zone multifamily tower in Fort Lauderdale's Flagler Village.
$68.5M construction loan (North River Partners + Amzak Capital), ~$100M total capitalization

Title: Soren
Description: A downtown Salt Lake City mixed-use development anchored by Soren, a 28-story, 367-unit residential tower paired with a 10-story, 209-room hotel, with site excavation underway since early 2026.
DescriptionLong: Soren is the residential centerpiece of Domain Companies' mixed-use development at 370 South West Temple, at the corner of West Temple and 400 South in downtown Salt Lake City. Designed by Solomon Cordwell Buenz, the 278-foot, 28-story tower will hold 367 apartments above roughly 6,400 square feet of retail, alongside a separate 129-foot, 10-story hotel of 209 rooms with a rooftop bar and ground-floor restaurant. Estimated costs run about $164 million for the tower and $109 million for the hotel. Excavation and site-preparation work got underway in February 2026, with more extensive excavation to follow pending permit approval; completion is anticipated in late 2028 or early 2029.

StatusHistory.note: Domain Companies unveiled a mixed-use residential tower + hotel at 370 South West Temple in downtown Salt Lake City.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: 367-unit residential tower + separate 209-key hotel + ground-floor retail per Building Salt Lake.

Title: 711 North Pearl
Description: A 35-story, 370-unit luxury apartment tower under construction on the edge of the Dallas Arts District, developed by Switzerland's Empira Group with delivery targeted for 2026.
DescriptionLong: 711 North Pearl is a 35-story residential tower rising at 711 North Pearl Street on the edge of the Dallas Arts District and Central Business District. Developed by Switzerland-based Empira Group and designed by GFF Architects, the tower delivers 370 market-rate apartments — studios through three-bedrooms, each with a private balcony — plus eight exclusive penthouses, about 9,000 square feet of ground-floor retail and a seven-level, 515-space parking garage. A ninth-floor amenity level offers a pool with city views, a fitness center, co-working space, an art makers studio and a resident lounge, with a landscaped streetscape connecting to nearby cultural venues and Klyde Warren Park. The tower broke ground in 2024 with delivery targeted for 2026.

StatusHistory.note: Swiss firm Empira Group unveiled a 35-story, 370-unit residential tower at 711 N. Pearl St. in downtown Dallas.
Located in the downtown Dallas core near the Arts District.

Title: Four Seasons Hotel & Residences Charleston
Description: South Carolina's first Four Seasons — a three-building development in Charleston's Historic District replacing a former Days Inn, with a 139-room hotel and 36 branded residences, targeted for 2028.
DescriptionLong: The Four Seasons Hotel & Residences Charleston will be South Carolina's first Four Seasons property, rising on a 1.9-acre former Days Inn site at 155 Meeting Street in Charleston's Historic District, near Horlbeck Alley and the City Market. Developed by Tampa-based Strategic Property Partners, the project comprises three buildings reaching as high as eight stories, designed by Morris Adjmi Architects (who also lead the residences' interiors), with hotel interiors by Lark Design Studios and landscape architecture by Hollander Design. The 139-room hotel will offer four restaurants and bars, an outdoor pool with private cabanas, a full-service spa, a fitness center, curated retail and 7,000 square feet of event space. Alongside it, 36 branded residences range from two to five bedrooms (roughly 2,000–3,700 square feet), priced from $5 million, with Four Seasons services including in-residence dining and concierge. Completion is targeted for 2028.

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons and Strategic Property Partners unveiled a new-build hotel (139 keys) + 36 branded residences in Charleston's historic district.
Delivery slipped to 2029 (was 2028); demo of Days Inn pushed to early 2026, sales office Q1 2026. 139 keys per Post & Courier.
Delivery slipped to 2029 (was 2028); demo of Days Inn pushed to early 2026, sales office Q1 2026. 139 keys per Post & Courier.

Title: The Lily River Oaks
Description: A $270M mixed-use development in Upper Kirby anchored by The Lily River Oaks, a 38-story, 331-residence luxury rental tower paired with a 10-story trophy office building, which broke ground April 2026.
DescriptionLong: The Lily River Oaks is a 38-story luxury rental tower at the heart of Southern Land Company's $270 million mixed-use development at 2811 Kirby Drive in Houston's Upper Kirby district, near River Oaks. Designed by Solomon Cordwell Buenz, The Lily delivers 331 rental residences — including 18 penthouses — with rooftop indoor and outdoor amenity spaces, a seventh-floor garden deck, a residential spa and guest suites. It is connected by a shared six-level parking podium to 2811 Kirby, a 10-story building with roughly 107,000 square feet of Class AA office space, alongside about 15,000 square feet of ground-level restaurant and retail space. The 953,000-square-foot project broke ground on April 15, 2026, with Southern Land targeting record office and apartment rents, and completion is slated for 2028.

StatusHistory.note: Southern Land Co. broke ground on The Lily, a 38-story, 331-unit tower paired with a 10-story office building on Kirby Drive in Upper Kirby.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: 331-unit residential high-rise + 10-story Class AA office + ground-floor restaurant/retail on a shared podium.
Southern Land broke ground on The Lily River Oaks (38-story, 331 residences) in Upper Kirby the week of Apr 15 2026; corrected start from 2026-04-21 which postdated the announcement

Title: First & Island
Description: A planned 35-story, 215-residence luxury condominium tower by Bosa Development on one of the last empty lots in downtown San Diego's Marina District, just south of Horizons Towers.
DescriptionLong: First & Island is a planned 35-story luxury condominium tower, rising roughly 415 feet with 215 residences, on a long-vacant parking-lot parcel at the corner of First Avenue and Island Avenue in downtown San Diego's Marina District, immediately south of Horizons Towers. Developed by Bosa Development and designed by Vancouver-based James KM Cheng Architects, the roughly 273,000-square-foot project sits at the convergence of the Marina District, East Village, Little Italy and Cortez Hill. Sales and permitting activity advanced through late 2025, with construction anticipated to begin in the 2026–2027 window and delivery targeted around 2027; amenities, floor plans and pricing have not yet been released.

StatusHistory.note: Bosa's First & Island, a 35-story downtown San Diego tower, advanced through permitting; later repositioned from for-sale condos to ~211 rental apartments.

Title: The Launiu Ward Village
Description: A 40-story, 486-residence luxury tower that broke ground in October 2025 as the 11th tower in Howard Hughes' Ward Village, with front-row views over Kewalo Basin and Diamond Head.
DescriptionLong: The Launiu Ward Village is a 40-story luxury condominium tower, plus a six-story podium, that broke ground on October 21, 2025 at 928 Ward Avenue near the corner of Ala Moana Boulevard in Honolulu's Kakaako district. The 11th tower in Howard Hughes' master-planned Ward Village, it delivers 486 market-rate residences ranging from studios to three-bedrooms, roughly 440 to 1,677 square feet, with views over Kewalo Basin, the Pacific coastline, Diamond Head and the Koolau mountains. Designed by Miami-based Arquitectonica with interiors by ODADA and landscape architecture by VITA, the tower features approximately 78,000 square feet of resort-style amenities.

StatusHistory.note: Howard Hughes broke ground on The Launiu, Ward Village's 11th residential tower (40 stories, 486 units) in Kakaako.
Within Howard Hughes' Ward Village master-planned community in Kakaako.
Completion targeted for 2028 per Swinerton (GC) and Howard Hughes
Condo sales launched in 2025; ~67% under contract (324 of 486) by mid-2026 per Swinerton

Title: Apiary Hotel & Residences
Description: South Denver's debut extended-stay lifestyle hotel, a 175-suite Marriott Bonvoy property in Belleview Station paired with luxury Apiary Residences, opened in January 2026.
DescriptionLong: Apiary Hotel & Residences is a beehive-inspired, mixed-use hospitality property in Denver's Belleview Station district near the Denver Tech Center, which opened to guests on January 27, 2026. Part of the Marriott Bonvoy portfolio, the all-suite hotel offers 175 spacious suites with kitchenettes as South Denver's first extended-stay lifestyle hotel. Alongside it, Apiary Residences provides one- to three-bedroom homes — including thirteen penthouses — with a separate entrance and amenities such as co-working spaces, meeting rooms, a residential lounge and spa-caliber wellness areas. Community-facing spaces include two dining concepts, Keepers Cocktail Lounge and June Gap Market & Café, plus the 900-square-foot Nectar Room meeting space, with light-rail access to downtown. The project was developed by Stonebridge and Copford Capital Management with The Beck Group as architect and general contractor.

Title: Chapter SLC
Description: A 252-unit, 696-bed student housing development that broke ground April 2026 on the former OfficeMax site near the University of Utah, with completion targeted for June 2028.
DescriptionLong: Chapter SLC is a 252-unit, 696-bed student housing development that broke ground on April 9, 2026 at 900 East 400 South in Salt Lake City's East Central neighborhood, on the former OfficeMax site near the University of Utah. Developed by Cole West and CRG and designed by Lamar Johnson Collaborative, the project rises five wood-frame residential stories over 2.5 concrete parking levels, with a unit mix of 41 studios, 21 one-bedrooms, 64 two-bedrooms and 126 four-bedrooms, plus ground-floor commercial space, 170 parking stalls and bicycle parking. Amenities are pitched at students, including a soda shop, ski simulator, Pilates studio, library, jumbotron and rooftop pool. Completion is expected in June 2028.

StatusHistory.note: CRG and Cole West unveiled Chapter SLC, a student-focused apartment community at 410 S. 900 East near the University of Utah.
CRG/Cole West broke ground + fully capitalized Chapter SLC (251 units, 693 beds, 6 stories) per Mar 11 2026 press release; corrected start from 2026-04-09 and specs from 252u/5f

Title: Andia by Bosa
Description: A 40-story luxury condominium tower in downtown San Diego's East Village by Bosa Development — the city's first new for-sale high-rise in years, with nearly 400 residences.
DescriptionLong: Andia by Bosa is a 40-story luxury condominium tower at 1160 Ninth Avenue in downtown San Diego's East Village, developed by Bosa Development as the city's first new for-sale high-rise in roughly six years. Designed by Joseph Wong Design Associates (JWDA) with Chris Dikeakos Architects as architect of record and interiors by OTJ, the glass tower carries warm-toned accents across nearly 400 one- and two-bedroom residences. A 15,000-square-foot fifth-floor pool terrace with a 75-foot lap pool and a top-floor sky lounge and terrace headline an amenity-forward program aimed at downtown's luxury buyers.

StatusHistory.note: Bosa Development converted its full downtown San Diego block to for-sale condos, launching Andia (~399 residences, 40 stories).
Bosa Development converted its full downtown San Diego block to for-sale condos, launching Andia (~399 residences, 40 stories).
Andia is under construction (started 2024) with a public sales gallery open since June 2025 — record still showed "announced".
