Developed by Mitsui Fudosan with Hilton as operator, the hotel offers 197 generously sized guestrooms anchored by Waldorf Astoria's signature Peacock Alley and three restaurants. Above it sit the Waldorf Astoria Residences Tokyo Nihonbashi — the Asia-Pacific region's first Waldorf Astoria branded residences, with 71 homes on the tower's highest floors. Additional facilities include an indoor pool, spa, fitness center, banquet hall, and chapel.
Developer: Mitsui Fudosan, Nomura Real Estate Development

StatusHistory.note: Mitsui Fudosan and Hilton launched Waldorf Astoria Residences Tokyo Nihonbashi, APAC's first Waldorf Astoria residences
Nihonbashi 1-Chome Central District redevelopment (284m tower housing the Waldorf) began construction Dec 2021
Waldorf Astoria Residences sales launched Sept 2025; 71 units on floors 48-51, move-in fall 2027
Residences branding/sales launch (APAC's first Waldorf Astoria branded residences); hotel = 197 keys, residences = 71 units, fall 2027 opening

Title: Andaz Turks & Caicos at Grace Bay
Description: Hyatt's first Turks & Caicos resort and the debut Andaz in the Caribbean — a seven-storey beachfront hotel-and-residences retreat on 5.5 acres of Grace Bay with 59 rooms, 73 branded residences, multiple pools, three restaurants, and an indoor-outdoor garden spa.
DescriptionLong: Spread across 5.5 acres of the celebrated Grace Bay shoreline, Andaz Turks & Caicos at Grace Bay rises as a seven-storey, twin-building beachfront retreat pairing a boutique hotel with a collection of branded residences ranging from studios to penthouses. Guests move between multiple swimming pools overlooking the turquoise water, three restaurants and three bars, and an indoor-outdoor spa wrapped around a garden, complemented by a yoga pavilion, fitness studio, tennis, and watersports along one of the Caribbean's most famous beaches. Designed by RAD Architecture and Modus Operandi with locally inspired interiors — including penthouses by designer Francisco Jove — the property fuses contemporary island architecture with Andaz's signature residential, design-forward sensibility. A kids' activity club and full residential ownership with hotel services broaden its appeal to both vacationers and second-home buyers. As Hyatt's first hotel in Turks & Caicos and the debut of the Andaz brand in the Caribbean, it marks a notable luxury-lifestyle milestone for the region.

StatusHistory.note: Hyatt announced Andaz Turks & Caicos at Grace Bay, Andaz brand's Caribbean debut
Broke ground August 2023; construction at one floor every 45 days per Newsline TCI
Date correction: Hyatt announced Andaz Turks & Caicos at Grace Bay on Sept 25, 2018
Topping-off ceremony, delivered ahead of schedule per Hospitality Net
Now bookable, taking reservations for stays from June 1, 2027 per One Mile at a Time
Now bookable, taking reservations for stays from June 1, 2027 per One Mile at a Time
Vertical structure/roof completed significantly ahead of schedule

Title: The Dallas EDITION Hotel & Residences
Description: The EDITION brand's first Texas hotel — a 29-story, 214-key five-star hotel paired with 60 ultra-luxury branded residences, anchoring Kaizen's Chalk Hill development at the gateway to Uptown Dallas.
DescriptionLong: The Dallas EDITION Hotel and The Residences at The Dallas EDITION form the centerpiece of Chalk Hill, a mixed-use development anchoring the gateway to Uptown Dallas at 3031 N. Harwood Street, with direct access to the Katy Trail and the Dallas North Tollway. Developed by Kaizen Development Partners, the project marks the EDITION brand's first location in Texas, pairing a 214-key five-star hotel with 60 luxury residences, each featuring a private terrace. Architecture is by BOKA Powell with interiors by Bonetti/Kozerski and landscape design by SWA and SCDA. Residents and guests share a signature restaurant and bar, ballroom and meeting spaces, a cinematic pool deck, a full-service spa, and a holistic wellbeing center.

StatusHistory.note: EDITION's first Texas hotel & residences anchor Kaizen's Chalk Hill in Uptown Dallas; per Dallas Innovates, 2026-03-26
Kaizen unveils the Dallas EDITION Hotel & Residences as centerpiece of the $650M Chalk Hill development, Uptown Dallas

Title: The Nell New York
Description: The first hotel ever within landmarked Rockefeller Center — an intimate Midtown debut of Aspen's legendary Little Nell, set across nine floors of 10 Rockefeller Plaza with a destination restaurant, spa, and Relais & Châteaux–caliber service.
DescriptionLong: The Nell New York is the first hotel from Aspen Hospitality's celebrated Little Nell brand to open outside Aspen, set within the landmarked Rockefeller Center in Midtown Manhattan. Occupying the upper floors of 10 Rockefeller Plaza — above the studios where NBC's Today and Saturday Night Live are filmed — the property brings roughly 136 rooms and suites of five-star service to the heart of New York. Developed in partnership with Tishman Speyer and the Crown family, the hotel pairs refined residential-style accommodations with a lobby lounge, restaurant, fitness center, and spa, courting both leisure and business travelers. It marks a marquee adaptive reuse of vacant office space into luxury hospitality at one of Manhattan's most iconic addresses.
Developer: Aspen Hospitality, Tishman Speyer, The Georgetown Company

StatusHistory.note: Aspen Hospitality unveiled plans for The Little Nell / The Nell at 10 Rockefeller Plaza, NYC's first Rockefeller Center hotel
Aspen Hospitality unveiled plans for The Little Nell / The Nell at 10 Rockefeller Plaza, NYC's first Rockefeller Center hotel

Title: 310 Greenwich Street
Description: A proposed 72-story, 1,090-foot supertall residential tower expanding Independence Plaza in Tribeca, Manhattan, by Vornado Realty Trust and Stellar Management with architecture by Morris Adjmi.
DescriptionLong: 310 Greenwich Street is a proposed 72-story, 1,090-foot supertall residential tower in Tribeca, Manhattan, rising as an expansion of the historic Independence Plaza complex on Parcel 3C near Greenwich and Harrison Streets. Developed by Vornado Realty Trust and Stellar Management with architecture by Morris Adjmi Architects, the roughly 705,000-square-foot tower would deliver up to 976 residences, including a substantial affordable-housing component under New York City's Universal Affordability Preference. Its design pairs a gray-paneled masonry base of large square windows with an upper shaft of converging lattice columns that taper toward the crown, echoing Tribeca's industrial loft heritage while reshaping the Lower Manhattan skyline.

StatusHistory.note: Vornado Realty Trust & Stellar Management revealed plans for 1,090-ft, 72-story, 976-unit supertall at Independence Plaza (Parcel 3C); EAS filed with DCP. Per The Real Deal, Commercial Observer, NY YIMBY, May 2026.
Vornado + Stellar filed plans/EAS for a 1,090-ft, 72-story, 976-unit supertall expanding Independence Plaza in Tribeca per NY YIMBY, TRD, Commercial Observer.

Title: The St. Regis Nashville
Description: A 46-story contemporary Art Deco tower by Turnberry on downtown Nashville's Demonbreun Street, pairing a St. Regis hotel with branded residences and condominiums behind a "tower in a garden" facade of tiered plantings.
DescriptionLong: The St. Regis Nashville and The Residences at The St. Regis Nashville is a 46-story luxury hotel and condominium tower planned at 805 Demonbreun Street in downtown Nashville, anchoring Turnberry's "Marriott block" beside the developer's JW Marriott. Developed and operated by Turnberry Associates, the project pairs a St. Regis hotel of roughly 179 keys with a collection of branded furnished residences and condominium homes. Designed by architect Morris Adjmi with Smallwood, Reynolds, Stewart, Stewart as architect of record and interiors by Meyer Davis, the tower is planned with fine dining, a lobby bar, a full-service spa, and separate amenity decks for hotel guests and residents.
Architect: Morris Adjmi Architects, Smallwood Reynolds Stewart Stewart

StatusHistory.note: Turnberry to develop St. Regis Nashville & Residences at 805 Demonbreun, Morris Adjmi design; per Turnberry/PRNewswire, 2024-01-23
Never broke ground; NBJ (May 2026) reports no site progress since 2024 MDHA approval — correct over-stated coming-soon back to announced (original reveal Jan 2024)
46-story tower at 805 Demonbreun St; ~179 hotel keys, 141 condos + 40 furnished branded residences

Title: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Caye Chapel
Description: Four Seasons' first private-island resort and residences, set on the 280-acre Caye Chapel off the coast of Belize with overwater bungalows, a reversible golf course, and oceanfront estates.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Caye Chapel is a private-island luxury development on Caye Chapel, a roughly 265-acre island off the coast of Belize near Ambergris Caye. Developed by a consortium of Mexico-based real-estate groups led by Thor Urbana with Inmobilia and GFA Grupo Inmobiliario, the resort blends a Four Seasons hotel of around 100 rooms, suites, and overwater bungalows with a collection of branded private residences and estate lots. Signature amenities include two beach clubs, a cenote-inspired spa, and a 10-hole reversible championship golf course designed by Greg Norman, positioning Caye Chapel among the Caribbean's most ambitious private-island resorts.

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons broke ground on Caye Chapel Resort and Residences Sept 2, 2023; US$200M project by Thor Urbana / Inmobilia / GFA
Four Seasons announced Resort and Residences Caye Chapel, its first private island in the Western Hemisphere
Four Seasons announced Resort and Residences Caye Chapel, its first private island in the Western Hemisphere
Four Seasons broke ground on the private-island Caye Chapel resort and residences, ~$200M initial investment

Title: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Deer Valley
Description: A ski-in/ski-out Four Seasons resort and branded residences in Deer Valley's new East Village, pairing slope-side guestrooms with fully serviced mountain homes by ODA Architecture.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Deer Valley is a luxury alpine development at the base of Deer Valley Resort's new East Village in Park City, Utah — billed as the first major luxury alpine village built in North America in over four decades. Developed by Extell Development Company with design by ODA Architecture and operated by Four Seasons, it combines 134 guest rooms and suites with 123 fully serviced private residences ranging from one to six bedrooms. The ski-in/ski-out resort anchors the broader East Village expansion that is set to roughly double the footprint of Deer Valley.

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons and Extell announced Resort and Residences at Deer Valley East Village, Park City
Construction began spring 2025 per TownLift; completion expected 2028
Extell closed $600M construction loan from JVP Management, believed largest hotel/condo construction loan in Utah history

Title: Pendry Barbados
Description: Pendry's first Caribbean resort and residences on the Platinum Coast of Barbados, pairing oceanfront guestrooms with branded homes around a private marina and the brand's debut Pendry Yacht Club.
DescriptionLong: Pendry Barbados is a luxury beachfront resort and branded-residence community on the Platinum Coast of Saint Peter, Barbados, marking Pendry Hotels & Resorts' first international destination. Developed on the grounds of the former Port Ferdinand Luxury Marina with architecture by Robert Glazier, the property pairs roughly 80 oceanfront guestrooms and suites with 46 whole-ownership Pendry Residences. Amenities include a marina, multiple dining concepts, Spa Pendry, a fitness center, and expansive event spaces, alongside family offerings such as the Paintbox Children's Club and Compass Sports — bringing Montage International's contemporary luxury flag to the Caribbean.

StatusHistory.note: Pendry unveils first international resort & residences in St. Peter, Barbados, reimagining Port Ferdinand marina; per Hotel News Resource, 2023-04-19
Pendry Barbados topped off ahead of 2027 opening; ~80 oceanfront keys
Pendry Barbados topped off ahead of 2027 opening; ~80 oceanfront keys

Title: Six Senses Myoko
Description: A Kengo Kuma–designed mountain resort and branded residences at the foot of Mount Myoko in the Suginohara ski area, blending Six Senses wellness with private onsen and year-round alpine experiences.
DescriptionLong: Six Senses Myoko is a slopeside luxury hotel and branded-residence development planned for the Suginohara slopes of Mt. Myoko in Niigata Prefecture, one of Japan's most storied and snow-rich ski regions. Operated under IHG's Six Senses brand for developer Patience Capital Group, the resort pairs a 57-key alpine hotel with 21 private branded residences, all designed by acclaimed Japanese architect Kengo Kuma in collaboration with Kume Sekkei. The wellness-focused retreat draws on Six Senses' signature sustainability and rejuvenation ethos, weaving natural timber, onsen hot-spring bathing and an expansive spa into a contemporary mountain setting framed by Myoko's legendary powder snowfall and long, scenic ski runs. Within reach of Tokyo by bullet train, Six Senses Myoko is poised to become a landmark anchor for the wider redevelopment of this historic Japanese mountain destination.
Developer: Patience Capital Group, IHG Hotels & Resorts

StatusHistory.note: Six Senses Myoko announced — IHG/Six Senses management agreement with Patience Capital Group for a slopeside 57-key hotel + 21 branded residences at Mt. Myoko, Kengo Kuma design
Six Senses Myoko = 57 hotel keys + 21 branded residences, at Suginohara/Myoko Kogen (per IHG/Six Senses signing)

Title: Raffles Tokyo
Description: Raffles' first hotel in Japan, occupying the upper floors of a redeveloped landmark tower in Tokyo's Hamamatsuchō district with sweeping views toward Tokyo Bay and the city skyline.
DescriptionLong: Raffles Tokyo is the storied Raffles brand's first hotel in Japan, occupying the upper floors of the landmark World Trade Center Building redevelopment in Tokyo's Hamamatsuchō district in Minato. Operated by Accor and designed by Melbourne studio Layan, the hotel offers 130 guestrooms and suites with sweeping views toward Tokyo Tower, Rainbow Bridge, and Tokyo Bay. Facilities include six food-and-beverage outlets, a spa, swimming pool, bathhouse, fitness studio, and more than 12,000 square feet of meeting space, bringing Raffles' legendary service to the heart of the Japanese capital.
Developer: World Trade Center Building, Inc., Tokyo Tatemono

StatusHistory.note: Raffles to debut first Tokyo hotel in 2028 atop redeveloped World Trade Center, Hamamatsucho; per Hotel News Resource, 2024-07-22
Raffles' first Japan hotel atop the World Trade Center Building redevelopment; 130 rooms incl. 27 suites

Title: 14501 W. Dixie Highway
Description: A proposed 235,065-square-foot mixed-use redevelopment on 2.66 acres in the Arch Creek Estates neighborhood near North Miami, with three buildings — a nine-story parking and commercial structure linked by sky bridges to two six-story residential and retail buildings — containing 110 condominium units, 25,250 square feet of commercial space, and a rooftop pool deck.
DescriptionLong: 14501 W. Dixie Highway is a proposed nine-story mixed-use building from RG Realty Investments on a 2.66-acre assemblage along West Dixie Highway at NE 145th Street in the Biscayne Gardens area of unincorporated Miami-Dade County. Designed by The Gado Group, it pairs 110 one- to three-bedroom apartments behind floor-to-ceiling impact glass over roughly 25,250 square feet of retail, distinguished by interlocking structures linked by two sky bridges and crowned by a rooftop pool and lounge. The project is advancing through standard zoning rather than the Live Local Act.

StatusHistory.note: RG Realty Investments and The Gado Group file a Miami-Dade pre-application for a 9-story, 110-unit mixed-use building at 14501 W. Dixie Highway (Biscayne Gardens area)
RG Realty Investments and The Gado Group file a Miami-Dade pre-application for a 9-story, 110-unit mixed-use building at 14501 W. Dixie Highway (Biscayne Gardens area)

Title: PGA Office Center
Description: An 86-acre corporate campus at I-95 and PGA Boulevard, anchored by twin six-story office buildings totaling about 515,000 square feet, designed to accommodate 2,000 employees with 150 EV charging stations, a rooftop solar PV array, and Category 5 hurricane resilience.
DescriptionLong: This 86-acre corporate campus at I-95 and PGA Boulevard in Palm Beach Gardens comprises twin six-story office buildings — Phase 1 at 266,000 square feet (opened 2022) and Phase 2 at 249,000 square feet (approved December 2022) — connected by a glass-enclosed walkway and supported by twin three-story parking garages with 150 EV charging stations and a rooftop solar photovoltaic array. The site includes a helistop, a 3,000-gallon emergency fuel station for company vehicles during storms, native plantings across 90 percent of the landscape, and is engineered to withstand Category 5 hurricanes for operational continuity. The original 2013 zoning approval cleared up to one million square feet of office development across the site, leaving roughly half a million square feet of future capacity beyond the two completed phases — enough for another 2,000 employees.

StatusHistory.note: Palm Beach Gardens City Council approves Phase 1: 266,000 sqft six-story FPL office building + garage on 86-acre I-95/PGA Blvd campus
First FPL office building began vertical construction in 2020 (opened 2023); second twin building still in planning

Title: The Monarch San Antonio
Description: A $185 million, 17-story luxury hotel in San Antonio's Hemisfair district, part of Hilton's Curio Collection, steps from the River Walk and the Alamo.
DescriptionLong: The Monarch is a $185 million, 17-story luxury hotel anchoring San Antonio's reimagined Hemisfair district, steps from the River Walk and the Alamo and overlooking Hemisfair's Civic Park. Developed by Zachry Hospitality as part of Hilton's Curio Collection, the tower blends contemporary design with a sense of place drawn from the city's history and the monarch-butterfly migration that gives it its name. The signature rooftop restaurant, Aleteo, is wrapped in butterfly-wing glass with sweeping views across downtown, joined by a lively lobby bar, refined guest rooms and suites, and event and gathering spaces. Its location at the heart of the Hemisfair revitalization places guests within walking distance of the park, the convention district and the river's cultural corridor.

Title: Chalet Alpina
Description: An ultra-luxury resort and 32 residences at the base of Aspen Mountain, anchoring the revival of the historic Lift One corridor with a new lift, ski museum and public park.
DescriptionLong: Chalet Alpina is a ski-in/ski-out luxury resort and residences at the original Lift One base on the west side of Aspen Mountain, spanning roughly two and a half blocks along South Aspen Street. Developed by Aspen-based HayMax Capital with Los Angeles resort developer Irongate Group, it combines six whole-ownership Mountain Homes and 26 fractional Resort Residences with a hotel, restaurants and retail, a new telemix lift that brings skiers closer to downtown, and the restoration of the historic Skiers' Chalet buildings — one reborn as a restaurant, the other as an Aspen ski-history museum. Architecture is by Guerin Glass Architects with interiors by Martin Brudnizki Design Studio, above a rooftop pool, a wellness spa, and underground parking.
Architect: Guerin Glass Architects, Martin Brudnizki Design Studio

StatusHistory.note: The Lift One base project at Aspen Mountain is rebranded Chalet Alpina by HayMax Capital and Irongate Group — a ski-in/ski-out resort and residences with a new telemix lift and restored historic Skiers' Chalet buildings
Completion expected 2029; above-ground work 2026–2028 (Resident, corroborated by AG Aspen)

Title: Six Senses Telluride
Description: Six Senses' Mountain West debut, a 77-room wellness resort with 24 branded residences set above Telluride in Mountain Village, framed by the 14,000-foot peaks of the San Juans.
DescriptionLong: Six Senses Telluride is a wellness-driven luxury resort and branded-residence project in Mountain Village, above Telluride, framed by the 14,000-foot peaks of the San Juan Mountains. Developed by the Vault Home Collection under the Tiara Telluride entity and operated under IHG's Six Senses brand, it pairs a 77-key resort with 24 two-to-five-bedroom residences. Cedar-and-stone architecture with interiors by Rose Ink Workshop targets LEED Silver and features a Six Senses spa, a rooftop pool, omakase and specialty dining, and a speakeasy bar — IHG's first Six Senses resort and residences in North America's Mountain West.

StatusHistory.note: IHG unveils Six Senses Telluride in Mountain Village — a 77-key resort plus 24 branded residences, IHG's first Six Senses in North America's Mountain West

Title: The Birdsall, Auberge Resorts Collection
Description: Houston's first Auberge property — a 105-room boutique hotel with 44 private residences and a members' club, anchoring The RO, a 17-acre walkable district near River Oaks.
DescriptionLong: The Birdsall is a 34-story luxury hotel-and-residences tower in Houston's River Oaks, anchoring Transwestern Development's 'The RO' enclave and marking Auberge Resorts Collection's first property in Houston. Designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox with hotel interiors by Roman and Williams and residential interiors by Dillon Kyle Architects, it pairs a 105-key boutique hotel with 44 private residences and a members' club. Named for celebrated River Oaks architect Birdsall Briscoe, the reclaimed-brick tower offers a 70-foot pool, a full-service spa, and residences crowned by private terraces of more than 500 square feet.

StatusHistory.note: Construction underway on The Birdsall as The RO Phase 2 breaks ground in River Oaks
Construction underway on The Birdsall as The RO Phase 2 breaks ground in River Oaks
34-story tower, 105-key Auberge hotel beneath 44 residences, in River Oaks' "The RO" district (PaperCity)

Title: Rodeo Dunes
Description: Dream Golf's new public-access destination resort on Colorado's high plains — more than 2,000 acres of natural sand with a Coore & Crenshaw course and up to six courses planned.
DescriptionLong: Rodeo Dunes is a destination golf resort by Dream Golf — the Keiser family behind Bandon Dunes and Sand Valley — set on natural sand dunes near Roggen on Colorado's eastern plains, about an hour northeast of Denver. Its debut walking-only 18-hole course is designed by Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw, with a second 18 by Jim Craig and room for up to six full courses plus a short course and putting green. Caddie-supported and remote, the links-style property is positioned as Colorado's premier big-sky golf retreat.

StatusHistory.note: Dream Golf (Keiser family) announces Rodeo Dunes on Colorado's eastern plains near Roggen — debut walking-only course by Coore & Crenshaw, a second 18 by Jim Craig
Construction begins on the first golf course (Coore & Crenshaw) at Rodeo Dunes
Construction begins on the first golf course (Coore & Crenshaw) at Rodeo Dunes

Title: Rosewood Residences Turtle Creek
Description: Rosewood's standalone branded condominium in Dallas — a 17-story Beaux-Arts-inspired tower of roughly 33 large residences along Turtle Creek and the Katy Trail.
DescriptionLong: Rosewood Residences Turtle Creek is a 17-story, 33-residence luxury condominium tower at 3555 Dickason Avenue in Dallas's Turtle Creek corridor, a block from the storied Rosewood Mansion. Developed by One Turtle Creek LP under the Rosewood brand — Rosewood's first standalone branded residences in Texas — it features Beaux-Arts architecture by Lucien Lagrange and interiors by AD100 designer Dan Fink. Residences range from roughly 2,000 to 7,000 square feet above amenities including a rooftop infinity pool, landscaped gardens, a fitness center, library, private dining, concierge and valet service, and a dedicated pet spa.

StatusHistory.note: Rosewood plans a 17-story Rosewood Residences Turtle Creek in Dallas, developed by One Turtle Creek LP under the Rosewood brand — Beaux-Arts design by Lucien Lagrange
Construction commences on Rosewood Residences Turtle Creek
Construction commences on Rosewood Residences Turtle Creek
17-story tower topped out at full 237 ft (CandysDirt/Mann Report/Multi-Housing News)
$97.5M construction loan closed (University Place Asset Mgmt + Axonic Capital), per CandysDirt

Title: Canyon Ranch Austin
Description: A roughly $121 million, 223,000-square-foot Canyon Ranch destination wellness resort in the Texas Hill Country near Lake Travis, the brand's first new build in years.
DescriptionLong: Canyon Ranch Austin is a ground-up wellness resort set on more than 600 acres of Texas Hill Country ranchland in Spicewood, overlooking Lake Travis west of Austin. Developed by Canyon Ranch with residences designed by Texas firm Lake Flato, it is anchored by the largest spa in Texas — 40,000 square feet with 37 treatment rooms — alongside a 27,000-square-foot fitness barn, a longevity and medical center, multiple pools, miles of trails, an equestrian program, and lakefront watersports. Dining centers on the resort's signature restaurant Estella, with programming spanning brain health, sleep, metabolic medicine, and a first-of-its-kind women's wellness practice.

StatusHistory.note: Canyon Ranch unveils Canyon Ranch Austin, a 600+-acre Lake Travis wellness resort in Spicewood with capital partner VICI Properties
Canyon Ranch unveils Canyon Ranch Austin, a 600+-acre Lake Travis wellness resort in Spicewood with capital partner VICI Properties
Canyon Ranch unveils Canyon Ranch Austin, a 600+-acre Lake Travis wellness resort in Spicewood with capital partner VICI Properties
Canyon Ranch unveils Canyon Ranch Austin, a 600+-acre Lake Travis wellness resort in Spicewood with capital partner VICI Properties
Reservations opened 6/16/2026; 141 guest rooms confirmed (PR Newswire, official Canyon Ranch release)

Title: Virgin Hotels Denver
Description: Virgin Hotels' first Colorado property, a 241-room hotel anchoring the 41-acre Fox Park district on the site of the former Denver Post printing plant.
DescriptionLong: Virgin Hotels Denver is the brand's first Colorado property and a signature anchor of Fox Park, the 41-acre redevelopment of the former Denver Post printing plant in the Globeville district along Fox Street. Designed by Tryba Architects with Saunders Construction and developed by Vita alongside Trammell Crow and the World Trade Center Denver campus, the new-build offers Chambers-style guestrooms, an 8,000-square-foot spa, a rooftop lounge with skyline views, and Virgin's signature Commons Club, Shag Room and Funny Library Coffee Shop.

StatusHistory.note: Virgin Hotels Denver announced at the Fox Park groundbreaking — Virgin's first Colorado hotel anchoring the 41-acre Globeville redevelopment of the former Denver Post printing plant

Title: Dallas Proper Hotel and Residences
Description: A 34-story Proper-branded luxury tower at the gateway to Uptown Dallas, pairing a 200-room hotel with roughly 80 branded residences designed by SOM with interiors by Kelly Wearstler.
DescriptionLong: Dallas Proper Hotel & Residences is a 34-story luxury tower anchoring Lincoln Property Company's four-acre mixed-use development at 2500 Cedar Springs Road in Uptown Dallas, near the Crescent and the Katy Trail. Operated under Proper Hospitality's Proper Hotels brand, it pairs roughly 200 guestrooms with about 80 branded residences, with architecture by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill and interiors by Kelly Wearstler. Amenities include a 15,000-square-foot wellness and padel club, multiple restaurants and bars, a rooftop pool deck, and a members-only Proper Club — positioned as Proper's second Texas hotel.

StatusHistory.note: Proper Hospitality + Lincoln Property unveil 34-story Dallas Proper: 200-key hotel + 80 SOM-designed residences at 2500 Cedar Springs Rd, Uptown

Title: The East Bend
Description: A 47-acre redevelopment of a former East Bank scrapyard site into a mixed-use Nashville neighborhood on the Cumberland River, planned with residential, retail, public green space, and cultural amenities — anchored by what would be Nashville's first dedicated children's museum.
DescriptionLong: The East Bend is a multi-decade, mixed-use redevelopment of a 47-acre former PSC Metals scrapyard on Nashville's East Bank, fronting the Cumberland River just south of Nissan Stadium. Led by a David Byerley–directed investor group, the master plan reimagines the long-industrial riverfront as a walkable, family-oriented neighborhood of residences, neighborhood retail, parks and greenways, and cultural and entertainment space — with zoning that bars stadiums and arenas and caps hotels, signaling a residential-and-culture vision rather than an entertainment district. A dedicated children's museum is under discussion as a potential cultural anchor.
ProjectType: Mixed-Use, Residences, Retail, Park, Cultural, Museum, Entertainment

StatusHistory.note: 47-acre former PSC Metals riverfront scrapyard on Nashville's East Bank sold for $245M to a David Byerley–led group (TEB LLC) for redevelopment as The East Bend, a multi-decade mixed-use neighborhood

Title: Ambergris Cay
Description: A 1,100-acre private island all-inclusive resort 600 miles south of Miami in the Turks and Caicos, with 29 bungalows and 11 villas (3-11 bedrooms), each with a private heated plunge pool, an on-island international airport with a 5,700-foot runway, and full-service amenities including a Club House, spa, fitness pavilion, tennis courts, and the Calico restaurant and bar.
DescriptionLong: Ambergris Cay is a 1,100-acre private island all-inclusive resort on the southeast edge of the Turks and Caicos string, 600 miles south of Miami and tracing the world-famous Caicos Banks. Originally established as a private homeowner community and opened as a resort in 2019, the property now offers a luxury boutique hotel feel across vast and largely untouched terrain. Accommodation spans 29 bungalows — including the recently opened collection of 12 Waterside Bungalows with direct calm-water access, private heated plunge pools, and kayaks and paddleboards at the door — and 11 villas ranging from three to eleven bedrooms, all bookable on an all-inclusive basis with private heated plunge pools throughout. Amenities include a Club House, full-service spa, fitness pavilion, tennis, the Hangover Tiki Bar, and Calico restaurant for all-day dining with international chefs and made-to-order menus. Guests reach the island through Ambergris Cay International Airport, with one of the longest runways in the Caribbean at 5,700 feet — accommodating aircraft up to 92 feet in length for direct private jet arrival, with complimentary onward transfer flights from Providenciales or South Caicos for commercial guests. The resort was named a 2026 Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice Best of the Best winner, ranking among the Caribbean's top all-inclusive properties.

Title: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Dominican Republic at Tropicalia
Description: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Dominican Republic at Tropicalia is a low-density luxury resort and branded-residence community set along the sands of Playa Esmeralda in Miches, on the Dominican Republic's northeastern coast facing Samana Bay. Spread across roughly 60 acres of beachfront framed by tropical forest and lagoons, the property pairs a tropical-modernist resort with private oceanfront residences and beach villas. Guests enjoy a spa and wellness program, a collection of restaurants and bars, pools, and direct beach access, all designed around sustainability and a light environmental footprint. Natural materials and open-air living define a setting that aims to set a new standard for green luxury in the Caribbean.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Dominican Republic at Tropicalia is the brand's first property in the Dominican Republic, set on a 60-acre beachfront site along Playa Esmeralda on the southern shores of Samaná Bay in the country's lush northeast. Developed by Cisneros Real Estate as part of the family's long-envisioned Tropicalia community, the resort features tropical-modernist architecture by acclaimed Brazilian architect Isay Weinfeld, with 95 guest rooms and suites and 25 private residences of three and four bedrooms, all stepping toward the sand. Conceived around sustainability and a light environmental footprint, the low-slung, nature-led design pairs Four Seasons service with a protected stretch of Caribbean coastline, positioning the resort as a flagship for sophisticated, regenerative luxury travel in the region.

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons & Cisneros Real Estate announce 60-acre oceanfront resort + branded residences at Playa Esmeralda, Miches
Construction of the resort begins; Bouygues Construction named as general contractor

Title: Hoshinoya Niseko
Description: Hoshinoya Niseko is a luxury residence hotel perched at the top of the Hirafu slope in Kutchan, in Japan's powder-snow capital of Niseko on the island of Hokkaido. The first residence hotel under its esteemed Japanese brand, it offers ski-in/ski-out access to one of the area's largest ski resorts along with easy reach of the town's dining and nightlife. Guest rooms are designed as residential retreats, each with a separate kitchen and a living-dining room, while a top-floor terrace features an onsen pool framed by sweeping views toward Mount Yotei. The property blends traditional Japanese hospitality and minimalist design with the comforts of an alpine home, immersed in the surrounding nature year-round.
DescriptionLong: HOSHINOYA Niseko is the ninth property in Hoshino Resorts' flagship HOSHINOYA brand and its first to incorporate branded residences, planned for the upper slopes of the Hirafu ski area in Hokkaido's Niseko region. Designed by Azuma Architect & Associates — the practice behind every HOSHINOYA to date — the ski-in/ski-out retreat rises across seven levels in reinforced concrete and steel, with around 62 units that owners can enroll in a rental program operated as hotel accommodation by Hoshino Resorts. The building is crowned by a signature rooftop onsen framing sweeping views of the iconic, near-symmetrical cone of Mount Yotei, channeling the brand's hallmark blend of refined Japanese hospitality, contemplative design and a powerful connection to landscape. Positioned directly on Hirafu's coveted upper runs, it offers direct access to Niseko's celebrated powder in winter and a tranquil mountain base for green-season exploration, all wrapped in the quiet luxury and ryokan-rooted service that define the HOSHINOYA name.

StatusHistory.note: Hoshino Resorts announces HOSHINOYA Niseko — its 9th Hoshinoya and first residence hotel, atop Hirafu, Kutchan
Hoshino Resorts announces HOSHINOYA Niseko — its 9th Hoshinoya and first residence hotel, atop Hirafu, Kutchan
Construction commenced Oct 2024 as the project sold to a Tokyu Land-led consortium; completion revised to Feb 2029
Construction commenced Oct 2024 as the project sold to a Tokyu Land-led consortium; completion revised to Feb 2029
Floors = 7 (5 above grade + 2 below), reinforced concrete + steel, per construction reporting.

Title: Six Senses Niseko
Description: Six Senses Niseko is a wellness-driven alpine resort in the Hirafu area of Japan's renowned Niseko ski region on the northern island of Hokkaido. Set against the backdrop of Mount Yotei and some of the world's most reliable powder snow, the property pairs Six Senses' signature sustainability and wellness ethos with ski access. Guests can expect an extensive spa and wellness program, dining venues, and a traditional onsen bathhouse, with design that draws on natural materials and the surrounding landscape. The resort is positioned as a year-round mountain retreat combining winter sports in season with hiking, nature, and onsen-style relaxation.
DescriptionLong: Six Senses Niseko brings the wellness-driven luxury brand to Hokkaido's famed Niseko ski region, set in the Hirafu area at the foot of the legendary powder slopes beneath Mount Yotei. Operated under the Six Senses banner, part of IHG Hotels & Resorts, the intimate alpine retreat is planned with around 76 rooms and suites — including a penthouse with a generous terrace — alongside the brand's signature wellness offering anchored by a spa and a traditional Japanese onsen bathhouse. Two restaurants and a bar round out a hospitality experience that fuses Six Senses' sustainability ethos and barefoot-luxury sensibility with the deep snow, soaring birch forests and year-round mountain adventure that have made Niseko one of the world's most sought-after alpine destinations. Designed as a sanctuary for both winter skiers and green-season travelers, the resort offers ski access in the colder months and hiking, cycling and onsen rituals when the snow recedes.

StatusHistory.note: Six Senses announced for Niseko (Hirafu area), Hokkaido under IHG
Six Senses announced for Niseko (Hirafu area), Hokkaido under IHG

Title: Four Seasons Resort and Private Residences Okinawa
Description: Four Seasons Resort and Private Residences Okinawa is an expansive beachfront resort and branded-residence community along the western coast of Okinawa in Onna Village, set within one of the world's celebrated longevity Blue Zones. Stretching across more than 100 acres of coastline, the low-density plan weaves hotel accommodations, private villas, and oceanfront residences into a walkable seaside village. Contemporary Japanese architecture and natural materials harmonize with the subtropical setting, complemented by lush gardens and landscaped grounds. Amenities include restaurants and bars, a spa, fitness and pool facilities, a beach club, and private onsen baths, all anchored by a longevity- and wellness-focused lifestyle.
DescriptionLong: The Four Seasons Resort and Private Residences Okinawa is an expansive oceanfront luxury destination taking shape along the celebrated coastline of Onna Village on Japan's subtropical island of Okinawa. Developed by Malaysia's Berjaya Land in partnership with Four Seasons and designed by Kengo Kuma & Associates with landscape architecture by EDSA, the resort spreads across a site of more than 130,000 square meters facing the East China Sea. The program pairs roughly 127 guest rooms with about 124 branded condominium residences and 28 private villas, many featuring their own onsen baths and plunge pools, set amid lush gardens that step down toward white-sand beaches and clear turquoise water. Drawing on Kuma's signature use of natural materials and layered, landscape-attuned architecture, the resort is conceived as a wellness-oriented retreat blending Okinawan craft, indoor-outdoor living and panoramic ocean views, delivering Four Seasons service in one of Asia's most coveted island settings.

StatusHistory.note: Berjaya's US$1.12B Four Seasons Resort and Private Residences Okinawa announced in Onna Village
Berjaya's US$1.12B Four Seasons Resort and Private Residences Okinawa announced in Onna Village
Berjaya Land breaks ground on the resort and private residences; contractors Fujita Corporation and Seikou Okinawa Construction

Title: The Oberoi, Mayfair
Description: The Oberoi, Mayfair is a luxury boutique hotel in London's Mayfair district, set within a restored historic listed building of roughly 33,000 square feet on Brook Street in the heart of the West End. Forming the hospitality centerpiece of a larger mixed-use quarter that weaves together offices, residences, shops, restaurants, and revitalized public spaces, the hotel brings a celebrated standard of refined, understated service to one of the city's most prestigious addresses. Guests can expect intimately scaled accommodations, world-class personalized service, and meticulous attention to detail throughout. Its corner setting makes it a polished new gateway into Mayfair's storied streetscape.
DescriptionLong: The Oberoi, Mayfair marks the celebrated Indian luxury group Oberoi Hotels & Resorts' first hotel in the United Kingdom, set within Grosvenor's transformative South Molton development in the heart of Mayfair. Designed by Hopkins Architects, the wider scheme reweaves a cluster of blocks between Davies Street, Brook Street and South Molton Lane into a pedestrian-friendly mixed-use quarter of shops, restaurants, workspace and homes, anchored by a new luxury hotel beside the Bond Street Elizabeth line entrance. For Oberoi, the London debut brings the brand's renowned, deeply personal service and richly detailed interiors to one of the world's most prestigious retail and residential districts, with elegant guest rooms and suites, signature dining and wellness spaces woven into a sustainable, heritage-sensitive reinvention of the streetscape. It positions Oberoi at the center of Mayfair's luxury landscape, steps from Bond Street's flagship boutiques and the green expanses of Hyde Park and Grosvenor Square.

StatusHistory.note: Oberoi announces its first UK hotel at 40-46 Brook Street, Mayfair, within Grosvenor's South Molton scheme
Oberoi announces its first UK hotel at 40-46 Brook Street, Mayfair, within Grosvenor's South Molton scheme
On-site construction work began November 2023 within Grosvenor's South Molton scheme

Title: Mandarin Oriental, Setouchi
Description: Mandarin Oriental, Setouchi is a waterfront urban resort on the harbor front of Takamatsu, the capital of Kagawa Prefecture and a gateway to Japan's scenic Seto Inland Sea. Overlooking calm, island-dotted waters, the hotel pairs refined Asian-inspired hospitality with a coastal sense of place and a mild year-round climate. Guests can expect elegant rooms and suites, a selection of restaurants and bars, and a comprehensive spa and wellness facility. Conceived as part of a multi-island collection, it serves as both a serene seaside retreat and a launch point for exploring the region's art islands and coastal culture.
DescriptionLong: Mandarin Oriental, Setouchi is the centerpiece of the group's ambitious expansion into Japan's Seto Inland Sea region — a 92-room urban resort set on the waterfront of Takamatsu, the harbor capital of Kagawa Prefecture and the gateway to the celebrated art islands of the Setouchi region. Conceived as part of a planned trio of boutique Mandarin Oriental properties scattered around the Inland Sea, the Takamatsu resort pairs the brand's signature service with a strong sense of place rooted in the region's maritime culture, contemporary-art scene and slow-travel ethos. Guests are positioned to explore Naoshima, Teshima and the other islands made famous by the Setouchi Triennale, returning to refined rooms and suites, destination dining and a spa, all oriented toward the calm, island-dotted horizon of the Inland Sea. The result is envisioned as a sophisticated coastal base that blends design-led luxury with the understated beauty and craft traditions of western Japan.

StatusHistory.note: Mandarin Oriental announces Setouchi resort trio; Takamatsu is a 92-key waterfront urban resort
Mandarin Oriental announces Setouchi resort trio; Takamatsu is a 92-key waterfront urban resort
Mandarin Oriental announces Setouchi resort trio; Takamatsu is a 92-key waterfront urban resort

Title: London Museum
Description: The London Museum is a free public museum in London's West Smithfield neighborhood, set within the painstakingly restored Victorian General Market halls. Across interconnected gallery experiences, it tells the sweeping story of the city through a vast collection of objects, from a contemporary data-driven entry hall to subterranean galleries reaching down toward Roman street level. A dramatic viewing window lets visitors watch trains pass beneath the galleries, blending working infrastructure with cultural display. The destination pairs immersive permanent collections with rotating programming and event spaces, anchoring a landmark of adaptive reuse in the heart of historic Smithfield.
DescriptionLong: The London Museum's new home is an ambitious adaptive reuse of the historic Smithfield market buildings at West Smithfield, bringing one of the world's great city museums back to the heart of the City of London. Led by Stanton Williams and Asif Khan with conservation architect Julian Harrap, the project rescues the long-vacant General Market and Poultry Market — soaring Victorian iron-and-glass halls — and converts them into luminous galleries, public gathering spaces and event venues. The design choreographs a journey from light-filled market halls down into dramatic sunken galleries set within the building's brick vaults, organizing the visitor experience around themes of the city's past, present and future. Restored domes, recovered original ironwork and a new public entrance reanimate a once-derelict corner of London, while the museum is conceived as a free, around-the-clock civic destination — open, porous and woven into the surrounding streets, markets and the new Elizabeth line connections nearby.
Architect: Stanton Williams, Asif Khan, Julian Harrap Architects

StatusHistory.note: Museum of London rebrands as the London Museum and confirms the restored Victorian General Market at West Smithfield as its new home

Title: Earls Court Development
Description: Earls Court Development is a landmark mixed-use regeneration in West London, transforming roughly 44 acres of former exhibition-center land into one of the capital's largest new urban quarters. The plan delivers around 4,000 new homes alongside some 2.5 million square feet of workspace, cultural venues, shops, and restaurants, all knitted together by roughly 20 acres of green space including a major new park. Built around a zero-carbon energy strategy with substantial biodiversity gains and a thousand new trees, the scheme pairs sustainability with a vibrant, walkable public realm and improved east-west connections. A tall landmark tower anchors a varied skyline across the phased neighborhood.
DescriptionLong: The Earls Court Development is one of London's most significant urban regeneration schemes — a multi-billion-pound transformation of the 40-acre former Earls Court exhibition centre site straddling Kensington and Chelsea and Hammersmith and Fulham. Led by the Earls Court Development Company with a masterplan team including Hawkins\Brown and Studio Egret West, the project reknits a long-dormant railway-severed site back into the city, organized around a generous new central park and a network of public squares, streets and east-west connections. The plans bring roughly 4,000 new homes, around 230,000 square metres of workspace, and three new cultural venues, alongside shops, restaurants and community space, with sustainability and biodiversity woven through the green spine at its heart. Conceived as a series of distinct character areas rather than a single megastructure, the development aims to create a vibrant, mixed-use west London district that reanimates the legacy of the storied Earls Court name.

StatusHistory.note: Updated 7.5M sq ft Earls Court masterplan submitted for planning; ~4,000 homes, ~2.5M sq ft workspace across ~44 acres
Updated 7.5M sq ft Earls Court masterplan submitted for planning; ~4,000 homes, ~2.5M sq ft workspace across ~44 acres
Updated 7.5M sq ft Earls Court masterplan submitted for planning; ~4,000 homes, ~2.5M sq ft workspace across ~44 acres
Over-stated as construction: full planning consent from both boroughs only granted Dec 2025; enabling works slated to begin 2026, ground not yet broken (Construction Enquirer)

Title: Canada Water Masterplan
Description: Canada Water Masterplan is a large-scale mixed-use regeneration on roughly 53 acres in the Canada Water district of southeast London, reshaping a former dockside area into a new town center. Delivered in phases, it brings thousands of new homes together with around 2.5 million square feet of workspace and about a million square feet of shops, leisure, and cultural space, organized around new streets, a town square, and a 3.5-acre public park. The scheme emphasizes a revitalized waterfront, a state-of-the-art leisure center, and generous public realm that reconnects the neighborhood to its historic docks. It is conceived as a vibrant, walkable destination blending living, working, and culture beside the water.
DescriptionLong: The Canada Water Masterplan is one of central London's largest regeneration projects — a 53-acre reshaping of the Rotherhithe peninsula into the capital's first new town centre in a generation, delivered as a joint venture between British Land and AustralianSuper alongside Southwark Council. Masterplanned around the existing dock and ecology, the scheme weaves together as many as 3,000 net-zero-carbon homes, around two million square feet of workspace, and a vibrant mix of shops, leisure, culture and community facilities, all knitted by generous public realm including a new town square, a fresh high street, and waterside parks. Early phases have already introduced a leisure centre and a new food-and-culture destination, with pedestrian-first streets, dockside promenades and biodiverse landscaping setting the tone for a walkable, mixed-income district. The ambition is a genuinely complete neighborhood — somewhere to live, work and gather — anchored by sustainability and the restored waters of Canada Dock.

StatusHistory.note: British Land / AustralianSuper Canada Water masterplan — phased 53-acre town-center regeneration in Rotherhithe
British Land / AustralianSuper Canada Water masterplan — phased 53-acre town-center regeneration in Rotherhithe
British Land / AustralianSuper Canada Water masterplan — phased 53-acre town-center regeneration in Rotherhithe
Plot A1 (35-storey 'The Founding' + Three Deal Porters workspace) topped out per British Land

Title: Ensign House
Description: Ensign House is a 56-story residential skyscraper on the Isle of Dogs in London's Docklands, rising roughly 755 feet to the tip of a slender, illuminated crowning spire that lights the Canary Wharf skyline at night. Inspired by Art Deco design in the tradition of New York's great towers, the bronze-toned building delivers around 500 apartments, from affordable homes to a crowning penthouse, above flexible ground-floor commercial space. Residents enjoy elevated amenities including a sky-high clubhouse lounge with sweeping views and a landscaped pocket park at the tower's base. Its sculptural silhouette is designed to be a defining new marker on the Docklands waterfront.
DescriptionLong: Ensign House is a soaring 56-storey residential landmark rising to roughly 225 metres at South Quay on the Isle of Dogs, steps from the Canary Wharf financial district. Designed by Maccreanor Lavington for developer Far East Consortium, the tower replaces a 1980s office block with an unmistakable silhouette: an Art Deco-influenced bronze-toned shaft drawing on the romance of early-twentieth-century skyscrapers, capped by a tapering, illuminated spire crown that reads dramatically across the East London skyline. Inside, the building delivers around 500 homes ranging from compact apartments to a crowning penthouse, served by residents' amenities and elevated views over the docks, the river and the City beyond. As one of the tallest residential buildings in the South Quay cluster, it adds a sculptural, period-inflected counterpoint to the glass towers of the surrounding Docklands.

StatusHistory.note: 56-story, ~755 ft Ensign House residential tower approved at 17 Admirals Way, South Quay (Isle of Dogs)
56-story, ~755 ft Ensign House residential tower approved at 17 Admirals Way, South Quay (Isle of Dogs)

Title: Cambridge House, Auberge Resorts Collection
Description: Cambridge House, Auberge Resorts Collection is a luxury hotel and members' club in London's Mayfair, occupying a meticulously restored Grade I-listed Palladian mansion overlooking Green Park at one of the city's most storied addresses. The hotel offers elegantly appointed guest rooms alongside a handful of private residences, all sharing a rich roster of amenities that includes a grand Georgian ballroom, a destination restaurant, characterful bars and lounges, and an intimate subterranean club. A dramatic double-level spa anchors the wellness experience with extensive hydrotherapy facilities, treatment rooms, and a heated swimming pool. Blending centuries of history with contemporary five-star hospitality, it brings a singular sense of place to the heart of the West End.
DescriptionLong: Cambridge House, Auberge Resorts Collection is the careful resurrection of one of Mayfair's grandest addresses — the Grade I-listed Georgian mansion at 94 Piccadilly long known as the In and Out Naval and Military Club, which once hosted royalty and the political elite. Restored and reimagined by PDP London for the Reuben Brothers, the property pairs meticulously conserved period interiors with a contemporary luxury hotel and private members' club. Signature moments include a restored Georgian ballroom, an open-air courtyard planted with jasmine, and a dramatic double-level spa anchored by two swimming pools, alongside intimate dining rooms, lounges and a collection of suites overlooking Green Park. The result is conceived as a discreet sanctuary in the heart of Mayfair, blending heritage craftsmanship with the residential warmth and personalized service that define the Auberge portfolio.

StatusHistory.note: Reuben Brothers appoint Auberge Resorts Collection to operate Cambridge House — a 102-key hotel at 94 Piccadilly (former In & Out club), Auberge's UK debut
Reuben Brothers appoint Auberge Resorts Collection to operate Cambridge House — a 102-key hotel at 94 Piccadilly (former In & Out club), Auberge's UK debut
Reuben Brothers appoint Auberge Resorts Collection to operate Cambridge House — a 102-key hotel at 94 Piccadilly (former In & Out club), Auberge's UK debut
Spring-2026 opening (was 2027-01); GM/MD appointed, fit-out near complete; 102 keys per The Caterer & Boutique Hotelier
Spring-2026 opening (was 2027-01); GM/MD appointed, fit-out near complete; 102 keys per The Caterer & Boutique Hotelier

Title: The Shepherd Mayfair
Description: The Shepherd Mayfair is an 82-room boutique hotel set in Shepherd Market, one of Mayfair's most storied corners in London's West End. Arranged over six floors on the site of a former mews hotel, it offers rooms and suites ranging from intimate retreats to expansive signature suites. Guests can move between three dining and drinking concepts: an all-day British brasserie, a relaxed cafe-style lounge, and a hidden late-night cocktail bar reached by a discreet staircase. An adjacent pair of reimagined Georgian townhouses houses a small collection of one- and two-bedroom private residences, extending the hotel's craftsmanship into long-stay living.
DescriptionLong: The Shepherd Mayfair is an 82-room boutique hotel set within the storied lanes of Shepherd Market, one of Mayfair's most characterful and village-like corners. Designed by the architecture practice Buckley Gray Yeoman, the roughly 33,600-square-foot property spans six floors and is conceived as a love letter to the neighborhood's bohemian heritage — pairing richly layered, residential-feeling interiors with a tightly curated sense of place. The hotel anchors itself with a trio of distinct food-and-drink destinations, including the Fayre brasserie and the intimate Teddy's cocktail bar, alongside generously appointed guest rooms and suites that trade grand scale for warmth, texture and craft. Founded by hotelier Timothy Shepherd, the project aims to deliver the feel of a private Mayfair address rather than a conventional luxury hotel — a discreet retreat moments from Green Park, Berkeley Square and Piccadilly.

StatusHistory.note: 82-room boutique hotel plus 16 private residences announced for Shepherd Market, Mayfair, on the former Park Lane Mews Hotel site
82-room boutique hotel plus 16 private residences announced for Shepherd Market, Mayfair, on the former Park Lane Mews Hotel site
82-room boutique hotel plus 16 private residences announced for Shepherd Market, Mayfair, on the former Park Lane Mews Hotel site
82-room boutique hotel plus 16 private residences announced for Shepherd Market, Mayfair, on the former Park Lane Mews Hotel site

Title: Blues Note Hotel
Description: A proposed 15-story, 191-room four-star hotel in downtown Memphis crowned by a guitar-shaped rooftop pool, paired with a nine-story, 65-unit condominium tower across from Robert Church Park and FedExForum.
DescriptionLong: The Blues Note Hotel is a proposed mixed-use development for downtown Memphis, planned for the southeast corner of Fourth Street and Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Avenue — directly across from Robert Church Park and catty-corner to FedExForum. Led by Forum Hotel and Hospitality Development with architecture by A2H, the centerpiece is a 15-story, 191-room four-star hotel topped by a signature guitar-shaped rooftop pool that nods to the city's musical heritage. The plan also calls for a nine-story residential building with 65 condominiums, a three-level parking structure, and a second-phase boutique hotel, knitting hospitality, for-sale housing, dining, and retail into one of downtown's most prominent corners at the edge of the Beale Street entertainment district.

StatusHistory.note: Blues Note Hotel Development files plans for a $50M+ mixed-use tower (with a guitar-shaped rooftop pool) across from FedExForum in downtown Memphis
Blues Note Hotel Development files plans for a $50M+ mixed-use tower (with a guitar-shaped rooftop pool) across from FedExForum in downtown Memphis

Title: The St. Regis Kapalua Bay Resort
Description: The St. Regis Kapalua Bay Resort is a luxury oceanfront resort on Maui's Kapalua coast in Hawaii, spread across roughly 25 acres along a celebrated stretch of shoreline. The property offers expansive multi-bedroom, ocean-view residences that blend residential space with full resort service. Amenities include multiple pools and a cascading lagoon pool, direct access to a sheltered bay, championship golf and tennis nearby, and a 40,000-square-foot spa with nineteen treatment rooms. With tens of thousands of square feet of indoor and outdoor event space, it pairs intimate seclusion with the polished hospitality of one of the world's most storied luxury brands.
DescriptionLong: The St. Regis Kapalua Bay Resort brings the St. Regis brand to Maui's storied northwest coast, transforming The Resort at Kapalua Bay into an ultra-luxury oceanfront retreat on roughly 25 acres above Kapalua Bay. Owned by Memphis-based Kemmons Wilson Hospitality Partners, the property offers 146 spacious, multi-bedroom residential-style accommodations framed by Pacific and outer-island views. A 40,000-square-foot spa with 19 treatment rooms anchors the wellness program, while a multi-tiered cascading lagoon pool, additional outdoor pools, and nearly 35,000 square feet of indoor-outdoor event space complete a resort built around one of Hawaii's most celebrated stretches of coastline — all delivered with signature St. Regis service and butler-led hospitality.

StatusHistory.note: Marriott signs agreement to bring St. Regis to The Resort at Kapalua Bay, Maui; conversion to St. Regis brand
Marriott signs agreement to bring St. Regis to The Resort at Kapalua Bay, Maui; conversion to St. Regis brand

Title: Andaz Heber Valley
Description: Andaz Heber Valley is a four-season mountain resort in Heber City, Utah, set within The Slope, a lifestyle community along the scenic Provo River. The destination combines a luxury lifestyle hotel with a collection of branded residences, including multistory townhome villas and condominiums. Wellness sits at the heart of the experience: residences feature private dry-heat saunas and rooftop hot tubs, complemented by an elevated pool deck, a next-generation fitness club, and a year-round tubing hill. Surrounded by skiing, fly fishing, mountain biking, hiking, golf, and boating, and threaded with bike paths and pedestrian connections, it offers a wellness-rooted basecamp in the Utah mountains.
DescriptionLong: Andaz Heber Valley is the Andaz brand's debut mountain resort and the centerpiece of The Slope — a new legacy alpine village taking shape along the Provo River in Utah's Heber Valley, minutes from Deer Valley's expanded ski terrain. Developed by Angstrom Development Group, the destination pairs an 85-room hotel with 202 fully furnished, hotel-branded residences comprising 140 townhome villas and 62 condominiums attached to the hotel. Interiors draw on the craft and warmth of mountain lodges in Japan and Italy, and every villa is crowned by a rooftop deck with a private dry-heat sauna and hot tub — billed as the only branded residences in Utah to offer in-home wellness suites of that kind. Residences are oriented to capture sweeping views, from sunsets behind Mount Timpanogos to vistas across the surrounding Wasatch peaks, with the resort's broader wellness, dining, and slope-adjacent amenities woven throughout the village.

StatusHistory.note: Hyatt announces Andaz Heber Valley — the brand's first mountain destination, within The Slope masterplan
Hyatt announces Andaz Heber Valley — the brand's first mountain destination, within The Slope masterplan
Construction began in fall 2025 at The Slope ahead of the brand announcement
Branded residences (villas and condominiums) launched for sale in spring 2026
Groundbreaking April 2026 (corrects earlier 2025 start) per Utah Business; Phase 1 villas deliver 2027, full project 2029.

Title: The Lowline Hotel
Description: The Lowline Hotel is a 197-room lifestyle hotel on Upper King Street in the heart of Charleston, South Carolina's historic district, born from the full reimagining of a former chain hotel. Drawing on the city's maritime heritage, interiors layer deep navy tones, oak finishes, and nautical motifs, with custom artwork in the guest rooms. The hotel centers on a garden-inspired indoor-outdoor restaurant and bar, a signature Lowcountry restaurant, a cafe and market, a rooftop taproom, and a golf simulator lounge. An indoor pool, fitness center, and roughly 8,000 square feet of flexible event space make it a social hub steps from Charleston's dining, shopping, and culture.
DescriptionLong: The Lowline is a 197-room lifestyle hotel from JdV by Hyatt set along King Street in downtown Charleston, created through a full reinvention of the former Hyatt Place Charleston Historic District by Birmingham-based Highline Hospitality Partners. Conceived to channel the creativity and cultural rhythm of the Lowcountry, the property pairs a Charleston-inspired indoor-outdoor bar and restaurant wrapped around a secluded garden with a coffee shop, an indoor pool, and a private dining room outfitted with a golf simulator. Nearly 8,000 square feet of flexible event space serves gatherings of all kinds, while a design rooted in local art and craft makes the hotel as much a neighborhood gathering place as a place to stay.

StatusHistory.note: Highline Hospitality Partners acquires the former Hyatt Place/Hyatt House at 560 King Street to convert into The Lowline Hotel (JdV by Hyatt)
Highline Hospitality Partners acquires the former Hyatt Place/Hyatt House at 560 King Street to convert into The Lowline Hotel (JdV by Hyatt)
Highline Hospitality Partners acquires the former Hyatt Place/Hyatt House at 560 King Street to convert into The Lowline Hotel (JdV by Hyatt)
Reservations opened for July 2026 stays
197 keys, Upper King St; reservations opened ahead of summer 2026 debut (conversion of former Hyatt Place), per ABC News 4.

Title: The Paramount
Description: Set to become Tennessee's tallest building, The Paramount is a 60-story luxury condominium tower on Church Street in downtown Nashville, holding just 140 residences from pieds-à-terre to full-floor penthouses.
DescriptionLong: The Paramount is a 60-story, roughly 750-foot luxury condominium tower in the heart of downtown Nashville, poised to become the tallest building in Tennessee. Developed by Nashville's Giarratana, the slender high-rise holds just 140 residences — an intentionally low density spanning pied-à-terre layouts to expansive half- and full-floor penthouses that crown the skyline. Floor-to-ceiling glass frames panoramic views across the Cumberland River valley and the downtown core, while a curated amenity program and concierge service position the tower at the very top of the city's for-sale market. A dedicated experience gallery showcases full-scale kitchen and bath mockups alongside interior finish samples, underscoring the project's focus on bespoke, design-forward living at altitude.

StatusHistory.note: Giarratana closes a $340M construction loan (Benefit Street Partners) plus ~$120M equity
Reached roughly the 10th story with the first glass curtain-wall panel installed
Condo sales surpass $100M in early pre-sales (38 condos presold)
Tower going vertical — first glass/curtainwall installation underway, condo floor work started, reportedly ahead of schedule, per WKRN.

Title: 601 N. Ashley Drive
Description: A planned luxury high-rise on a 0.97-acre site at the corner of Tampa and Twiggs streets in downtown Tampa, directly across from Curtis Hixon Waterfront Park along the Hillsborough River. The 540-foot, 40-to-50-story tower will pair luxury condominium residences with a hotel component, replacing the 12-story former GTE Building, currently being demolished to clear the site.
DescriptionLong: 601 N. Ashley Drive is a planned luxury high-rise development on a 0.97-acre site at the corner of Tampa and Twiggs streets in downtown Tampa, directly across from Curtis Hixon Waterfront Park along the Hillsborough River. The site is currently being cleared through active demolition of the 12-story former GTE Building — originally the Wallace S. Stovall Tampa Tribune building — including its basement, foundation, and adjacent surface parking lot. The site holds FAA height approval for a 540-foot tower (558 feet above mean sea level), the maximum envelope permitted at the location, and is planned to rise between 40 and 50 stories. The development is being designed as a luxury condominium tower with a hotel component, and the developer has signaled interest in a branded residential product that would shape both interior and exterior design. The project joins One Tampa, under construction directly across the street, and Pendry Residences Tampa, a half-mile south, as part of the wave of luxury residential development reshaping Tampa's downtown skyline. Final architect of record, unit count, and construction timeline have not yet been publicly released; the developer has indicated additional design and program details will come later in 2026.

StatusHistory.note: Stock Development acquires the 0.97-acre site for $40M and pivots the project to a luxury condominium tower overlooking Curtis Hixon Waterfront Park

Title: Wyld Oaks
Description: A 215-acre master-planned mixed-use district in northwest Orange County, anchored by the State Road 429 Beltway, with planned residential neighborhoods, retail and restaurant space, hospitality uses, an AdventHealth health village and medical campus, educational uses, parks and trails, entertainment venues, and approximately 1.5 million square feet of industrial space.
DescriptionLong: Wyld Oaks is a 215-acre master-planned mixed-use development along the western side of State Road 429 just south of West Kelly Park Road in northwest Orange County, Florida. The original land assemblage totaled roughly 315 acres, with portions sold off for healthcare and other uses — including an AdventHealth emergency room and helipad already operating on site. At full buildout the district is planned to include residential neighborhoods, retail and restaurant space, hospitality, an AdventHealth health village and medical campus, medical and professional office space, educational uses, parks, trails, entertainment venues including the planned Wyld Green outdoor venue, and approximately 1.5 million square feet of industrial space — supported by the project's WyldTech fiber infrastructure initiative. The master developer is advancing the project through a horizontal-then-vertical model: nearly two miles of internal roads, sidewalks, and street lighting have already been completed, and individual parcels are now being sold to vertical developers. The site sits on one of Central Florida's fastest-growing corridors, with more than 13,000 housing units planned or under construction in the surrounding northwest Orange County market.
ProjectType: Mixed-Use, Residences, Retail, Hotel, Healthcare, Office, Education, Park, Entertainment

StatusHistory.note: 215-acre, ~$1.2B master-planned mixed-use destination unveiled in northwest Apopka along the Kelly Park corridor
215-acre, ~$1.2B master-planned mixed-use destination unveiled in northwest Apopka along the Kelly Park corridor
215-acre, ~$1.2B master-planned mixed-use destination unveiled in northwest Apopka along the Kelly Park corridor
Wyld Oaks master plan breaks ground in northwest Apopka
Hathaway Development acquires an 11-acre parcel ($13M) for Exchange at Wyld Oaks (325 apartments) — the first multifamily community at Wyld Oaks
Horizontal infrastructure complete (2 mi roads, utilities); vertical construction underway with first apartment communities breaking ground 2026, per Florida YIMBY.

Title: The Boxer
Description: A proposed eight-story boutique affordable mixed-use development on a 0.16-acre site in Wynwood, with 14 three-bedroom residences priced for households earning between 80 and 120 percent of area median income and 4,100 square feet of ground-floor retail.
DescriptionLong: The Boxer is a proposed boutique affordable mixed-use development planned for a 0.16-acre site in Wynwood, eight stories above grade and built around 14 three-bedroom residences with 4,100 square feet of ground-floor retail. The income mix is structured to keep the building accessible across multiple workforce-income tiers: two homes are designated for households earning up to 80 percent of area median income, four for households at 100 percent AMI, and the remaining eight for households up to 120 percent AMI — calibrated against the Miami-Dade County AMI of $87,000 as of May 2025. The development originated from a Miami-Dade County land deal in which the parcel — with an estimated market value of $3.45 million — was conveyed for $10, with the developer committing $800,000 in scholarships for County Commission District 3 (Wynwood, Miami Shores, and parts of Brownsville) as part of the agreement. Construction is not anticipated to start for at least 24 months, with permits, entitlements, and approvals still to be secured.

StatusHistory.note: Boutique 8-story, 14-unit income-restricted residential project plus ~4,100 sq ft retail planned at 20 NE 29th St, Wynwood (Rilea Group + Ciprés)

Title: Palm Beach Gardens IcePlex
Description: A proposed two-story, approximately 123,000-square-foot indoor ice sports facility in Plant Drive Park, Palm Beach Gardens, anchored by two NHL-size rinks supporting hockey, figure skating, public skating, curling, and sled hockey, with a retail store, academy space, full-service restaurant, performance center, and community rooms.
DescriptionLong: The Palm Beach Gardens IcePlex is a proposed indoor ice sports facility in Plant Drive Park, sited between North Military Trail and Burns Road in northern Palm Beach County. The two-story building is designed for approximately 88,450 square feet on the ground floor and 34,795 square feet on the second level — roughly 123,000 square feet total — and centers on two NHL-size rinks supporting youth hockey, figure skating, public skating, adult leagues, curling, and sled hockey programs. The ground floor also houses a retail store, academy space, locker rooms, a dedicated medical space, and a quick-serve concession area. The second floor adds multipurpose rooms, a large community/conference room, a performance center, a full-service restaurant, and viewing areas overlooking the rinks. Green building principles are being integrated throughout for energy and water efficiency. The facility is being developed in cooperative agreement with the City of Palm Beach Gardens, and once completed will have the capacity to host regional, national, and international ice sports events alongside its core community recreation programming.

StatusHistory.note: Palm Beach North Athletic Foundation's two-rink IcePlex planned at Plant Drive Park; secures a $10M founding gift
Palm Beach North Athletic Foundation's two-rink IcePlex planned at Plant Drive Park; secures a $10M founding gift
$50M+ funding secured and a 40-year ground lease executed; groundbreaking targeted for ~July 2025
Funding secured and 40-year ground lease executed; groundbreaking targeted for ~July 2025 with an ~18-month build — queued for review pending confirmation that construction has commenced
Broke ground 2025-07-03 at Plant Drive Park; now in active construction (~18-month build to Dec 2026), per Florida Construction News.
Broke ground 2025-07-03 at Plant Drive Park; now in active construction (~18-month build to Dec 2026), per Florida Construction News.

Title: The Ritz-Carlton Punta Nizuc
Description: The Ritz-Carlton Punta Nizuc is an oceanfront luxury resort and branded-residence community in the Punta Nizuc enclave of Cancun, Mexico, set on a rare sliver of land between the Nichupte Lagoon and the Caribbean Sea. Turnkey two- to four-bedroom residences frame water views and blur the line between refined interiors and the tropical landscape. Residents enjoy dedicated concierge service and access to the resort's oceanfront pool complex, spa, and gourmet restaurants. Wellness experiences, curated dining, and white-glove service define daily life in one of the Caribbean coast's most coveted natural settings.
DescriptionLong: The Ritz-Carlton, Cancún, Punta Nizuc marks the luxury brand's return to Cancún, rising on an oceanfront site at the southern tip of the hotel zone where the Caribbean meets the Riviera Maya. The development pairs a 131-room hotel — a joint venture of FibraHotel and Fibra Danhos — with 126 Ritz-Carlton branded residences in a LEED-certified building by Beyond Ventures. Designed by Lissoni & Partners with Cherem Arquitectos, the resort centers on an oceanfront pool complex, a destination spa, and six restaurants, all backed by Ritz-Carlton service. Set minutes from Cancún International Airport, the property positions branded residential ownership within one of Mexico's most established luxury-travel gateways.

StatusHistory.note: Marriott with FibraHotel, Fibra Danhos and Beyond Ventures bring The Ritz-Carlton back to Cancún (~$400M) at Punta Nizuc — hotel plus branded residences
Construction reported already underway at the October 2024 announcement, targeting a 2027 inauguration

Title: Soho House Los Cabos
Description: Soho House Los Cabos is the members' club brand's first outpost in Mexico, set in the Cabo del Sol enclave of Cabo San Lucas where the Sea of Cortez meets the Pacific. The intimate retreat pairs a small collection of club bedrooms with private residences and villas, several featuring plunge pools, all built from terracotta, local woods, and handcrafted finishes that settle the property into its desert-and-sea landscape. Members and guests gather around one of the largest Soho House pools, a beachfront sunset bar, a coastal Italian restaurant, and a cabaret for live performances. A full wellness and fitness club rounds out an experience tuned to creative community, design, and the Baja shoreline.
DescriptionLong: Soho House Los Cabos brings the members' club and hotel brand to the beaches of Cabo del Sol on Mexico's Baja peninsula, its debut in the country. Set directly on the Sea of Cortez, the beachfront house blends the brand's signature mix of bedrooms for members and guests with pools, a spa, restaurants, and indoor-outdoor lounge spaces designed around the desert-coast landscape. Developed in partnership with FIBRA SOMA, the project extends Soho House's global collection of design-forward social clubs to one of Mexico's most sought-after luxury-leisure destinations, anchoring the Cabo del Sol community's hospitality offering.

StatusHistory.note: SOMA Group announces Soho House at Cabo del Sol — Soho House's first in Mexico (Phase 2 of the development)
SOMA Group announces Soho House at Cabo del Sol — Soho House's first in Mexico (Phase 2 of the development)
SOMA Group announces Soho House at Cabo del Sol — Soho House's first in Mexico (Phase 2 of the development)
SOMA Group announces Soho House at Cabo del Sol — Soho House's first in Mexico (Phase 2 of the development)

Title: Conrad Los Cabos at Oleada
Description: Conrad Los Cabos at Oleada is an oceanfront luxury resort and branded-residence community within the Oleada Pacific Living and Golf development, a short drive from downtown Cabo San Lucas on Mexico's Baja California Sur coast. A limited collection of two- to four-bedroom residences offers expansive terraces, private plunge pools, and uninterrupted Pacific views alongside the resort's guest rooms and suites. Owners and guests share a spa with ten treatment rooms, a fitness center, multiple pools and outdoor decks, and a range of dining venues from a beachfront grill to a lobby bar. Contemporary art, bold design, and a mile-long beach define a polished retreat on the southern tip of the peninsula.
DescriptionLong: Conrad Los Cabos at Oleada marks the Conrad brand's arrival on Mexico's Baja California Sur coast, anchoring the master-planned Oleada community in Los Cabos. The resort pairs 175 guestrooms and suites with 43 Conrad-branded residences, wrapped around oceanfront pools, a destination spa, and a collection of restaurants and lounges oriented to the Sea of Cortez. Contemporary architecture and indoor-outdoor living frame the desert-meets-ocean setting, while residence owners receive full Conrad service and access to the resort's amenities. The property joins a fast-growing roster of ultra-luxury hospitality along the Los Cabos corridor.

StatusHistory.note: Hilton signs Conrad Los Cabos (Conrad's Baja debut) within the OLEADA Pacific Living & Golf community
Hilton signs Conrad Los Cabos (Conrad's Baja debut) within the OLEADA Pacific Living & Golf community
Hilton signs Conrad Los Cabos (Conrad's Baja debut) within the OLEADA Pacific Living & Golf community
175 total: 111 guestrooms + 21 suites (=132 keys) + 43 Conrad Residences, in the OLEADA community, per Hilton official. units=43 = residences.

Title: Anson at Palmetto Bluff
Description: Anson is the fourth and final village of Palmetto Bluff, a master-planned Lowcountry community in Bluffton, South Carolina, unfolding along the southeastern shore where the New River winds through marsh grass beneath ancient oak canopies. Conceived as the conservation-minded companion to the established Wilson and Moreland villages, Anson opens new waterfront access toward the southern Intracoastal Waterway. Residents enjoy a walkable championship golf course routed through maritime forest and wetlands, a riverfront marina with wet slips, and miles of preserved natural landscape.
