Refined yet rooted in place, Anson pairs timeless Lowcountry architecture with an unhurried, nature-first way of living.
DescriptionLong: Anson is the third village rising within Palmetto Bluff, the sprawling Lowcountry community along the May River near Bluffton, South Carolina. At its heart is Anson Point, a new 18-hole championship course designed by Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw on the property's eastern end — a routing that takes advantage of the maritime forest, tidal marsh, and river frontage that define the Bluff. Developed by South Street Partners, Anson will grow into a full village of homes, neighborhoods, and gathering places alongside the course, complementing Palmetto Bluff's existing villages, its Montage resort, marina, and vast nature preserve. The expansion deepens one of the Southeast's most celebrated private sporting and residential communities.

StatusHistory.note: Anson named as Palmetto Bluff's fourth and final village under South Street Partners / Henderson Park ownership
Anson named as Palmetto Bluff's fourth and final village under South Street Partners / Henderson Park ownership

Anson is the third/final residential village at Palmetto Bluff (not a Montage hotel); anchored by Anson Point, an 18-hole Coore & Crenshaw private course that opened Feb 2026. Developer South Street Partners.

Title: The Loren at Turtle Cove
Description: An intimate ultra-luxury beachfront resort and residences at Turtle Cove on Providenciales, blending boutique hotel suites with private villas and oceanfront condominiums.
DescriptionLong: The Loren at Turtle Cove brings the ultra-luxury Loren brand to Providenciales, set along the protected waters of Turtle Cove on the north shore of Turks & Caicos. The low-density retreat pairs 33 guest suites with 25 oceanfront condominium residences and six beachfront villas, arranged around pools, a spa, and beach-club dining that open directly to the Caribbean. Architecture and interiors emphasize natural stone, water, and light, framing reef and ocean views and the brand's understated, residential approach to resort living. Owners and guests share full hotel service in one of the island's most established waterfront settings.

StatusHistory.note: Loren Hotels announces its Turks & Caicos debut at Turtle Cove — the group's third property
Loren Hotels announces its Turks & Caicos debut at Turtle Cove — the group's third property
Loren Hotels announces its Turks & Caicos debut at Turtle Cove — the group's third property
Topped out at the fifth floor with two crowning penthouses; MEP installation underway
Topped out at the 5th floor by Sept 2025, MEP underway; opening targeted 2026 (corrects 2027), per Caribbean Journal.
Topped out at the 5th floor by Sept 2025, MEP underway; opening targeted 2026 (corrects 2027), per Caribbean Journal.

Title: 101 Franklin Street
Description: A Tribeca office-to-residential conversion that lifts a mid-century building to 21 stories and 72 luxury condominiums behind a masonry-inspired Steven Harris Architects facade.
DescriptionLong: 101 Franklin Street — also addressed as 250 Church Street, on the block between Franklin and Leonard — is a luxury office-to-residential conversion in the heart of Tribeca. Designed by Steven Harris Architects for a development partnership of Skylight Real Estate Partners, Cannon Hill Capital Partners, and TPG, the project adds four floors atop the existing mid-century superstructure to reach 21 stories and roughly 304 feet, expanding the building to about 251,000 square feet with 72 condominium homes above ground-floor retail and enclosed parking. The design leans into the neighborhood's cast-iron and masonry heritage, wrapping the base in light stone with double-height arched windows beneath a wraparound canopy. The result is a contextual addition to one of Manhattan's most coveted residential enclaves.
Developer: Skylight Real Estate Partners, Cannon Hill Capital Partners, TPG

StatusHistory.note: Office-to-condo conversion plus four-floor expansion of a 1948 tower at 101 Franklin Street (250 Church Street), Tribeca — 72 condominiums
Office-to-condo conversion physically underway on-site per NY YIMBY (Aug 2025) & 6sqft; renderings revealed Mar 2026

Title: The Travis
Description: A luxury high-rise development in downtown Austin near the Rainey Street District, pairing hundreds of upscale residences with a companion hotel-and-condominium tower.
DescriptionLong: The Travis is a luxury high-rise development rising on the eastern edge of downtown Austin near the Rainey Street District, conceived by Genesis Real Estate Group with architecture by GDA Architects. The project centers on a residential tower of roughly 423 apartments complemented by a companion tower combining a hotel with for-sale condominiums, knitting living, hospitality, and street-level retail into a single vertical address. Floor-to-ceiling glass, private terraces, and resort-style amenity decks orient residents toward views of Lady Bird Lake and the downtown skyline, while the ground plane activates one of the fastest-growing pockets of the urban core.

StatusHistory.note: The Travis unveiled — a ~50-story apartment tower at 80 Red River Street in the Rainey Street District of downtown Austin
The Travis unveiled — a ~50-story apartment tower at 80 Red River Street in the Rainey Street District of downtown Austin

Title: Uga Ghiri
Description: Uga Resorts' eighth property, an all-inclusive 15-villa retreat on the historic Sutherland Estate in Ella, opening July 2026 with views of Sri Lanka's Nine Arch Bridge.
DescriptionLong: Uga Ghiri is an all-inclusive luxury boutique retreat set across a ten-acre tea estate on the historic Sutherland Estate in Ella, deep in Sri Lanka's hill country overlooking the iconic Nine Arch Bridge. The eighth property from Sri Lankan group Uga Resorts, it pairs fifteen standalone contemporary villas — each roughly 1,200 square feet with a separate living area and en-suite bath — with a restored colonial-era manor house serving as the culinary heart of the resort. Guests enjoy an infinity pool, a destination spa, and signature experiences ranging from tea-picking and Ceylon arrack tastings to market-to-table cooking masterclasses.
Architect: Philip Weeraratne, Gayan Karunarathne, Yasasi Hettiarachchi

StatusHistory.note: 15-villa Uga Ghiri retreat on the Sutherland Estate, Ella (Sri Lanka hill country); majority of trade outlets cite a mid-2026 opening (Hospitality Net).
15-villa Uga Ghiri retreat on the Sutherland Estate, Ella (Sri Lanka hill country); majority of trade outlets cite a mid-2026 opening (Hospitality Net).

Title: Sweetbird North
Description: An eight-story, 120,500-square-foot landmark office and retail building defined by layered, veiled volumes and a finely calibrated mesh facade.
DescriptionLong: Sweetbird North is an eight-story office-and-retail landmark in Miami's Design District, developed by Miami Design District Associates with Raycliff Capital and designed by Snøhetta. Rising on a roughly 17,000-square-foot site, the approximately 120,500-square-foot building stacks two levels of street-facing retail beneath six floors of workspace, all wrapped in a sculptural, layered metal-mesh double-skin facade that cuts solar heat gain while preserving daylight — a climate-responsive signature tuned to Miami's tropical setting.
Developer: Miami Design District Associates, Raycliff Capital

StatusHistory.note: Snøhetta unveils Sweetbird North, an 8-story office-and-retail building in Miami's Design District (Dezeen / The Real Deal / Parametric Architecture).
Sweetbird North is an office (floors 3–8) + retail (floors 1–2) building in the Miami Design District — reclassified from residential. No residential units (Parametric Architecture, The Real Deal).

Title: Palm Beach Atlantic University Residence Hall
Description: A 25-story residence hall bringing 990 student beds across 275 units, with extensive dining, fitness, and student-union amenities and an adjoining parking garage.
DescriptionLong: The Palm Beach Atlantic University Residence Hall is a 25-story, roughly 319,000-square-foot student-housing tower rising in Downtown West Palm Beach at the corner of South Dixie Highway and Acacia Road. Developed by Gilbane through a public-private partnership with the university and designed by lead architect CUBE 3, it is the largest residence hall in PBA's history, delivering 275 apartment-style units and roughly 990 beds in two-, three-, and four-bed configurations wrapped around a structured parking garage. Resident amenities include a 28,000-square-foot dining hall with an outdoor plaza, a 13,000-square-foot fitness center with group studios and a golf simulator, and eleven student-union collaboration spaces.

StatusHistory.note: PBAU 25-story residence hall proposed at 1200 S. Dixie Hwy, downtown West Palm Beach (Florida YIMBY).
PBAU and Gilbane broke ground on the 25-story residence hall in downtown West Palm Beach (Florida YIMBY).
$240M financial close (P3); fall 2027 delivery (Florida YIMBY, Gilbane).
$240M financial close (P3); fall 2027 delivery (Florida YIMBY, Gilbane).

PBA and Gilbane broke ground on the 25-story residence hall, per Florida YIMBY / PBA

Title: Four Seasons Resort and Residences AMAALA
Description: A 220-key Four Seasons wellness resort with 26 branded residential villas at AMAALA's Triple Bay, set on the Red Sea with a 27-hole oceanfront golf course.
DescriptionLong: The Four Seasons Resort and Residences AMAALA at Triple Bay is an ultra-luxury wellness destination set along the Red Sea on Saudi Arabia's northwestern coast, developed by Red Sea Global as the inaugural resort of the AMAALA destination. The property pairs 202 guest rooms, suites, and garden villas with 26 ultra-exclusive branded private residential villas, each with a private pool and ranging from roughly 552 to over 1,000 square meters. Anchored by the expansive HYLIAA Wellness & Spa and six open-air dining venues, the resort is powered entirely by renewable energy and zero-waste systems, positioning it as a flagship of Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 luxury-tourism ambitions.
Developer: Red Sea Global, Four Seasons Hotels and Resorts

StatusHistory.note: Red Sea Global and Four Seasons announce Four Seasons Resort and Residences AMAALA at Triple Bay.
Four Seasons Resort and Residences AMAALA at Triple Bay opened to guests June 15, 2026 — 202 keys + 26 branded villas (Red Sea Global, Zawya).
Four Seasons Resort and Residences AMAALA at Triple Bay opened to guests June 15, 2026 — 202 keys + 26 branded villas (Red Sea Global, Zawya).
Four Seasons Resort and Residences AMAALA at Triple Bay opened to guests June 15, 2026 — 202 keys + 26 branded villas (Red Sea Global, Zawya).

Title: Fairmont The Red Sea
Description: Fairmont The Red Sea is a luxury resort on Shura Island within Red Sea Global's flagship destination off Saudi Arabia's west coast.
DescriptionLong: Fairmont The Red Sea is a luxury resort on Shura Island within Red Sea Global's flagship destination off Saudi Arabia's west coast, set among dormant volcanoes and an archipelago of pristine islands. Operated under Accor's Fairmont brand, the resort offers 193 elegantly designed rooms and suites alongside several dining concepts, including a signature restaurant built out over the turquoise water. Guests have access to a comprehensive wellness spa and an 18-hole championship golf course as part of the island's Shura Links. Conceived as a sustainable, low-impact retreat, the resort joins SLS, The Red Sea EDITION, and InterContinental as a cornerstone of Shura Island's luxury hospitality collection.

StatusHistory.note: Fairmont-branded resort announced for Red Sea Global's Shura Island — 193 keys, 18-hole golf, overwater dining.
Fairmont-branded resort announced for Red Sea Global's Shura Island — 193 keys, 18-hole golf, overwater dining.
Accor's 2026 openings line-up lists a May 2026 opening (now past). Queued for review — confirm the resort actually opened before advancing coming-soon -> open.

Title: Six Senses Antognolla
Description: A 71-key Six Senses resort and 79 branded residences rising within a restored 12th-century castle estate in Umbria, anchored by an 18-hole Robert Trent Jones Jr. golf course.
DescriptionLong: Six Senses Antognolla transforms a 12th-century castle and its surrounding hamlet into a five-star resort and branded-residence community set across a roughly 540-hectare estate of olive groves, vineyards, and cypress in Umbria, near Perugia in the green heart of Italy. Operated by Six Senses with construction by Rizzani de Eccher, the resort pairs a 71-key hotel — including sixteen suites within the restored medieval castle — with an expansive wellness center, an 18-hole golf course, and a collection of branded residences at Borgo Massarello ranging from apartments and villas to six-bedroom farmhouses.

StatusHistory.note: 71-key Six Senses Antognolla within a restored 12th-century castle, Umbria; 16 suites in the castle, plus branded residences. GC Rizzani de Eccher.
71-key Six Senses Antognolla within a restored 12th-century castle, Umbria; 16 suites in the castle, plus branded residences. GC Rizzani de Eccher.

Title: Aman Residences Tokyo
Description: Aman's first standalone branded residences worldwide — 91 homes crowning the top floors of Tokyo's Mori JP Tower in Azabudai Hills, overlooking Tokyo Tower.
DescriptionLong: Aman Residences, Tokyo is the brand's first-ever standalone branded residential project, occupying the top eleven floors of the Mori JP Tower — Japan's tallest building — at the heart of Mori Building's Azabudai Hills district between Roppongi and Toranomon. The 91 residences float high above the capital with sweeping views toward Tokyo Tower and the skyline beyond, wrapped in serene, light-filled interiors by Yabu Pushelberg that translate Aman's pared-back aesthetic into vertical city living. Owners enjoy a private suite of Aman amenities including a spa, indoor pool, fitness and wellness facilities, dining, lounges, and the brand's signature anticipatory service, set within a vast new mixed-use neighborhood of gardens, galleries, shops, and restaurants.

Title: Memphis Art Museum
Description: Memphis Art Museum is the bold new downtown home of the Memphis Brooks Museum of Art, rising on the Mississippi River bluff in downtown Memphis.
DescriptionLong: Memphis Art Museum is the bold new downtown home of the Memphis Brooks Museum of Art, rising on the Mississippi River bluff along Front Street in downtown Memphis, Tennessee. Designed by Pritzker Prize-winning Swiss architects Herzog & de Meuron in partnership with Memphis firm archimania, the roughly 122,000-square-foot building pairs a luminous glass gallery pavilion with a sculpted earthen base that reconnects the city to its riverfront. The campus expands gallery space by about 50 percent and adds generous free public space, including a street-level community courtyard and a rooftop sculpture garden overlooking the river. Home to the region's largest collection of world art spanning thousands of years, the museum anchors a new downtown cultural campus and offers free admission to Shelby County residents.

StatusHistory.note: Brooks Museum selects Herzog & de Meuron (with archimania) to design new downtown riverfront museum via international competition; public opening set for Dec 6, 2026 per Memphis Flyer/Brooks.
Brooks Museum selects Herzog & de Meuron (with archimania) to design new downtown riverfront museum via international competition; public opening set for Dec 6, 2026 per Memphis Flyer/Brooks.
Groundbreaking ceremony; construction begins on the Herzog & de Meuron–designed downtown museum.

Title: Cello Tower
Description: Cello Tower is a 32-story luxury condominium high-rise anchoring the Origin at Symphony Park district in downtown Las Vegas.
DescriptionLong: Cello Tower is a 32-story luxury condominium high-rise anchoring the Origin at Symphony Park district in downtown Las Vegas, marking the city's first new residential-only high-rise in over a decade. Developed by Red Ridge Development and designed by Perkins Eastman with interiors by One Line Design Studio, the 240-residence tower rises 379 feet above the emerging Symphony Park enclave. Residences range from one-bedroom layouts to an exclusive top-floor penthouse collection, served by more than 40,000 square feet of amenities across three dedicated floors. Highlights include a resort-style pool with skyline views, a wellness and spa level, a golf simulator, an outdoor cigar lounge, and a ground-floor retail destination.

StatusHistory.note: First look unveiled at 32-story, 379-ft, 240-unit luxury condo tower anchoring Origin at Symphony Park (Red Ridge Development), downtown Las Vegas.
First look unveiled at 32-story, 379-ft, 240-unit luxury condo tower anchoring Origin at Symphony Park (Red Ridge Development), downtown Las Vegas.
Ceremonial groundbreaking (Mayor Berkley + Red Ridge CEO Patrick Brennan); over $140M in sales at the time.
Ceremonial groundbreaking (Mayor Berkley + Red Ridge CEO Patrick Brennan); over $140M in sales at the time.
Completion now Q2 2027 (phase one Q4 2026) per Las Vegas Review-Journal; start slipped to spring 2026 on tariff headwinds

Title: Kimpton Turks & Caicos
Description: Kimpton Turks & Caicos is a 192-room new-build lifestyle resort rising on the white sands of Grace Bay in Providenciales.
DescriptionLong: Kimpton Turks & Caicos is a 192-room new-build lifestyle resort rising on the celebrated white sands of Grace Bay in Providenciales, marking IHG's Kimpton brand debut in the Caribbean. Developed by Molo Hotel Group and operated by Lighthouse Hotel Management, the beachfront resort is designed by Glasgow studio Ica with Turks & Caicos firm Conservative Architecture and landscape architects EDSA. Guests will find three outdoor pools, a spa and fitness center, and seven distinct dining venues ranging from a beachfront restaurant and rooftop bar to a juice bar and ice-cream parlor. Signature Kimpton touches — evening social hour, complimentary bicycles, in-room yoga mats, and a pet-friendly welcome — bring boutique character to one of the world's most coveted beaches.

StatusHistory.note: IHG marks Turks & Caicos debut: 192-room new-build Kimpton on Grace Bay, Providenciales, slated to open by 2027.
IHG marks Turks & Caicos debut: 192-room new-build Kimpton on Grace Bay, Providenciales, slated to open by 2027.

STATUS REVIEW (LOW confidence): record is marked "construction" but only a May 2024 IHG brand-signing announcement could be found in trusted press — no groundbreaking or vertical-construction evidence. Proposing demotion to "announced" for human review (do not assume construction). Bio upgrade included. Title: Ace & Ivy
Description: Ace & Ivy is a boutique, wellness-forward luxury condominium tower in Houston's River Oaks neighborhood.
DescriptionLong: Ace & Ivy is a boutique, wellness-forward luxury condominium tower at 3100 West Alabama Street in Houston's River Oaks neighborhood. Developed by Houston-based Everlight Group and designed by acclaimed San Antonio firm Lake Flato, the building offers 31 residences, including three penthouses, each appointed with its own in-home sauna. Amenities center on longevity and recovery — a hydrotherapy spa with steam and contrast therapy, red-light therapy rooms, a fitness center and yoga studio, an expansive pool deck, a residents' lounge, and a mahjong parlor. The development also brings an indoor pickleball club and curated retail and dining to the edge of Upper Kirby, with sales led by Douglas Elliman Development Marketing.

StatusHistory.note: Ace & Ivy revealed: boutique 31-residence wellness condo at 3100 W. Alabama, River Oaks, Houston (Everlight Group / Lake Flato); Douglas Elliman sales, from $2.8M.

Title: Hi Line Square
Description: Hi Line Square is a landmark mixed-use development anchoring the Hi Line sub-neighborhood of the Dallas Design District.
DescriptionLong: Hi Line Square is a landmark mixed-use development anchoring the Hi Line sub-neighborhood of the Dallas Design District at 1650 Oak Lawn Avenue. Developed by McCourt Partners with HN Capital Partners and designed by Perkins & Will, the project pairs a 30-story residential tower of 300 units — including penthouses, townhomes, and what backers call Dallas's highest swimming pool — with a 17-story office tower and roughly 45,000 square feet of curated retail and dining. The architecture blends the district's industrial heritage in brick and concrete with sleek glass-and-metal stepped terraces, organized around a pedestrian plaza centered on a preserved heirloom tree. The development is positioned as a dynamic urban gateway uniting luxury residential living, hospitality-inspired workspace, showrooms, and dining.

StatusHistory.note: McCourt Partners & HN Capital introduce Hi Line Square — two-tower mixed-use landmark (30-story residential + 17-story office + retail) for the Dallas Design District.
Corrects construction start 2026 -> 2027 (press release + The Real Deal both state construction begins 2027).

Title: Four Seasons Private Residences Las Vegas
Description: Four Seasons Private Residences Las Vegas is an ultra-luxury, twin-tower high-rise community in the gated MacDonald Highlands enclave of Henderson, Nevada.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Private Residences Las Vegas is an ultra-luxury, twin-tower high-rise community set within the private, gated MacDonald Highlands golf community of Henderson, Nevada. Developed by Azure Resorts & Hotels with Luxus Developments and Two Roads Development and designed by global architecture firm WATG, the project pairs 171 branded condominium residences with a collection of standalone custom homes. Residences range from spacious two-bedroom layouts to expansive penthouses, complemented by sweeping outdoor living space and Strip and mountain views. Owners enjoy legendary Four Seasons service alongside roughly 90,000 square feet of amenities and dining curated by the Wolfgang Puck Fine Dining Group, set against the contemporary desert architecture of the Las Vegas Valley's first residential high-rise since the Great Recession.

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons-branded standalone residences unveiled for MacDonald Highlands, Henderson — 171 high-rise residences plus standalone homes; first Vegas Valley residential high-rise since the recession.
Lands $781M construction financing (Concord Summit Capital); excavation complete, foundations pouring, ~75% sold. Delivery slips 2026 -> mid-2027.
Lands $781M construction financing (Concord Summit Capital); excavation complete, foundations pouring, ~75% sold. Delivery slips 2026 -> mid-2027.
Dual towers going vertical above Henderson per Fox5 Vegas; topping-off targeted end of 2026, completion mid-2027

Title: Azabudai Hills
Description: Japan's tallest building anchors this 8.1-hectare Mori "modern urban village" in Minato, home to Aman Residences Tokyo and the world's first Janu hotel.
DescriptionLong: Azabudai Hills is an 8.1-hectare mixed-use district in Tokyo's Minato ward, developed by Mori Building over nearly three decades of planning and opened in November 2023. Conceived by Pelli Clarke & Partners as a "modern urban village," it weaves three high-rise towers through 24,000 square meters of greenery, a 6,000-square-meter central plaza, roughly 150 shops and restaurants, an international school, the teamLab Borderless digital art museum, and a Keio University preventive-medicine center. Its centerpiece, the 330-meter, 64-story Mori JP Tower, is Japan's tallest building, with a lotus-inspired glass crown. The top eleven floors (54–64) hold Aman Residences, Tokyo — 91 hotel-branded residences with a residents-only 1,400-square-meter Aman Spa, marking Aman's first standalone urban branded-residence project. The adjacent Residence A tower houses Janu Tokyo, the global debut of Aman's sister brand, with about 122 rooms, a 3,500-square-meter spa, and six restaurants and bars. Interiors and low-rise structures were shaped by Sou Fujimoto and Thomas Heatherwick, and the district carries LEED Platinum Neighborhood Development certification, the first in Tokyo.
Architect: Pelli Clarke & Partners, Heatherwick Studio, Sou Fujimoto

Title: Mori JP Tower
Description: At 330 meters, Japan's tallest building — the lotus-crowned office and residential centerpiece of Azabudai Hills, holding Aman Residences Tokyo on its top floors.
DescriptionLong: Mori JP Tower is the centerpiece of Mori Building's Azabudai Hills district in Minato, Tokyo, and at 330 meters across 64 floors it is Japan's tallest building, a title it holds until the 390-meter Torch Tower completes near Tokyo Station in 2027. Designed by Pelli Clarke & Partners, the tower wears a pearl-gray glass facade evoking a lotus flower, crowned by four curved glass petals lit at night. Its lower and middle floors hold roughly 214,000 square meters of office space for some 20,000 workers, with a Keio University preventive-medicine center on the fifth and sixth floors and a 34th-floor sky lobby open to visitors. The top eleven floors, 54 through 64, are given over to Aman Residences, Tokyo — 91 hotel-branded homes with a residents-only 1,400-square-meter Aman Spa, Aman's first standalone urban branded-residence project. The tower is pursuing WELL, LEED ND and LEED BD+C certification within a district already certified LEED Platinum for Neighborhood Development.

StatusHistory.note: Toranomon-Azabudai (Azabudai Hills) city plan authorized — Mori JP Tower greenlit
Mori JP Tower tops out as Japan's tallest building
Azabudai Hills grand opening — Mori JP Tower opens as Japan's tallest building
Azabudai Hills grand opening — Mori JP Tower opens as Japan's tallest building

Title: Aman Beverly Hills
Description: Aman's first West Coast hotel — set within the 17.5-acre One Beverly Hills district, a Foster + Partners-designed campus that also brings two Aman-branded residential towers, the Waldorf Astoria, the Beverly Hilton, and 10 acres of botanical gardens onto a single site at Wilshire and Santa Monica Boulevards.
DescriptionLong: Aman Beverly Hills is Aman's first hotel on the U.S. West Coast — a flagship property anchoring the 17.5-acre One Beverly Hills district at the intersection of Wilshire and Santa Monica Boulevards. Designed by Foster + Partners as part of a campus that also includes the Waldorf Astoria, the Beverly Hilton, and two Aman-branded residential towers, the Aman hotel program centers on full Aman-level service, an Aman Spa, multiple restaurants, and direct access to roughly 10 acres of botanical gardens woven through the district. The property carries Aman's signature low-density approach to luxury hospitality within one of the largest single new development sites in modern Beverly Hills history.
Developer: Aman Resorts, Cain International, Eldridge Industries

StatusHistory.note: Aman named flagship operator of One Beverly Hills (Aman Beverly Hills hotel + residences)
Aman named flagship operator of One Beverly Hills (Aman Beverly Hills hotel + residences)
One Beverly Hills groundbreaking ceremony (Aman hotel + residences + club)
Vertical construction begins on the One Beverly Hills / Aman towers
One Beverly Hills finalizes $4.3B financing to complete the development
One Beverly Hills finalized $4.3B construction financing ($2.8B JPMorgan senior + $1.5B VICI mezzanine), covering up to 200 Aman-branded residences.
One Beverly Hills finalized $4.3B construction financing ($2.8B JPMorgan senior + $1.5B VICI mezzanine), covering up to 200 Aman-branded residences.

Title: 1221 Grundy
Description: A boutique extended-stay hotel of 144 apartment-style suites in the Gulch, operating under Marriott's Apartments by Bonvoy brand.
DescriptionLong: The Hook is a boutique, apartment-style hotel occupying the lower levels of the 16-story tower at 1221 Grundy Street in Nashville's Gulch. Its 144 extended-stay suites operate under Marriott's newer Apartments by Bonvoy brand, pairing the space and self-sufficiency of an apartment with the service and consistency of an upscale hotel — a polished alternative to short-term rentals in one of the country's busiest tourism markets. Guests share the tower's rooftop amenity level, with a pool and meeting space, and sit directly above 15,000 square feet of ground-floor retail earmarked for an experiential entertainment tenant. The concept follows developer Tidal Real Estate Partners' earlier Apartments by Bonvoy property, The Ann in Savannah, Georgia.

StatusHistory.note: 16-story dual-brand hotel/timeshare tower (1221 Grundy) reaches final planning stage in North Gulch
On-site construction begins on the 16-story 1221 Grundy tower ($72.1M permit; Brasfield & Gorrie CM)
1221 Grundy: 16 stories, 144 extended-stay hotel keys + 168 timeshare residences + 14,500 sf retail, North Gulch
1221 Grundy: 16 stories, 144 extended-stay hotel keys + 168 timeshare residences + 14,500 sf retail, North Gulch
1221 Grundy: 16 stories, 144 extended-stay hotel keys + 168 timeshare residences + 14,500 sf retail, North Gulch
1221 Grundy: 16 stories, 144 extended-stay hotel keys + 168 timeshare residences + 14,500 sf retail, North Gulch
1221 Grundy: 16 stories, 144 extended-stay hotel keys + 168 timeshare residences + 14,500 sf retail, North Gulch

Title: 1221 Grundy Street
Description: A 16-story Gulch tower pairing an extended-stay boutique hotel on its lower floors with urban timeshare residences above, topped by a rooftop pool deck.
DescriptionLong: 1221 Grundy Street is a 16-story dual-branded hotel and Marriott Vacation Club tower rising in Nashville's North Gulch. Developed by Tidal Real Estate Partners and designed by ESa (Earl Swensson Associates), the tower pairs roughly 144 hotel guest rooms with 168 Marriott Vacation Club timeshare residences above ground-floor retail, steps from the Gulch's dining, music and entertainment scene. With a Sage Hospitality–operated hotel and Marriott's City Collection timeshare brand under one roof, 1221 Grundy brings a vertically integrated hospitality landmark to one of downtown Nashville's fastest-growing submarkets.

StatusHistory.note: 16-story dual-brand hotel + Marriott Vacation Club timeshare tower proposed in North Gulch
16-story dual-brand hotel + Marriott Vacation Club timeshare tower proposed in North Gulch
16-story dual-brand hotel + Marriott Vacation Club timeshare tower proposed in North Gulch
Construction starts on the 1221 Grundy Street North Gulch tower
Tidal closes $181.8M construction loan for the North Gulch hotel/timeshare tower
16-story North Gulch tower combining 144 hotel rooms + 168 timeshare units over 14,000+ sf retail (developer Tidal Real Estate Partners; architect ESa).

Title: Costa Palmas
Description: A 1,500-acre luxury resort and residential community on a two-mile stretch of Baja's East Cape, anchored by Four Seasons and Aman resorts, a 250-slip marina, and an RTJ II golf course.
DescriptionLong: Costa Palmas is a 1,500-acre luxury resort and residential community set on two miles of calm, swimmable Sea of Cortez shoreline near the town of La Ribera on Baja California Sur's East Cape, about 45 minutes from Los Cabos International Airport. Masterplanned to weave together desert, mountain, and sea, the community brings together two flagship resorts — Four Seasons Resort Los Cabos and Amanvari, Aman's first property in Mexico — alongside a collection of branded and custom residences, a Robert Trent Jones II 18-hole golf course, a 250-slip full-service marina able to accommodate yachts up to 250 feet, a Marina Village of shops and restaurants, a Beach & Yacht Club, a sports park, organic farms and orchards, and a roster of dining venues. Three kilometers of white-sand beach, freshwater estuaries, and the Sierra de la Laguna mountains frame a community designed around controlled, long-term growth and direct water access.

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons + Irongate reveal the Costa Palmas master community on Baja's East Cape

Title: Aman Residences, Amanvari
Description: Aman-branded residences on a secluded estuary at Costa Palmas, with organic contemporary design, internal courtyards, and commanding Sea of Cortez views.
DescriptionLong: Amanvari Residences bring the full Aman lifestyle into private ownership on a secluded estuary at Costa Palmas on Baja's East Cape. Tucked between golden dunes, a verdant estuary, and the edible bounty of local farms, the homes rise from the surrounding landscape with commanding views over the Sea of Cortez. An organic, contemporary design aesthetic flows seamlessly from indoor to outdoor living through a series of internal courtyards and lounge areas, with expansive floor-to-ceiling glass walls and playful screening framing Baja's skies and desert-meets-sea setting. Owners enjoy all the beauty, simplicity, and personal attention of an Aman resort balanced with the seclusion of a private home, plus access to the wider Costa Palmas community.

StatusHistory.note: Aman announces its Mexico debut — Amanvari at Costa Palmas
Amanvari near completion — reservations open ahead of Aug 1, 2026 opening
Amanvari near completion — reservations open ahead of Aug 1, 2026 opening
Set within the 1,500-acre Costa Palmas master-planned community on the Sea of Cortez, La Ribera (Aman's debut in Mexico).

Title: Four Seasons Resort & Residences, Los Cabos at Costa Palmas
Description: A 118-key Four Seasons resort on the Sea of Cortez at Costa Palmas, the brand's first marina offering, with suites, villas, dining, pools, and a spa.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Resort Los Cabos at Costa Palmas brings the brand's hospitality to Baja's less-traveled East Cape, set on the calm, swimmable waters of the Sea of Cortez. The 118-key resort offers suites, villas, and residences alongside inspired dining, a series of pools, a spa and athletic center, and a swimming cove, all designed with a contemporary sensibility where light and shade, art and architecture, and indoor and outdoor space meet. Opened in 2019 as the anchor of the Costa Palmas community, it marked Four Seasons' first-ever marina offering, with guests and owners enjoying direct access to the marina, the Robert Trent Jones II golf course, the Beach & Yacht Club, and three kilometers of white-sand beach.
Developer: Irongate Group, Four Seasons Hotels and Resorts

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons + Irongate announce Los Cabos resort at Costa Palmas (141 keys)
Four Seasons Resort Los Cabos at Costa Palmas opens
Four Seasons Resort Los Cabos at Costa Palmas opens

Title: Costa Palmas Golf Club
Description: A Robert Trent Jones II 18-hole course at Costa Palmas that weaves from hillside to marina to beach, with generous landing areas and sweeping Sea of Cortez views.
DescriptionLong: The Costa Palmas Golf Club is an 18-hole course designed by Robert Trent Jones II that weaves from the hillside down to the marina and the beach, revealing breathtaking views of the mountains and the Sea of Cortez at every hole. With generous landing areas off the tees, the course is built to be a delight to play regardless of experience or skill level, anchoring the community's golf offering alongside a full practice facility. A beloved feature is the on-course stop at hole eight for lunch and drinks, part of a hospitality-forward approach that extends from the clubhouse to the fairways.

Title: Costa Palmas Marina & Marina Village
Description: Baja's first luxury marina at Costa Palmas, a 250-slip full-service harbor for yachts up to 250 feet, ringed by a village of boutiques and seafood dining.
DescriptionLong: The Costa Palmas Marina is Baja's first luxury marina and the social engine of the community — a 250-slip, full-service harbor that accommodates motor yachts up to 250 feet and serves as a magnet for an international community of yachters and sportfishermen. Exclusive yacht and charter-fishing services operate out of the harbor, while the surrounding Marina Village wraps the water in a curated Village Square of boutiques and restaurants serving fresh-caught seafood. The marina connects the resorts, residences, and the Beach & Yacht Club, anchoring the day-to-day rhythm of life on the water at Costa Palmas.

StatusHistory.note: Costa Palmas private marina revealed as part of the master community

Title: Casa Blake
Description: A boutique hotel and condominium residence on the water's edge in the Marina Village at Costa Palmas, billed as the social heart of the community.
DescriptionLong: Casa Blake is a boutique hotel and collection of condominium homes on the water's edge in the Marina Village at Costa Palmas. Designed for inspired, casual glamor and playful sophistication, it is envisioned as the living room of the village and the soul of the community — a luxe, free-spirited gathering place and a pied-à-terre in the heart of the action. The first Costa Palmas boutique residential experience, Casa Blake blends hotel hospitality with ownership in the most social corner of the marina.

StatusHistory.note: Irongate unveils Casa Blake — first Costa Palmas-branded residences at Marina Village
232 residences across two marina-side buildings (~24,000 m²) per Build Group / ingenor; under construction as of Feb 2026

Title: 201 Arkona Court
Description: A 26-story luxury condominium by the Norton Museum with a single full-floor residence per level, a duplex penthouse, a private cultural club, and a ground-floor market café.
DescriptionLong: This boutique luxury condominium tower is planned for a half-acre site at 201 and 203 Arkona Court in West Palm Beach's emerging Cultural Quarter, just east of Olive Avenue and steps from the Norton Museum of Art. Rising 26 stories and totaling roughly 156,400 square feet, the tower devotes an entire floor to each of its 16 main residences — single-level homes of at least 6,000 square feet beginning on the 11th floor — topped by a two-level penthouse, with 16 smaller guest suites on the sixth and seventh floors. A 10,500-square-foot private membership club occupies the eighth and ninth floors, with a kitchen, bar, pool, fitness center, and treatment rooms, conceived as a cultural gathering place open to residents and the public alike. A ground-floor market café and 2,270 square feet of retail open the base to the street, served by 123 parking spaces. Designed to complement the Norton's expansion and a planned cultural art park to the north, the tower treats the park as its front door and opens onto it.

StatusHistory.note: Easton Street Capital and Donald Trump Jr.'s 1789 Capital have filed plans for a 26-story West Palm Beach condo with full-floor residences and a private cultural club.
Palm Beach Post reporting describes 15 floors plus a 16th-floor penthouse (one ~5,000 sf unit per floor); corrected from 26 floors.

Revert to 26 stories — the Palm Beach Post's "15 floors + 16th-floor penthouse" appears to count residential floors, not total building height, conflicting with the curated 26-story figure. Flagging for manual review rather than overwriting on conflicting data.

Title: Wild Spring Dunes
Description: A Dream Golf destination resort on 2,400 acres of rolling dunes in East Texas, anchored by a Tom Doak championship course with a second Coore & Crenshaw layout planned.
DescriptionLong: Wild Spring Dunes is a destination golf community spread across 2,400 acres of rolling, sand-based terrain in the piney woods of East Texas, roughly two and a half hours southeast of Dallas near Nacogdoches. The first Texas project from Dream Golf — the team behind Bandon Dunes and Sand Valley — it is anchored by a championship course from Tom Doak's Renaissance Golf Design that routes across dramatic elevation changes, natural springs, brush-lined carries, and dry gullies, with eleven bridges linking holes across the property and long views over the wooded valley. A walking-first experience offered with caddies, the resort is planned to grow to 36 holes with a second layout by Coore & Crenshaw, a short course, and on-property lodging, cabins, and homesites set among the dunes. Conceived in the minimalist, walkable spirit of its sibling resorts, Wild Spring Dunes places world-class course design at the heart of an emerging Lone Star golf destination.

StatusHistory.note: Dream Golf (Keiser family) unveils 36-hole East Texas golf resort north of Nacogdoches
Preview play opened on first 8 holes of the Tom Doak course; full grand opening targeted Oct 2026
Preview play opened on first 8 holes of the Tom Doak course; full grand opening targeted Oct 2026
Full 18 (Tom Doak first course) opens for public play beginning Sept 8, 2026 with the formal grand opening that month — corrected from Oct 2026.

Title: The Mix
Description: A $3 billion, 112-acre mixed-use district rising on the former Wade Park site at the Dallas North Tollway and Lebanon Road in Frisco, organized around a nine-acre central park.
DescriptionLong: The Mix is a $3 billion, 112-acre mixed-use community taking shape at the southeast corner of the Dallas North Tollway and Lebanon Road in Frisco's fast-growing North Platinum Corridor, reviving the long-stalled Wade Park site. Master-planned around a nine-acre central park with roughly 16 acres of open green space, the walkable district blends thousands of residences across apartments and townhomes with about 375,000 square feet of retail, dining and entertainment, some two million square feet of office space, and two hotels. Wide pedestrian promenades, water features, and a curated public realm knit the neighborhoods together, while StreetLights Residential leads the first luxury multifamily phase. Developed by The Retail Connection and its partners, The Mix is conceived as a signature live-work-play destination for one of the most active growth corridors in North Texas.

StatusHistory.note: JVP unveils $3B, 112-acre mixed-use development on the former Wade Park site
Phase 1 groundbreaking at Dallas Pkwy & Lebanon Rd; AECOM Hunt is GC
One year into construction, going vertical; Whole Foods confirmed as retail anchor

Title: Four Seasons Hotel and Residences Dallas at Turtle Creek
Description: A 35-story Four Seasons hotel and branded-residence tower on Turtle Creek Boulevard, pairing a 240-room five-star hotel with 118 private residences in one of Dallas's most prestigious enclaves.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Hotel and Residences Dallas at Turtle Creek is a 35-story, roughly 464-foot luxury tower rising at the corner of Turtle Creek Boulevard and Cedar Springs Road, overlooking the parkland and bayou of one of Dallas's most coveted neighborhoods. Designed by Pelli Clarke & Partners with HKS as executive architect and landscape by SWA Group, the tower places a 240-key Four Seasons hotel on its lower floors and 118 private Four Seasons–branded residences above, ranging from pied-à-terre layouts to full-floor penthouses. Residents and guests share an extensive amenity program including multiple restaurants and bars, a signature spa and wellness floor, pools, a fitness center, a private residential club lounge, and full Four Seasons concierge and service. Developed by Carpenter & Company in partnership with Dallas's Perot family, the project brings the brand's first ground-up Dallas hotel-and-residence offering to the leafy Turtle Creek corridor near the Katy Trail, Uptown, and the Dallas Arts District.
Architect: Pelli Clarke & Partners, HKS, SWA Group

StatusHistory.note: $750M Four Seasons hotel and branded residences unveiled for Turtle Creek
Turtle Creek (3001 Turtle Creek Blvd); ~233 hotel keys alongside 118 residences (developer Carpenter & Company; architect Pelli Clarke & Partners).

Title: Shoma Bay
Description: A 28-story, 386-residence condominium blending an art-deco aesthetic with a modern facade, wrapped in resort-style amenities on the shores of Biscayne Bay.
DescriptionLong: Shoma Bay is a 28-story, 386-residence condominium tucked into North Bay Village, the small island enclave set between Miami Beach and the mainland in Biscayne Bay. The design melds a classic art-deco sensibility with a clean modern facade, opening onto studios through penthouses with spacious balconies, floor-to-ceiling windows, and light-filled layouts. Amenities lean resort-style and community-minded: a resident lounge and bar, a spa with a Hammam, golf simulators, a fitness center, a pet spa, a kids club, a wine cellar, a zen garden, and a grocery delivery room, alongside a 6,030-square-foot pool deck with cabanas, a summer kitchen, and panoramic water views over Biscayne Bay. A 2,600-square-foot co-working business lounge, a coffee-bar lounge, and dedicated gaming and cinema areas round out a building designed to integrate living, work, wellness, and play.

StatusHistory.note: Shoma Group launched Shoma Bay, a luxury waterfront condo tower in North Bay Village, in September 2022
Shoma Bay broke ground in North Bay Village, debuting a chauffeured Rolls-Royce resident perk
Shoma landed a $29.9M predevelopment loan from Forman Capital; project 30%+ presold per The Real Deal

Title: Sorin
Description: A 12-story Passive House condominium in Hudson Yards with a curved stone-and-glass facade, one- to four-bedroom residences, and roof and cellar recreation spaces.
DescriptionLong: Sorin is a 12-story, 120-foot luxury condominium rising at 439 West 36th Street in Manhattan's Hudson Yards, on an interior lot between Dyer and Tenth Avenues. Spanning roughly 48,138 square feet, the building is crafted to exacting Passive House standards for year-round energy efficiency and air quality, wrapped in a distinctive stone-and-glass facade marked by gentle curves, organic textures, floor-to-ceiling windows, and stepped setbacks with rounded corners that carve out private terraces. The collection of one- to four-bedroom residences is paired with recreation space on the roof and in the cellar level, a below-grade community facility, and ground-floor commercial space, a short walk from the 34th Street-Hudson Yards station.

StatusHistory.note: Original reveal: ZD Jasper's Passive House Hudson Yards condo first detailed by CityRealty, Nov 2023 (site assembled from Extell, permits filed 2022-23)
$117.5M construction financing from Maxim Capital Group (arranged by Meridian), Sept 2024, funding completion of the Hudson Yards Passive House condo
Condo sales launch — ZD Jasper brought on Serhant to launch sales at the 40-unit Sorin in Q2 2026 (per The Real Deal)

Title: Bvlgari Hotel Miami Beach
Description: The first Bvlgari Hotel in the United States, a waterfront restoration of a 1950s Modernist landmark with around 100 rooms and suites, a pool, spa, and a Niko Romito restaurant.
DescriptionLong: Bvlgari Hotel Miami Beach is the first U.S. property in the Bvlgari Hotels & Resorts collection, an adaptive-reuse transformation of a 1950s Miami Modern (MiMo) landmark on the Miami Beach waterfront beside the Art Deco district. Designed by Milan studio ACPV Architects (Antonio Citterio Patricia Viel), the hotel pairs roughly 100 mostly-suite rooms with a waterfront pool, a Bvlgari Spa, and signature dining from three-Michelin-star chef Niko Romito, bringing the Italian jeweler's understated, craft-driven luxury to South Florida's most storied stretch of coastline.

StatusHistory.note: Bvlgari Hotels announces its US debut in Miami Beach — adaptive reuse of a 1950s MiMo landmark (Sleeper Magazine)

Title: Ocean One
Description: An eight-story downtown mixed-use development bringing 371 rental apartments and roughly 25,600 square feet of ground-floor retail to Federal Highway, with a pool, pickleball court, and fitness center.
DescriptionLong: Ocean One is an eight-story mixed-use rental community rising in downtown Boynton Beach, Florida, along North Federal Highway between I-95 and the Intracoastal corridor. Developed by a joint venture of Hyperion Development Group, Winter Properties and Silverstein Properties and designed by Miami firm Arquitectonica, the project pairs 371 studio-to-two-bedroom apartments with roughly 25,600 square feet of ground-floor retail, landscaped pedestrian plazas and a 600-plus-space parking garage. Resort-style amenities include a pool and spa deck, fitness center, pickleball court, co-working space and a sports simulator, positioning Ocean One as a placemaking anchor for Boynton Beach's emerging downtown core.

StatusHistory.note: Ocean One unveiled — 371-unit, 8-story mixed-use project on N. Federal Highway, downtown Boynton Beach (Florida YIMBY)
$108M construction loan from Madison Realty Capital (with Silverstein equity) closed — the unlock for vertical work (The Real Deal)
Delivery now tracking 2029 (per Boynton Beach CRA / Boynton Weekly); cranes on site June 2026 — corrects prior 2028
Delivery now tracking 2029 (per Boynton Beach CRA / Boynton Weekly); cranes on site June 2026 — corrects prior 2028

Title: Capella Residences Bangkok
Description: A 45-story, 262-unit Capella-branded wellness residence overlooking Lumpini Park, the centerpiece of BDMS's WellEra complex with on-demand health protocols, biometric monitoring, and dedicated wellness floors.
DescriptionLong: Capella Residences Bangkok is a 262-unit luxury branded-residence tower and Capella Hotel Group's first wellness-integrated branded residences, forming the residential heart of WellEra, a wellness complex in Bangkok's Lumpini district at the corner of Sarasin and Langsuan Roads overlooking Lumpini Park. Developed in partnership with BDMS Wellness Clinic, the residences weave preventive and longevity medicine, restorative wellness programming and Capella's signature service into everyday living, set alongside an urban wellness retreat and curated lifestyle retail at one of central Bangkok's most prestigious park-front addresses.

StatusHistory.note: Capella Hotel Group launches 262-unit Capella Residences Bangkok within the WellEra wellness complex, Lumpini (eHotelier)

Title: WellEra
Description: A wellness-integrated mixed-use complex beside Lumphini Park weaving together a branded residence, a preventive-care clinic, an urban wellness retreat, and lifestyle retail into one connected ecosystem.
DescriptionLong: WellEra is a wellness-integrated mixed-use complex by Thai healthcare group BDMS in central Bangkok's Lumpini district, billed as Southeast Asia's first development to fuse preventive and longevity medicine with everyday living. Overlooking Lumpini Park near Sarasin and Langsuan Roads, WellEra brings together a flagship BDMS Wellness Clinic, the Capella-branded wellness residences, an urban wellness retreat and curated lifestyle retail — a bet on the global longevity megatrend that positions Bangkok as an international wellness destination.

StatusHistory.note: BDMS launches THB 29B WellEra wellness complex in central Bangkok's Lumpini district (Kaohoon International)

Title: One Scottsdale
Description: A 120-acre luxury mixed-use district at the Loop 101 and Scottsdale Road interchange, planned for up to 2.8 million square feet of retail and office space, 2,000 residences, and 400 hotel rooms in North Scottsdale.
DescriptionLong: One Scottsdale is a 120-acre master-planned mixed-use district at the northeast corner of Scottsdale Road and Loop 101 in North Scottsdale, Arizona, developed by DMB Associates. Designed as a walkable urban core for the Loop 101 corridor, it blends luxury for-sale condominiums, Class-A apartments and office space, chef-driven restaurants, retail and hospitality, anchored by the City of Hope Cancer Center and a Home2 Suites hotel. Branded residential collections such as Portico and Atavia bring private elevators, rooftop decks, two-car garages and resort-style amenities to one of the Valley's fastest-growing destinations.

StatusHistory.note: Original One Scottsdale master plan / rezoning approved 2002 (amended 2016), DMB Associates

Title: Trout National - The Reserve
Description: A private, invitation-only destination golf club designed by Tiger Woods' TGR Design on a reclaimed silica sand mine in southern New Jersey, founded by MLB All-Star and Millville native Mike Trout.
DescriptionLong: Trout National - The Reserve is an ultra-private destination golf club carved from roughly 280 acres of reclaimed silica sand-mining land in the Millville and Vineland area of southern New Jersey. Designed by Tiger Woods' TGR Design team, the par-72 championship layout stretches beyond 7,400 yards across sweeping sandy waste areas and large, boldly undulating greens, with water in play on five holes. Beyond the main course, members enjoy a lit par-3 short course known as The Bullpen, on-site lodging, and a state-of-the-art practice and performance center. Conceived by hometown baseball star Mike Trout in partnership with developer John Ruga, the club pairs world-class design with an intimate, invitation-only membership in a region better known for its Pine Barrens than its golf.

Title: National Geographic Museum of Exploration
Description: A reimagined 100,000-square-foot Museum of Exploration inside National Geographic's Base Camp headquarters in downtown Washington, blending immersive exhibits, a state-of-the-art theater, and a walking dark ride.
DescriptionLong: The Museum of Exploration transforms National Geographic's 17th Street headquarters — known as Base Camp — into a roughly 100,000-square-foot, experience-driven home for the Society's storied legacy of exploration. Anchored by a glass pavilion and a landscaped public courtyard, the museum weaves together exclusively curated exhibitions, iconic photography, world-famous artifacts, and an education center with cutting-edge attractions including an immersive theater and a walking "dark ride." A signature green wall and extensive sustainability measures, from reused building materials to rooftop solar that supplies a meaningful share of the campus's power, frame a downtown cultural destination designed to inspire a new generation of explorers and conservationists.

Title: Virginia Museum of Fine Arts Expansion
Description: The largest expansion in the Virginia Museum of Fine Arts' history — a 173,000-square-foot McGlothlin Wing II by SmithGroup that adds galleries, event space, and a new public face to the Richmond campus.
DescriptionLong: The Virginia Museum of Fine Arts Expansion is the largest growth project in the Richmond museum's history, adding the SmithGroup-designed McGlothlin Wing II across the south and west of its Museum District campus. The roughly 173,000-square-foot addition introduces a fluted concrete-and-glass façade that plays with natural light and a new fifth floor, with expanded galleries for American, African, contemporary and photographic art, a 500-seat special-events space, a new café and bar, and additional special-exhibition space, while renovating tens of thousands of square feet of existing galleries. The museum remains open to the public throughout construction.

StatusHistory.note: McGlothlin Wing II expansion, opening 2029; VMFA campus in Richmond's Museum District (SmithGroup)
$261M McGlothlin Wing II expansion + SmithGroup design unveiled Oct 2024 (The Architect's Newspaper)

Title: Louis Vuitton 103 Champs-Élysées
Description: Louis Vuitton's first-ever hotel, set within a landmark 1898 Beaux-Arts building on the Champs-Élysées, crowned by a rooftop restaurant with views of the Arc de Triomphe.
DescriptionLong: Louis Vuitton's flagship destination at 103 Champs-Élysées occupies a restored 1898 Haussmann-era building in Paris's 8th arrondissement that formerly housed HSBC's French headquarters. Conceived by LVMH as an immersive Louis Vuitton maison rather than a conventional hotel, the roughly 6,000-square-metre landmark blends house retail, a panoramic restaurant, an expansive spa, exhibition halls and a small number of ultra-exclusive private suites. Its façade is designed to evoke Louis Vuitton's signature Monogram trunk, anchoring the address to the brand's Art of Travel heritage on the most famous avenue in Paris.

StatusHistory.note: LVMH revealed plans to convert 103 Champs-Élysées into Louis Vuitton's first hotel, Sept 2023 (Sortir à Paris)

Title: Avra at Metropolitan Naples
Description: A boutique 15-story luxury condominium of 56 residences within the mixed-use Metropolitan Naples district, near Fifth Avenue South.
DescriptionLong: Avra at Metropolitan Naples is a 15-story luxury high-rise of 56 residences anchoring the Metropolitan Naples mixed-use community at Fifth Avenue South and Davis Boulevard in downtown Naples, Florida. Developed by Development Partners International with Suffolk Construction as general contractor and architecture by Borges Architects, Avra pairs expansive single-floor residences with a rooftop sky pool billed as the highest downtown, offering sweeping Gulf and Naples Bay views. A fifth-floor amenity deck adds a putting green, fire pit and spa, alongside a fitness center, residents' lounge and covered parking, with street-level retail and dining below — a sophisticated address at the heart of one of Florida's most coveted coastal downtowns.

StatusHistory.note: Topped off Oct 31 2025 (Business Wire/PR Newswire); Fall 2026 occupancy, still marketing pre-occupancy Apr 2026 — near-opening
Topped off Oct 31 2025 (Business Wire / PR Newswire)

Title: Rosewood Residences Lido Key
Description: The world's first all-residential Rosewood — a beachfront condominium of 65 residences on Lido Key in Sarasota.
DescriptionLong: Rosewood Residences Lido Key is an 11-story, 65-residence luxury condominium at 930 Benjamin Franklin Drive on the Gulf-front sands of Lido Key in Sarasota, Florida — the first standalone residential property to carry the renowned Rosewood brand. Developed by the Ronto Group with Wheelock Street Capital, designed by Swedroe Architecture with interiors by Lillian Wu Studio, the building offers three- and four-bedroom homes of roughly 3,300 to 5,000 square feet. More than 30,000 square feet of indoor amenities include a private restaurant, theater, game room, fitness center and spa with guest suites, joined by two pools, cabanas and fire-table terraces outdoors, plus signature Rosewood service from in-residence dining to a personal chef — an ultra-luxury beachfront address on Sarasota's Gulf Coast.

StatusHistory.note: 11-story beachfront tower on Lido Key (Sarasota Magazine); topped out Nov 2024, delivery early 2026
Rosewood-branded beachfront condo on Lido Key; construction began early 2024 (Sarasota Magazine)
11-story structure topped out Nov 2024 (Sarasota Magazine)

Title: Palazzo at Bayfront
Description: An intimate enclave of 35 luxury residences completing the Bayfront mixed-use community on the Gordon River in downtown Naples.
DescriptionLong: Palazzo at Bayfront is a boutique enclave of 35 luxury bayfront residences completing the Bayfront mixed-use district at the gateway to Fifth Avenue South in downtown Naples, Florida, along the Gordon River. Developed by Stoneburner Development Company with BCBE Construction as general contractor and design by 4141 Design Group, the building offers spacious three- and four-bedroom condominiums of roughly 2,100 to 3,100-plus square feet. Residents enjoy a private clubhouse, a resort-style pool with spa and cabanas, a fitness center, and optional Gordon River boat slips with direct Gulf access — an intimate waterfront address steps from the dining, galleries and theater of one of Naples' most walkable neighborhoods.

StatusHistory.note: Stoneburner Development Group introduced Palazzo at Bayfront, a 35-residence boutique condo at the gateway to Fifth Avenue South (Florida Weekly)
Broke ground Jan 23 2026 (Naples Press)

Title: ONE Fort Myers
Description: A 21-story boutique waterfront condominium of 34 residences on the Caloosahatchee River in the historic Fort Myers River District.
DescriptionLong: ONE Fort Myers is a 21-story boutique waterfront tower of just 34 residences on First Street in the Fort Myers Historic River District, overlooking the Caloosahatchee River. Developed by JAXI Builders with architecture by Behar Font and interiors by Adriana Hoyos, the intimate tower features private elevator entrances, Viking kitchens and floor-to-ceiling glass framing river and downtown views. Resort-style amenities include a heated infinity-edge pool with cascading waterfalls, a private riverside beach with cabanas, and a riverfront kayak launch, delivering rare high-rise luxury to the heart of downtown Fort Myers, Florida, with sales led by SERHANT.

StatusHistory.note: Construction start ~May 2024 per Fort Myers CRA; original 2025 delivery slipped to ~2027 (Business Observer confirms active vertical sales mid-2025)
Construction start ~May 2024 per Fort Myers CRA; original 2025 delivery slipped to ~2027 (Business Observer confirms active vertical sales mid-2025)
ONE Fort Myers unveiled, presale office opened in the Historic River District (Florida Weekly)

Title: One Park Sarasota
Description: An 18-story, 86-residence luxury condominium tower in Sarasota's bayfront Quay district, designed by Hoyt Architects for Property Markets Group.
DescriptionLong: One Park Sarasota is an 18-story luxury condominium tower of 86 residences rising at The Quay on the downtown Sarasota Bayfront, directly across from The Bay Park. Developed by Property Markets Group in partnership with MoneyShow, designed by Sarasota's Hoyt Architects with Juneau Construction Company as general contractor, the tower frames sweeping views of Sarasota Bay through floor-to-ceiling glass. More than 40,000 square feet of amenities — including a wine room, dog park, children's playroom and dedicated remote workspaces — complement ground-floor retail, positioning One Park as a marquee waterfront address in one of Florida's fastest-growing cultural downtowns.

StatusHistory.note: Broke ground Oct 2024 at The Quay (Florida YIMBY)
Nearing topping out (Level 17 of 18), ~75% sold; Q1 2027 delivery (Your Observer / Florida YIMBY)
Property Markets Group And MoneyShow Unveil New Plans For One Park Sarasota

Title: The Palm 625 Sarasota
Description: A boutique five-story condominium of just 12 residences on South Palm Avenue in downtown Sarasota's historic Burns Square.
DescriptionLong: The Palm 625 is a boutique four-story condominium of just 12 residences on South Palm Avenue near Golden Gate Point in downtown Sarasota, Florida. Designed by Sarasota's Hoyt Architects with Voeller Construction as contractor, the intimate building delivers large-format luxury homes of roughly 2,400 to 4,400 square feet with open floor plans, just steps from the city's bayfront, marina and the shops, dining and arts of downtown Sarasota — a rare small-scale offering in one of the Gulf Coast's most sought-after urban waterfront enclaves.

StatusHistory.note: Corrected to four stories per Business Observer / Your Observer / Hoyt Architects (recorded as 5); delivery targeted Q3 2026

Title: Midtown at Bonita
Description: A 68-acre walkable mixed-use district in Bonita Springs pairing 400 luxury residences with about 200,000 square feet of retail and office space.
DescriptionLong: Midtown at Bonita is a 68-acre mixed-use community a quarter-mile east of Interstate 75 on Bonita Beach Road in Bonita Springs, Florida, conceived as a walkable live-work-dine downtown for the city's fast-growing east side. Developed by the family-owned Zuckerman Group, the district pairs 400 luxury apartments with a 165-room hotel, roughly 200,000 square feet of retail and office space, and a main-street-style entertainment core, all set against more than 30 acres of preserved open space including lakes and a natural preserve. National retailers anchoring the project signal Midtown's emergence as Southwest Florida's next major mixed-use destination.
ProjectType: Mixed-Use, Residences, Hotel, Retail, Office, Entertainment, Park

StatusHistory.note: 68-acre mixed-use district: 400 apartments + 165-room hotel + 200k sf retail/office + entertainment main street + 30+ ac open space (Gulfshore Business)
68-acre mixed-use district: 400 apartments + 165-room hotel + 200k sf retail/office + entertainment main street + 30+ ac open space (Gulfshore Business)
Groundbreaking ceremony Nov 6 2025 (Gulfshore Business)
Bonita Springs City Council approved the 68-acre Midtown master plan in 2020 (Gulfshore Business)

Title: 3300 Gulf Shore
Description: A 51-residence enclave of two eight-story towers on Gulf Shore Boulevard North in Naples, with a private 16-slip marina on Moorings Bay and panoramic Gulf and bay views.
DescriptionLong: 3300 Gulf Shore is a boutique waterfront condominium in The Moorings neighborhood of Naples, Florida, set on a roughly three-acre Moorings Bay site and composed of two intimate eight-story towers holding 51 estate-scale residences. Developed by Kolter Urban with architecture by MHK Architecture and BCBE Construction as general contractor, the residences range from about 2,000 to 7,500 square feet of refined coastal living. A 16-slip private marina with direct Moorings Bay access anchors the amenity collection, joined by two resort-style pools, a fitness and wellness center, a social lounge and concierge service — a rare new-construction luxury enclave on Naples' protected bayfront.

StatusHistory.note: Groundbreaking 2026-01-29 (recorded start 2025-10 slipped); Moorings Bay site with 16-slip private marina; delivery late 2027
Groundbreaking 2026-01-29 (recorded start 2025-10 slipped); Moorings Bay site with 16-slip private marina; delivery late 2027
Groundbreaking Jan 29 2026 with public events Jan 31–Feb 1 (developer / Gulf Shore Assn. of Condominiums)

Title: Sarasota Square Apartments
Description: A 355-unit, five- and six-story apartment community anchoring the redevelopment of the former Sarasota Square mall, with resort-style amenities along the U.S. 41 corridor.
DescriptionLong: Sarasota Square Apartments is a 360-unit residential community planned on a 4.5-acre parcel within the redevelopment of the former Sarasota Square Mall in the Palmer Ranch area of south Sarasota, Florida, at Sarasota Square Boulevard and U.S. 41. Developed by Woodfield Development, the apartments form the residential heart of a roughly 96-acre mixed-use town center — led by master developers Torburn Partners and Jamestown — that pairs a Whole Foods-anchored retail district with more than half a million square feet of shops and dining. The community brings modern rental living to one of Sarasota's most established and fast-evolving suburban corridors.

StatusHistory.note: Corrected construction→announced: 4.5-ac apartment parcel sold to Woodfield Mar 2026, 360 units, groundbreaking only expected summer 2026 — not yet under construction (Business Observer)
Corrected construction→announced: 4.5-ac apartment parcel sold to Woodfield Mar 2026, 360 units, groundbreaking only expected summer 2026 — not yet under construction (Business Observer)

Title: 924 & 934 North Magnolia Avenue
Description: A 17-story mixed-use high-rise in Downtown Orlando's North Quarter with 350 residences, ground-floor retail, townhomes along the Orlando Urban Trail, and a publicly accessible plaza.
DescriptionLong: 924 & 934 North Magnolia Avenue is a 17-story, 350-unit residential tower planned in the North Quarter District of Downtown Orlando, Florida, developed by the Acram Group and designed by Baker Barrios Architects. The L-shaped tower pairs roughly 3,300 square feet of ground-floor retail with about 13,000 square feet of resident amenities and an eight-level parking garage, and contributes a corner public parklet to the streetscape. Its traditional design — a tan-brick podium with arched windows, antique-brass detailing and townhouse-style stoops along Weber Street — brings a distinctive, walkable residential presence to one of downtown Orlando's fastest-growing neighborhoods.

StatusHistory.note: Acram Group's Magnolia Tower revealed in Downtown Orlando's North Quarter (Florida YIMBY); since redesigned to a single 17-story, 350-unit building

Title: The St. Regis Costa Mujeres Resort, Cancún
Description: A 163-key beachfront St. Regis resort in the Costa Mujeres enclave north of Cancún, with nine dining outlets, a Maya-inspired spa, and signature St. Regis Butler Service.
DescriptionLong: The St. Regis Costa Mujeres Resort is a 163-key beachfront resort in the Costa Mujeres enclave north of Cancún, Mexico, developed by AB Living under Marriott International and designed by Mexico City architecture firm Sordo Madaleno. Fronting white-sand beaches, protected mangroves and the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef, the resort offers nine dining venues, multiple pools, a beach club and a spa drawing on Maya heritage, all delivered with signature St. Regis Butler Service. A collection of St. Regis branded residences complements the resort within the master plan.
Architect: Sordo Madaleno, Maat Handasa, St. Regis

StatusHistory.note: Marriott/AB Living announce St. Regis Costa Mujeres (original reveal, Nov 2022)
St. Regis Costa Mujeres opened to guests 2026-06-25 (The Points Guy/Marriott); 163 rooms & suites; Costa Mujeres, north of Cancún
St. Regis Costa Mujeres opened to guests 2026-06-25 (The Points Guy/Marriott); 163 rooms & suites; Costa Mujeres, north of Cancún
Reservations opened ~May 2026 ahead of the June 25 opening (hotel bookings)

Title: Rosewood Mandarina
Description: An all-suite Rosewood resort on Mexico's Riviera Nayarit where jungle meets the Pacific, with four restaurants, the Asaya Spa, a Greg Norman golf course, and a polo and equestrian club.
DescriptionLong: Rosewood Mandarina is an ultra-luxury, all-suite jungle-and-beach resort on the shores of Mexico's Riviera Nayarit, set within the Mandarina master-planned development north of Punta de Mita. Owned and developed by Mexico City-based RLH Properties and operated by Rosewood Hotels & Resorts, the property spans three distinct ecosystems — lush coastal mountains, pristine beachfront, and agrarian flatlands — with architecture and interiors led by Caroline Meersseman of Bandó x Seidel Meersseman. Its 134 accommodations include three specialty suites and two expansive standalone villas: the multi-floor Canalan Beachfront Villa and the cliffside Cora Mountaintop Villa with its own infinity lap pool. Dining venues such as La Cocina and Buena Onda, a signature Sense spa, and immersive nature experiences position Mandarina among Rosewood's most ambitious destination resorts.

StatusHistory.note: Rosewood Mandarina opens on Mexico's Riviera Nayarit
Reservations/first arrivals from Apr 1, 2025; 134 all-suite accommodations incl. two standalone villas
Reservations/first arrivals from Apr 1, 2025; 134 all-suite accommodations incl. two standalone villas
Reservations/first arrivals from Apr 1, 2025; 134 all-suite accommodations incl. two standalone villas

Title: Adagio Sarasota
Description: A two-building, 172-residence mixed-use development planned for 1360 Ringling Boulevard in downtown Sarasota, anchored by an 18-story luxury condominium tower designed by Kobi Karp.
DescriptionLong: The Adagio is a two-tower luxury and mixed-income condominium development planned for 1360 Ringling Boulevard in downtown Sarasota, Florida. Designed by Miami-based architect Kobi Karp, the project pairs an 18-story tower of market-rate residences with a 10-story tower of attainable-housing units — 172 homes in total — and pursues density incentives under Florida's Live Local Act. The development team brings together established Florida builders filing as DT Sarasota Development LLC. Plans call for roughly 32,000 square feet of ground-floor retail and office space plus a restaurant, alongside resident amenities such as a pool deck, wellness center, and entertainment lounges, aiming to bring both luxury living and workforce housing to the walkable Ringling Boulevard corridor.
Developer: The Lutgert Companies, R&P Holdings, Barron Collier Companies

StatusHistory.note: Two-tower, 172-unit Adagio revealed for 1360 Ringling Blvd, downtown Sarasota under the Live Local Act

Title: The Edge Sarasota
Description: A boutique 10-story luxury condominium at Cocoanut Avenue and Fruitville Road in downtown Sarasota, with just 27 residences and panoramic views of Sarasota Bay and the skyline.
DescriptionLong: The Edge Sarasota is a 10-story boutique luxury condominium rising at 290 Cocoanut Avenue on the northern edge of downtown Sarasota, Florida. Developed by a joint venture of Jebco Ventures and Omnium Real Estate (ORE Development) with architecture by BDG Architects and interiors by DSDG Architects, the building delivers just 27 expansive two- and three-bedroom residences ranging from roughly 3,150 to 3,530 square feet. Floor-to-ceiling glass frames views of Sarasota Bay and the city skyline, while penthouse homes add private rooftop terraces. Amenities include a pool and spa, fitness center, concierge service, climate-controlled private garages, and a ground-floor fine-dining restaurant, positioning The Edge as one of the most exclusive addresses in Sarasota's walkable downtown core.

StatusHistory.note: The Edge revealed as a 10-story, 27-residence boutique condo at 290 Cocoanut Ave, downtown Sarasota
The Edge topped off at 10 stories per SRQ; construction began Dec 2024
The Edge topped off at 10 stories per SRQ; construction began Dec 2024
The Edge topped off at 10 stories per SRQ; construction began Dec 2024
Construction began Dec 2024 with a $46.5M Centennial Bank loan, ~half presold

Title: Purebred Farm
Description: A new 14-hole golf course at Destination Kohler in Wisconsin, designed by King Collins Dormer across prairie, farmland, and river-valley terrain just west of the championship Blackwolf Run layout.
DescriptionLong: Purebred Farm is a 14-hole, walking-only golf course at Destination Kohler in Kohler, Wisconsin, set just west of the celebrated Blackwolf Run courses. Developed by Kohler Co. and designed by the acclaimed firm King Collins Dormer, the minimalist, nature-driven layout threads roughly 5,000 yards across about 47 acres of prairie, river valley, and farmland in a multi-loop routing of mixed par-3, -4, and -5 holes inspired by Golden Age architecture. Its name honors the site's agricultural heritage, including the former Kohler Farms operation. The course anchors a broader resort expansion that also adds The Serve racquet facility and a renovated Carriage House, deepening Kohler's reputation as one of America's premier golf destinations.

StatusHistory.note: Destination Kohler announces Purebred Farm, a 14-hole walking course by King Collins Dormer west of Blackwolf Run

Title: The Gulfstream Hotel
Description: The landmark restoration of the historic 1925 Gulfstream Hotel on Lake Avenue in downtown Lake Worth Beach, reviving the six-story Mediterranean Revival icon as a luxury waterfront hotel.
DescriptionLong: The Gulfstream Hotel is a historic six-story boutique hotel at 1 Lake Avenue in downtown Lake Worth Beach, Florida, standing at the foot of the Lake Worth Bridge beside Bryant Park. Originally opened in 1925 and listed on the National Register of Historic Places, the long-vacant Mediterranean Revival landmark is being revived by developer Restoration St. Louis as a Marriott Tribute Portfolio property. The first phase delivers about 90 redesigned guest rooms, a signature restaurant, and flexible event space including a rooftop venue, while an adjacent new mixed-use building will add a further 50 hotel rooms, 83 market-rate apartments, a spa, and a ballroom. Backed in part by the city's Community Redevelopment Agency, the project restores one of the lakefront's most cherished landmarks as the anchor of downtown Lake Worth Beach.

StatusHistory.note: Restoration near-complete; reopening as Marriott Tribute Portfolio with ~90 Phase 1 keys, rooms bookable — normalize "Opening Soon" to coming-soon
Restoration near-complete; reopening as Marriott Tribute Portfolio with ~90 Phase 1 keys, rooms bookable — normalize "Opening Soon" to coming-soon
Rooms bookable via Marriott Tribute Portfolio ahead of opening

Title: Paragon
Description: A 23-story waterfront condominium on Vernon Boulevard in Long Island City — the neighborhood's first new waterfront condo in over a decade — built around the preserved historic Paragon Paint Factory.
DescriptionLong: Paragon is a 23-story waterfront condominium at 45-40 Vernon Boulevard in the Anable Basin area of Long Island City, Queens. Developed by ZD Jasper Realty and designed by Archimaera Architecture, the project incorporates the historic Paragon Paint Factory into its base beneath a brick-and-glass residential tower of 186 studio-to-four-bedroom homes. Interiors by March and White Design feature lofty ceiling heights and floor-to-ceiling windows framing the Manhattan skyline and East River. Amenities include a sky lounge, landscaped rooftop terrace, fitness center, an outdoor fitness terrace with pickleball court, golf simulator, and karaoke room, alongside a public waterfront park and esplanade. SERHANT. leads sales and marketing for one of the first new-construction condominiums on the Long Island City waterfront in over a decade.

StatusHistory.note: 23-story mixed-use waterfront tower revealed for the former Paragon Paint Factory site, 45-40 Vernon Blvd, Long Island City
Paragon topped out at 23 stories on the LIC waterfront per CityRealty

Title: Williamsburg Wharf
Description: A 3.75-acre resort-style waterfront development on Kent Avenue in Williamsburg, comprising five luxury high-rise towers by Naftali Group around a public waterfront esplanade.
DescriptionLong: Williamsburg Wharf is a 3.75-acre, master-planned waterfront community on the South Williamsburg shoreline of Brooklyn, spanning roughly one million square feet across five residential towers. Developed by Naftali Group in partnership with Access Industries, the resort-style development blends luxury condominiums and rental residences with extensive amenities and a public esplanade along the East River. The towers — One, Two, Three, and Four Williamsburg Wharf, with two more planned in a later phase — offer waterfront views, hospitality-grade services, and curated indoor-outdoor amenity spaces, anchoring the rapid transformation of the North Brooklyn waterfront.

StatusHistory.note: Naftali Group unveils 3.75-acre, ~$600M five-tower Williamsburg Wharf "urban resort" on the South Williamsburg waterfront
Multi-phase build: Phase 1 wrapped/occupied 2025, later towers vertical — normalize "Under Construction" to construction
Phase 1 wrapped construction per YIMBY

Title: The Huron
Description: A pair of 13-story waterfront condominium towers on the Greenpoint waterfront, designed by Morris Adjmi with an industrial-inspired brick-and-bronze aesthetic.
DescriptionLong: The Huron is a two-tower luxury condominium at 29 Huron Street on the Greenpoint waterfront in Brooklyn. Developed by Quadrum Global and designed by Morris Adjmi Architects, its H-shaped, twin 13-story form evokes a ship with two smokestacks, pairing a textured brick podium with a refined gridded facade that nods to the neighborhood's industrial craft heritage. The building holds 171 studio-to-four-bedroom residences and more than 23,000 square feet of private outdoor space across many of its homes, with sweeping views of the Manhattan skyline and East River. SERHANT. New Development led sales for the project, one of Greenpoint's top-selling new condominiums.

StatusHistory.note: Quadrum Global debuts The Huron, a twin 13-story Morris Adjmi-designed condo on the Greenpoint waterfront
Building occupied and ~70% sold; normalize "Now Open" to open
Condo sales launched Fall 2022

Title: One Domino Square
